text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: How to add an AirTag to Find My - Apple Support]

# How to add an AirTag to Find My

To help keep track of personal items, like keys or a backpack, you can add an AirTag or other compatible Find My network accessories to Find My. You can add items using AirTag setup or the Find My app.

- [Add an AirTag to Find My using AirTag setup](https://support.apple.com/en-us/101602#add-airtag)

- [Add a compatible Find My network accessory or AirTag using the Find My app](https://support.apple.com/en-us/101602#add-compatible)

- [If you're unable to add an AirTag](https://support.apple.com/en-us/101602#unable)

## Add an AirTag to Find My using AirTag setup

To add an AirTag, you need iOS or iPadOS 14.5 or later. To add an AirTag (2nd generation), you need iOS or iPadOS 26.2.1 or later.

[![Play the video for how to set up your AirTag.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/videos/VSD0322_AddAirTagiPhone_Thumbnail_ENUS.original.png)](https://cdsassets.apple.com/vid/live/hls/7WUAS350/videos/7b45e5dc_1770315121.m3u8)

![](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/videos/VSD0322_AddAirTagiPhone_Thumbnail_ENUS.original.png)

Audio

Subtitles

- Off

elapsed00:0044:44

remaining-00:00-44:44

Live

Live Streaming

1. Hold your AirTag near your iPhone or iPad, then tap Connect. If you have more than one AirTag and get the alert "More than one AirTag detected," make sure that you bring only one AirTag near your device at a time.
![iPhone screen showing Connect AirTag button.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/icloud/find-my/ios-26-iphone-16-pro-home-screen-airtag-connect.png)
2. Select a category to name your AirTag. If you choose Custom Name, select an emoji.
![iPhone screen showing how to select a name for AirTag.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/icloud/find-my/ios-26-iphone-16-pro-home-screen-airtag-connect-name-airtag.png)
3. To link your AirTag with your Apple Account, tap Agree or Continue again.

4. Tap Done.

metadata.title: How to add an AirTag to Find My - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: How to add an AirTag to Find My - Apple Support]

ple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/icloud/find-my/ios-26-iphone-16-pro-home-screen-airtag-connect-name-airtag.png)
3. To link your AirTag with your Apple Account, tap Agree or Continue again.

4. Tap Done.

## Add a compatible Find My network accessory or AirTag using the Find My app

To add an AirTag or Find My Network accessory, you need iOS or iPadOS 14.5 or later. To add an AirTag (2nd generation), you need iOS or iPadOS 26.2.1 or later.

1. Follow the manufacturer's instructions to connect your compatible Find My network accessory to your iPhone or iPad.

2. Open Find My on your iPhone or iPad.

3. Tap Items, then tap the Add button![No alt supplied for Image](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/inline-icons/ios-iwork-add-icon.png).
![In Find My, tap the add button to add a third-party Find My network accessory.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/icloud/find-my/ios-26-iphone-16-pro-find-my-items-add-magnifier.png)
4. Tap Add AirTag or Other Item.
![In Find My, tap Add Other Item to add a third-party Find My network accessory.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/icloud/find-my/ios-26-iphone-16-pro-find-my-items-add-other-item.png)
5. Follow the onscreen steps to finish setting up the item.

## If you're unable to add your AirTag

First, make sure that your iPhone or iPad has the correct settings. Then try to add your AirTag again.

### Check or update your device settings

- Update your device to the latest version of iOS or iPadOS. AirTag (2nd generation) requires iOS or iPadOS 26.2.1 or later.

- Open the Settings app, then tap Bluetooth and make sure that you turned on Bluetooth.

- Open the Settings app, tap Privacy & Security, then tap Location Services, and make sure that you turned on Location Services. Then scroll down, tap Find My and choose a level of access. For the most accurate location, turn on Precise Location.

- [Turn on iCloud Keychain](https://support.apple.com/109016)

- Check that your Apple Account setup is finished. Open the Settings app, then tap your name. Check for any alerts to Update Apple Account Settings.

- If you get a "Some iCloud data isn't syncing" notification, tap "Resume data sync."

- Make sure that you have a strong Wi-Fi or cellular connection.

- [Restart your iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-us/118259) or [restart your iPad](https://support.apple.com/en-us/101603).

metadata.title: How to add an AirTag to Find My - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: How to add an AirTag to Find My - Apple Support]

nc."

- Make sure that you have a strong Wi-Fi or cellular connection.

- [Restart your iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-us/118259) or [restart your iPad](https://support.apple.com/en-us/101603).

### Set up your AirTag again

- If the setup animation disappeared, press the side button or Sleep/Wake button on your iPhone or iPad to put it to sleep. Then wake and unlock it, and wait 15 seconds. The animation should reappear.

- To set up more than one AirTag, make sure that only one is near your device at a time.

- You might need to [replace the battery in your AirTag](https://support.apple.com/en-us/102600).

- If your AirTag still won't connect, you might need to [reset your AirTag](https://support.apple.com/102577).

- [Restart your iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-us/118259) or [restart your iPad](https://support.apple.com/en-us/101603).

### If new items cannot be added

If you get a message that new items cannot be added, manage the items that you have in Find My:

- Items include everything in the Items tab and AirPods in the Devices tab.

- You can add up to 32 items in Find My, including shared items and accessories.

- In addition to AirTag and the third-party Find My network accessories listed in the Items tab, AirPods Max count as one item, AirPods and AirPods Pro 1 count as two items, AirPods Pro 2 and later count as three items.

If you reach this limit, you might see a message that says you can’t add another item or accessory, or accept a new shared item invitation until you remove an item or accessory.

# How to add an AirTag to Find My

To help keep track of personal items, like keys or a backpack, you can add an AirTag or other compatible Find My network accessories to Find My. You can add items using AirTag setup or the Find My app.

- [Add an AirTag to Find My using AirTag setup](https://support.apple.com/en-us/101602#add-airtag)

- [Add a compatible Find My network accessory or AirTag using the Find My app](https://support.apple.com/en-us/101602#add-compatible)

- [If you're unable to add an AirTag](https://support.apple.com/en-us/101602#unable)

## Add an AirTag to Find My using AirTag setup

To add an AirTag, you need iOS or iPadOS 14.5 or later. To add an AirTag (2nd generation), you need iOS or iPadOS 26.2.1 or later.

[![Play the video for how to set up your AirTag.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/videos/VSD0322_AddAirTagiPhone_Thumbnail_ENUS.original.png)](https://cdsassets.apple.com/vid/live/hls/7WUAS350/videos/7b45e5dc_1770315121.m3u8)

![iPhone screen showing Connect AirTag button.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/icloud/find-my/ios-26-iphone-16-pro-home-screen-airtag-connect.png)

## Add a compatible Find My network accessory or AirTag using the Find My app

To add an AirTag or Find My Network accessory, you need iOS or iPadOS 14.5 or later. To add an AirTag (2nd generation), you need iOS or iPadOS 26.2.1 or later.

1. Follow the manufacturer's instructions to connect your compatible Find My network accessory to your iPhone or iPad.

2. Open Find My on your iPhone or iPad.

3. Tap Items, then tap the Add button![No alt supplied for Image](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/inline-icons/ios-iwork-add-icon.png).
![In Find My, tap the add button to add a third-party Find My network accessory.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/icloud/find-my/ios-26-iphone-16-pro-find-my-items-add-magnifier.png)

5. Follow the onscreen steps to finish setting up the item.

## If you're unable to add your AirTag

First, make sure that your iPhone or iPad has the correct settings. Then try to add your AirTag again.

### Check or update your device settings

- Update your device to the latest version of iOS or iPadOS. AirTag (2nd generation) requires iOS or iPadOS 26.2.1 or later.

- Open the Settings app, then tap Bluetooth and make sure that you turned on Bluetooth.

- Open the Settings app, tap Privacy & Security, then tap Location Services, and make sure that you turned on Location Services. Then scroll down, tap Find My and choose a level of access. For the most accurate location, turn on Precise Location.

- [Turn on iCloud Keychain](https://support.apple.com/109016)

- Check that your Apple Account setup is finished. Open the Settings app, then tap your name. Check for any alerts to Update Apple Account Settings.

- If you get a "Some iCloud data isn't syncing" notification, tap "Resume data sync."

- Make sure that you have a strong Wi-Fi or cellular connection.

- [Restart your iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-us/118259) or [restart your iPad](https://support.apple.com/en-us/101603).

If you reach this limit, you might see a message that says you can’t add another item or accessory, or accept a new shared item invitation until you remove an item or accessory.

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Restart your iPad - Apple Support]

# Restart your iPad

Learn how to turn your iPad off, then back on.

## How to restart an iPad without a Home button

1. Press and hold either volume button and the top button until the power off slider appears.
![Pressing and holding the volume and top buttons on an iPad without a Home button until the power off slider appears.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/ipad/ipad/restart-ipad-pro-face-id-ipad-mini.png)
2. Drag the slider, then wait 30 seconds for your device to turn off. If your device is frozen or unresponsive, [force restart your device](https://support.apple.com/guide/ipad/restart-ipad-ipad63d30b5a/14.0/ipados/14.0#iPad715e3eec).

3. To turn your device back on, press and hold the top button until you see the Apple logo.

## How to restart an iPad with a Home button

1. Press and hold the top button until the power off slider appears.
![Illustration of pressing the top button on an iPad without a home button to initiate the restart process.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/ipad/ipad/restart-ipad-home-button.png)
2. Drag the slider, then wait for 30 seconds for your device to turn off. If your device is frozen or unresponsive, [force restart your device](https://support.apple.com/guide/ipad/restart-ipad-ipad63d30b5a/14.0/ipados/14.0#iPad715e3eec).

3. To turn your device back on, press and hold the top button until you see the Apple logo.

## **How to restart an iPad in Control Center.**

In iOS 18 and later, you can turn off your device from Control Center.

1. Swipe down from the top-right corner of your screen to open Control Center.

2. Press and hold the Power button![](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/inline-icons/power-icon.png) in the top-right corner of your screen, then let go.
![Control center open on iPad with the power button icon highlighted.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/ipados/ipados-26-ipad-pro-control-center-power-icon.png)
3. Drag the slider, then wait for 30 seconds for your device to turn off.

4. To turn your device back on, press and hold the top button until you see the Apple logo.

## Can't restart?

[Learn what to do if your iPad is frozen, doesn't turn on, or doesn't respond](https://support.apple.com/kb/HT212017).

[Source: apple_support] [Title: If you forgot your Apple Account password - Apple Support]

# If you forgot your Apple Account password

Having trouble accessing your Apple Account? Reset your password to regain access.

Apple ID is now Apple Account. You can still sign in with the same email address or phone number and password.

## Use a trusted Apple device

The easiest way to reset your password is with your iPhone or other trusted Apple device — one that’s already signed in to your Apple Account, so that Apple knows that it’s yours. You also need a passcode (or password on Mac) set up on that device.

[Resetting your password on the web](https://support.apple.com/en-us/102656#web-reset) when you don’t have a trusted device is another way to regain access to your account.

## Reset your Apple Account password on your iPhone, iPad, or Apple Vision Pro

[![Play a video showing the steps for how to reset your Apple Account password on iPhone](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/videos/VSD0324_ForgotAppleAccountPasswordiPhone_Thumbnail_ENUS.original.png)](https://cdsassets.apple.com/vid/live/hls/7WUAS350/videos/8ea0d29f_1768938590.m3u8)

![](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/videos/VSD0324_ForgotAppleAccountPasswordiPhone_Thumbnail_ENUS.original.png)

Audio

Subtitles

- Off

elapsed00:0044:44

remaining-00:00-44:44

Live

Live Streaming

1. Open the Settings app, then tap \[your name\].

2. Tap Sign-In & Security.

3. Tap Change Password.
![iPhone showing the Change Password button in Settings.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/apple-account/ios-26-iphone-17-pro-settings-apple-account-sign-in-security-change-password.png)
4. Enter your device passcode. Then follow the onscreen instructions to change your Apple Account password.

You might be required to wait one hour before changing your Apple Account password or other critical security settings. Learn more [about Stolen Device Protection](https://support.apple.com/en-us/120340) and when a security delay might be required.

## Reset your Apple Account password on your Mac

1. From the Apple menu , choose System Settings.

2. Click \[your name\], then click Sign-In & Security.

3. Click Change Password.
![Mac showing sign-in and security settings.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/apple-account/macos-tahoe-26-system-settings-apple-account-sign-in-security.png)
4. Enter the password used to unlock your Mac. Then follow the onscreen instructions to change your Apple Account password.

metadata.title: If you forgot your Apple Account password - Apple Support

[Source: apple_support] [Title: If you forgot your Apple Account password - Apple Support]

-account/macos-tahoe-26-system-settings-apple-account-sign-in-security.png)
4. Enter the password used to unlock your Mac. Then follow the onscreen instructions to change your Apple Account password.

## If you’re trying to sign in on a new Apple device

If you’re not signed in to your Apple Account on an Apple device, you can select “Forgot password or don’t have an Apple Account?” during device setup on the Apple Account sign-in screen.

If you skipped signing in during setup, you can reset your password in Settings:

- iPhone or iPad: Open the Settings app, then tap Apple Account. Select “Sign in manually”, then tap “Forgot password?” and follow the onscreen instructions.

- Mac: From the Apple menu , choose System Settings. Click Sign in, then click “Forgot password” and follow the onscreen instructions.

## If you don’t have a trusted device

If you’re not able to use one of your own trusted devices, borrow a device from a family member or friend. You can also change your password on iforgot.apple.com.

### Reset your Apple Account password with the Apple Support app on a borrowed device

If you don’t have an Apple device but have access to your trusted phone number, use the Apple Support app. You can borrow an Apple device from a friend or family member, or use one at an Apple Store.

1. Open the Apple Support app on the borrowed Apple device. If you need to download the Apple Support app, [find it in the App Store](https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/apple-store/id1130498044?pt=2003&ct=support.201487&mt=8).

2. Scroll down to Support Tools, then tap Reset Password.

3. Tap “Help Someone Else”.

4. Enter your Apple Account email address or phone number.

5. Tap Continue, and follow the onscreen instructions to reset your password.

Any information that you enter won’t be stored on the device.

Any information that you enter won’t be stored on the device.

### Reset your Apple Account password on the web

You can also regain access to your account by resetting your password on the web. When possible, the web browser redirects you to a trusted device instead.

The reset process on the web might take a little longer. If you’ll have access to one of your trusted devices soon, it might be faster and easier to reset your password at that time.

# How to back up your iPhone or iPad with iCloud

After you back up your iPhone or iPad, you have a copy of your information to use in case your device is ever replaced, lost, or damaged. You can back up your device manually, or turn on iCloud Backup to automatically keep your device backed up.

If you have a device with iOS 8 or earlier, [learn how to keep your devices and data backed up](https://support.apple.com/en-us/120863#about).

[![Play the video](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/videos/VSD0176_YT843_BackUpToiCloudiPhone_Thumbnail_ENUS.original.png)](https://cdsassets.apple.com/vid/live/hls/7WUAS350/videos/6feb1d8a_1747946830.m3u8)

- [Automatically back up with iCloud Backup](https://support.apple.com/en-us/108366#automatic)

## Back up manually

1. [Connect your device to a Wi-Fi network](https://support.apple.com/en-us/111107).

2. Open the Settings app, tap your name, then tap iCloud.
![iPhone screen showing the Apple ID settings, with iCloud highlighted as the next step for backing up the device.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/icloud/ios-26-iphone-16-pro-settings-account-icloud-on-tap.png)
3. Tap iCloud Backup.

4. Tap Back Up Now. Stay connected to your Wi-Fi network until the process ends. Under Back Up Now, the date and time of your last backup is shown. If you get an alert that you don't have enough iCloud storage space to complete the backup, follow the onscreen steps to [buy more iCloud storage](https://support.apple.com/en-us/108349).
![An iPhone screen showing the "Back Up Now" option.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/icloud/ios-26-iphone-16-pro-settings-account-icloud-backup-back-up-now.png)

## Automatically back up with iCloud Backup

1. Open the Settings app, then tap your name. Tap iCloud, then tap iCloud Backup and make sure that Back Up This \[Device\] is on.

2. Connect your device to a power source.

3. [Connect your device to a Wi-Fi network](https://support.apple.com/en-us/111107).

4. Make sure that your device's screen is locked.

5. If you get an alert that you don't have enough iCloud storage space to complete the backup, follow the onscreen steps to [buy more iCloud storage](https://support.apple.com/en-us/108349).

# How to create a new Apple Account

With your Apple Account, you can access all Apple devices and services — such as iCloud, the App Store, and other Apple services.

Apple ID is now Apple Account. You can still sign in with the same email address or phone number and password.

Not sure if you have an Apple Account? Learn about how to [check if you already have an Apple Account](https://support.apple.com/en-us/102627).

## Create an Apple Account on one of your devices or on the web

Learn how to create an Apple Account on one of your Apple devices, in an Apple app, or on the web.

### Create your Apple Account on iPhone, iPad, or Apple Vision Pro

[![Play a video about how to create an Apple Account on iPhone, iPad, and Apple Vision Pro.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/videos/VSD0238_VSD0237_Thumbnail_CreateAppleAccountiPhone_ENUS.original.png)](https://cdsassets.apple.com/vid/live/hls/7WUAS350/videos/eb57933d_1758242759.m3u8)

#### Create your Apple Account when you set up your device

1. Tap “Forgot password or don’t have an account?”

2. Tap Create a Free Apple Account.
![iPhone screen showing the option to select Create a Free Apple Account.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/apple-account/ios-26-iphone-16-pro-device-setup-create-a-free-apple-account.png)
3. Select your birthday and enter your name. Tap Continue.

4. Provide your email address. This primary email address will be what you use to sign in to your Apple Account. If you don’t have an email address, tap “Don’t have an email address?” to get a free iCloud email address.

5. Follow the onscreen steps to verify your email address, create a strong password, set your country or region, and set up two-factor authentication. If you choose to skip this step, you’ll be prompted to do this later in Settings.

After you verify your email address, you can sign in to your new Apple Account to use the App Store and other Apple services such as iCloud. In certain countries or regions, you might be asked to [confirm that you're an adult](https://support.apple.com/en-us/125662). If you decide not to, you'll see a message in the Settings app where you can choose to confirm your age later.

As you use your new device, you might be asked to enter your phone number, or a payment method and billing information. Your phone number can help to verify your identity and recover your account if needed. If you enter a payment method, you won’t be charged until you make a purchase.

#### Create your Apple Account using the App Store on your device

1. Open the App Store and tap the My Account button.

2. Tap Create New Apple Account. If you don’t see this option, make sure that you’re [signed out of iCloud](https://support.apple.com/en-us/104958).
![iPhone screen showing the option to Create New Apple Account.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/ap

After you verify your email address, you can sign in to your new Apple Account to use the App Store and other Apple services such as iCloud. In certain countries or regions, you might be asked to [confirm that you're an adult](https://support.apple.com/en-us/125662). If you decide not to, you'll see a message in the Settings app where you can choose to confirm your age later.

\\* In China mainland, you might be required to provide a +86 phone number for your Apple Account. You can change the number later. This number is only required in China mainland for verification purposes.

### Create your Apple Account on a Windows PC

On your Windows PC, you can create an Apple Account in the Apple Music app or the Apple TV app.

1. Open the Apple Music app or Apple TV app.

2. Click Sign In at the bottom of the sidebar.

3. Click Sign In.

4. Click Create New Apple Account.

5. Follow the onscreen steps to provide an email address, create a strong password, and set your device region. This primary email address will be what you use to sign in to your Apple Account.\*

6. Enter your payment method and billing information, then click Continue. You can also choose None. You won’t be charged until you make a purchase.

7. Check your email for a verification email from Apple and verify your email address.

After you verify your email address, you can sign in to your new Apple Account to use Apple services.

\\* In China mainland, you might be required to provide a +86 phone number for your Apple Account. You can change the number later. This number is only required in China mainland for verification purposes.

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Restart your iPhone - Apple Support]

# Restart your iPhone

Learn how to turn your iPhone off, then back on.

## How to restart your iPhone X, 11, 12, 13, or later

1. Press and hold either volume button and the side button until the power-off slider appears.
![Pressing the volume button on the left and the side button on the right initiates the power-off sequence.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/iphone/iphone-x-and-later-restart.png)
2. Drag the slider, then wait 30 seconds for your device to turn off. If your device is frozen or unresponsive, [force restart your device](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/force-restart-iphone-iph8903c3ee6/ios).

3. To turn your device back on, press and hold the side button (on the right side of your iPhone) until you see the Apple logo.

## How to restart your iPhone 6, 7, 8, or SE (2nd or 3rd generation)

1. Press and hold the side button until the power-off slider appears.
![Press and hold the side button on your iPhone as the first step to restart it.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/iphone/iphone-6-and-earlier-restart.png)
2. Drag the slider, then wait 30 seconds for your device to turn off. If your device is frozen or unresponsive, [force restart your device](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/force-restart-iphone-iph8903c3ee6/ios).

3. To turn your device back on, press and hold the side button until you see the Apple logo.

## How to restart your iPhone SE (1st generation), 5, or earlier

1. Press and hold the top button until the power-off slider appears.
![The power button on top of iPhone SE (1st generation), 5, or earlier](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/iphone/iphone-se-restart.png)
2. Drag the slider, then wait 30 seconds for your device to turn off. If your device is frozen or unresponsive, [force restart your device](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/force-restart-iphone-iph8903c3ee6/ios).

3. To turn your device back on, press and hold the top button until you see the Apple logo.

## Can't restart?

[Learn what to do if your iPhone is frozen, doesn't turn on, or doesn't respond](https://support.apple.com/kb/ht201412).

Published Date:June 02, 2026

# How to back up your iPhone, iPad, and iPod touch

If you back up your iPhone, iPad, or iPod touch, you'll have a copy of your information to use in case your device is ever replaced, lost, or damaged.

## Choose a backup method

Before you get started, learn the [difference between iCloud backups and backups you make using your computer](https://support.apple.com/kb/ht204136). Then choose the method that's best for you.

![iCloud logo, representing iCloud backup method](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/nav-icons/icloud-app-nav-full-size.png)

[Back up with your Mac](https://support.apple.com/kb/HT211229)

![Laptop representing backing up iPhone, iPad, or iPod touch to a computer.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/nav-icons/pc-nav.png)

[Back up with your PC](https://support.apple.com/kb/HT212156)

Tell us more about what's happening, and we’ll suggest what you can do next.

[Source: apple_support] [Title: If the battery in your iPhone or iPad drains too quickly - Apple Support]

# If the battery in your iPhone or iPad drains too quickly

"Battery life" is the amount of time that your device works before it needs to be recharged. Battery life is a combination of many factors, such as how much you use your device and which apps you use. If you think your battery is draining too quickly, learn what to do.

[![Play the video](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/videos/VSD0260_BatteryoniPhone_Thumbnail_ENUS.original.png)](https://cdsassets.apple.com/vid/live/hls/7WUAS350/videos/5c80ee36_1761757249.m3u8)

![](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/videos/VSD0260_BatteryoniPhone_Thumbnail_ENUS.original.png)

Audio

Subtitles

- Off

elapsed00:0044:44

remaining-00:00-44:44

Live

Live Streaming

## Before you begin

[Make sure that you have the latest version of iOS or iPadOS](https://support.apple.com/en-us/118575). You'll need iOS 26 or later or iPadOS 26 or later to see the Daily Usage chart and to use Adaptive Power.

## Check for battery suggestions and Insights

iOS and iPadOS can suggest how you can extend battery life with battery suggestions and Insights.

1. Open Settings and tap Battery.

2. If there are any suggestions or Insights, you'll find them just above Daily Usage.

### Learn about suggestions

You might see suggestions like these:

- Enable auto-brightness: Auto-brightness is off. To improve battery life, [enable auto-brightness](https://support.apple.com/en-us/109351).

- Enable auto-lock: Auto-lock is off, and your device is often idle. To improve battery life, [enable auto-lock](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/iph7117338a8/ios).

- High Brightness: High screen brightness consumes a lot of energy, and your brightness is set to higher than the recommended level. To improve battery life, [reduce brightness](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/iph60ba71065/ios).

### Learn about Insights

With iOS 18 and later and iPadOS 18 and later, Insights inform you of activities happening in the background that can affect battery life and thermal performance for a while. When the activity finishes, battery life will return to normal. You might see one of these Insights:

- Ongoing iOS Update: A recent software update is finishing in the background.

- Ongoing Device Setup: Your device is completing setup steps in the background.

metadata.title: If the battery in your iPhone or iPad drains too quickly - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: If the battery in your iPhone or iPad drains too quickly - Apple Support]

You might see one of these Insights:

- Ongoing iOS Update: A recent software update is finishing in the background.

- Ongoing Device Setup: Your device is completing setup steps in the background.

## Check daily usage

1. Open Settings and tap Battery.

2. Check the Daily Usage chart.
![The Battery Settings screen, showing Daily Usage.](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/ios/ios-26-iphone-16-pro-settings-battery-crop.png)

The Daily Usage chart summarizes today's battery usage as compared with your usage over the last seven days. You'll see a message that indicates if you're using more, less, or similar battery as usual.

Below the chart, App and System Activity Usage shows the three apps that have used the most battery today. You can tap an app to display a chart showing today's battery usage for that app as compared with the usage over the last seven days.

To learn more about your battery usage, tap View All Battery Usage. A Daily Usage chart compares today's battery usage as compared with the usage over the last seven days. Tap a bar for a day to see detailed usage for that day. App and System Activity Usage shows battery usage throughout the day for a list of the apps you've used as well as system activities that have used battery.

In the details for each app, you might see usage types like these:

- Background Activity: Most of the app's battery usage—such as playing music or tracking location—happened while the app was active in the background. You can improve battery life by reducing your use of the app.

- No Mobile Coverage and Low Signal: Your device was searching for a Wi-Fi or cellular signal or being used in a low signal environment, which affected battery life. To improve battery life, if you can, [use your device in a place with a strong signal](https://support.apple.com/en-us/120745#signal). If you know you'll be in an area with no coverage for some time, [enable Airplane Mode](https://support.apple.com/en-us/108785).

- Notifications: The app's notifications wake the device. To improve battery life, you can adjust the app's notifications to wake the device less often.

- Connected to Charger: The app was used only while your device was charging, so the battery wasn't used.

metadata.title: If the battery in your iPhone or iPad drains too quickly - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: If the battery in your iPhone or iPad drains too quickly - Apple Support]

improve battery life, you can adjust the app's notifications to wake the device less often.

- Connected to Charger: The app was used only while your device was charging, so the battery wasn't used.

## Use Wi-Fi when you can

Wi-Fi uses less battery power than cellular networks. When you use apps that use the internet, make sure that Wi-Fi is on and connect to a Wi-Fi network if possible:

1. Open Settings and tap Wi-Fi.

2. Choose a Wi-Fi network.

If you can, use your device in a place with a strong signal. Both Wi-Fi and cellular connections use less energy when used in places with high signal strength. If you know you'll be in an area with no coverage for some time, [enable Airplane Mode](https://support.apple.com/en-us/108785).

With iPhone 12 models and later, iPhone SE (3rd generation), and [iPad models with 5G](https://support.apple.com/en-us/108787), you can turn on 5G Auto, which enables Smart Data mode. When 5G speeds don’t provide a noticeably better experience, your device automatically switches to LTE, saving battery life.

To turn on 5G Auto:

1. For iPhone, open Settings, tap Cellular, tap Cellular Data Options, and then tap Voice & Data. For iPad, open Settings, tap Cellular Data Options, and then tap Data.

2. Tap 5G Auto.

## If battery life is less than expected after an update

If you notice that your battery life has decreased after updating your device, wait a few days and then check again.

Even though you can use your device immediately after an update, certain tasks related to the update continue in the background and might affect battery life and thermal performance. While they're ongoing, if you open Settings and tap Battery, you might see the Insight [Ongoing iOS Update](https://support.apple.com/en-us/120745#Insights).

## Use Adaptive Power

With Adaptive Power, your iPhone can automatically extend battery life on days when you're using more power than usual. Adaptive Power can predict when you'll need extra battery and then make performance adjustments to help your battery last longer. When active, Adaptive Power can even turn on Low Power Mode to extend battery life automatically when your battery reaches 20 percent.

You can use Adaptive Power with iOS 26 or later and one of these devices:

- iPhone 17 models

- iPhone Air

- iPhone 16 models

- iPhone 15 Pro Max

- iPhone 15 Pro

[Learn more about Adaptive Power](https://support.apple.com/en-us/123707)

metadata.title: If the battery in your iPhone or iPad drains too quickly - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: If the battery in your iPhone or iPad drains too quickly - Apple Support]

later and one of these devices:

- iPhone 17 models

- iPhone Air

- iPhone 16 models

- iPhone 15 Pro Max

- iPhone 15 Pro

[Learn more about Adaptive Power](https://support.apple.com/en-us/123707)

## Use Low Power Mode

You can extend the battery life of your device by turning on [Low Power Mode](https://support.apple.com/en-us/101604). Low Power Mode reduces the amount of power that your device uses by reducing or disabling certain settings and features, like Mail fetch, Hey Siri, Background App Refresh, and some visual effects.

To use Low Power Mode:

1. Open Settings, tap Battery, and then tap Power Mode.

2. Turn on Low Power Mode.

You can also turn on Low Power Mode using [Control Center on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-us/108330) or [Control Center on iPad](https://support.apple.com/en-us/111790).

Low Power Mode automatically turns off when you have charged your device to above 80 percent.

## Check battery health

How long a battery lasts depends on battery capacity and your use of software and hardware features.

iPhone and iPad batteries, like all rechargeable batteries, have limited lifespan and might eventually need to be replaced. Here's where you can check your battery capacity:

- On iPhone 15 and later and [certain iPad models](https://support.apple.com/en-us/117759): Open Settings, tap Battery, and then tap Battery Health.

- On iPhone 14 and earlier: Open Settings, tap Battery, and then tap Battery Health & Charging.

If you see Service next to Battery Health, consider replacing your battery to restore full performance and capacity. Follow the onscreen instructions.

## Learn more

- [Learn more about iPhone battery health and capacity](https://support.apple.com/en-us/101575)

- [How to check battery health and history on your iPad](https://support.apple.com/en-us/117759)

- [Charge and maintain your iPhone battery](https://support.apple.com/en-us/105105)

- [Learn how to maximize battery performance](https://www.apple.com/batteries/maximizing-performance/)

- [Charge and maintain your iPad battery](https://support.apple.com/en-us/118418)

[Source: apple_support] [Title: How to download iOS 26 - Apple Support]

# How to download iOS 26

Learn how to download iOS 26.

![iOS 26 icon](https://cdsassets.apple.com/live/7WUAS350/images/ios/ios-26-badge-icon.png)

Before downloading new software, back up your device to make sure that you have a copy of your important information. [Choose your backup method](https://support.apple.com/en-us/118426).

## **Check compatibility**

You will only see updates that are compatible with your current software version. If you have not downloaded the most current release, you will not see the option to install the latest release.

iOS 26 is compatible with the following iPhone models:

- iPhone 17

- iPhone 17 Pro

- iPhone 17 Pro Max

- iPhone Air

- iPhone 16e

- iPhone 16

- iPhone 16 Plus

- iPhone 16 Pro

- iPhone 16 Pro Max

- iPhone 15

- iPhone 15 Plus

- iPhone 15 Pro

- iPhone 15 Pro Max

- iPhone 14

- iPhone 14 Plus

- iPhone 14 Pro

- iPhone 14 Pro Max

- iPhone 13

- iPhone 13 mini

- iPhone 13 Pro

- iPhone 13 Pro Max

- iPhone 12

- iPhone 12 mini

- iPhone 12 Pro

- iPhone 12 Pro Max

- iPhone 11

- iPhone 11 Pro

- iPhone 11 Pro Max

- iPhone SE (2nd generation and later)

[Learn more about iOS 26](https://www.apple.com/os/ios/).

## Download iOS 26

You can set up your iPhone to install system software and updates automatically, or you can check for updates manually in the Software Update settings.

### Turn on automatic updates

1. Open Settings.

2. Tap General.

3. Tap Software Update.

4. Tap Automatic Updates.

5. Choose one of the following options:

1. Automatically Install: When an update is available, iPhone downloads and installs the update overnight while charging and connected to Wi-Fi. You’re notified before an update is installed.

2. Automatically Download: Downloads updates automatically when your iPhone connects to Wi-Fi, charging, and locked so you can install them later. To see this option, first turn off Automatically Install.

### Install software updates

1. Open Settings.

2. Tap General.

3. Tap Software Update. The screen shows the installed version of iOS and whether an update is available.

4. If an update is available, tap Download and Install, then follow the onscreen instructions.

# Create original images with Image Playground on iPhone

In the Image Playground app, you can use Apple Intelligence\* to combine concepts, text descriptions, and people from your photo library to create images within seconds. You can also use Image Playground to create images in [Messages](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/create-original-images-with-image-playground-iph0063238b5/26/ios/26#iph7575bc65b), Freeform, and more.

![The Image Playground app window showing a created image of a dog wearing sunglasses.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/02204e51f1b0750d1e325d3c6ecffe27.png)

_Note:_ Apple Intelligence is not available on all [iPhone models](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/aside/iph275f4d617/26/ios/26) or in all languages or regions.\*\* To access the most recent available features, make sure you’re using the [latest version of iOS](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/aside/update_os/26/ios/26) and have [Apple Intelligence turned on](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/aside/iph3212e4900/26/ios/26).

## Create or edit an image

1. Go to the Image Playground app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/29a4f3117f60fda49acbc1aa49048f90.png) on your iPhone.

2. Do one of the following:

   - _Create a new image:_ Tap New Image.

   - _Edit an existing image:_ Tap an image, then tap Edit.
3. Add what you want to your image. You can combine up to seven elements to create original images.

   - _Concepts:_ Swipe left to browse concepts—themes, expressions, costumes, accessories, or places—then tap an option.

   - _A description:_ Tap “Describe an image,” enter a description, then tap ![the Send button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e5153ea84d2f18d6e5c985931efb9ab2.png).

   - _A person:_ Tap ![the Choose Person button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6e63887a664af76a52301cb02caf2b4a.png), choose a person from your photo library, then choose a starting point.

     To add a person from Photos, you need to [name them in the Photos app](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/find-and-name-people-and-pets-iph9c7ee918c/26/ios/26). If you already added a person and want to change how they look, tap ![the Choose Person button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6e63887a664af76a52301cb02caf2b4a.png), tap Edit, choose a new starting point, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3e3842b6ad789d0a02161c56d7037250.png). See [Customize a person’s appearance](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/create-original-images-with-image-playground-iph0063238b5/26/ios/26#iph83b5a04c0).

     If you provide a description that’s different from the image you use—for example, the name in Photos doesn’t match the name in your description—your image is personalized based on the photo you selected rather than the text description.

     **Tip:** You can also create an image of a person without using a photo from your library. Tap ![the Choose Person button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6e63887a664af76a52301cb02caf2b4a.png), tap Appearance, choose a skin tone and appearance, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3e3842b6ad789d0a02161c56d7037250.png).

   - _A phot

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Create original images with Image Playground on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)]

hoose a skin tone and appearance, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3e3842b6ad789d0a02161c56d7037250.png).

   - _A photo of something—like a pet, nature, or food:_ Tap ![the Photo Browser button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7aa3fba67a2a896892fea13eec0b8cca.png), then choose an option.
4. Tap Style, then select a style.

   - _Apple Intelligence styles:_ Select Animation, Illustration, or Sketch, or create Genmoji. When you [create Genmoji](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/create-your-own-emoji-with-genmoji-iph4e76f5667/26/ios/26), they’re saved with your stickers.

   - _ChatGPT styles:\*\*\*_ Tap ChatGPT, then select a style, or choose Any Style to describe what you want. For example, you could enter “a realistic image of a stork” for a baby shower invitation.

_Note:_ Changing the image style creates a new set of previews.

5. Refine your image by doing any of the following:

   - _See variations:_ Tap the preview bubble, then swipe left or right.

   - _Remove a concept:_ Tap ![the Delete button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c9919e3723f54a73de9bba2187445f66.png).
6. When you’re happy with your image, tap Save in the top-right corner to save it to your gallery. If you created a Genmoji, tap Add Emoji to add it with your stickers.

If you’re editing a previously saved image, tap Done—you’re asked if you want to update it or save it as a new image.

To view the image in your gallery, tap ![the Exit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ea56b8edd3ca92d7af8adc2abea59bd5.png).

In addition to using the Image Playground app, you can also use Image Playground features to create original images in apps like Freeform and Messages. See [Create and send original images in Messages](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/create-original-images-with-image-playground-iph0063238b5/26/ios/26#iph7575bc65b) and [Create original images in Freeform](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/create-original-images-with-image-playground-iph0063238b5/26/ios/26#iph9908e64fc).

metadata.title: Create original images with Image Playground on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Create original images with Image Playground on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)]

ion of iOS](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/aside/update_os/26/ios/26) and have [Apple Intelligence turned on](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/aside/iph3212e4900/26/ios/26).

## Customize a person’s appearance

When you create an image based on a person from your photo library, you can change their appearance by choosing a different photo as a starting point or changing personal attributes like hairstyle, facial hair, and eyewear.

1. Go to the Image Playground app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/29a4f3117f60fda49acbc1aa49048f90.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap New Image, or tap an image, then tap Edit.

3. Do any of the following:

   - _Choose a different photo:_ Tap Choose Other Photo, then tap a photo to choose it.

   - _Choose the hairstyle, facial hair, or eyewear:_ Tap Customize Appearance, then choose options from the pop-up menus.

     **Tip:** If the starting point still isn’t quite right, try entering a description in the “Additional details” field. For example, if you added prescription glasses, but they’re the wrong color, you can enter “blue glasses” or “make the glasses yellow.”

   - _Return to the original starting point:_ Tap Reset Appearance.
4. When you’re happy with your starting point, tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3e3842b6ad789d0a02161c56d7037250.png).

metadata.title: Create original images with Image Playground on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Create original images with Image Playground on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)]

nce.
4. When you’re happy with your starting point, tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3e3842b6ad789d0a02161c56d7037250.png).

## Copy, share, or save an image

1. Go to the Image Playground app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/29a4f3117f60fda49acbc1aa49048f90.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap an image, then do any of the following:

   - _Copy or duplicate your image:_ Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38f95724ed0a62ccb8a071d3df51b3b2.png), then tap Copy or Duplicate.

   - _Add a caption to your image:_ Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38f95724ed0a62ccb8a071d3df51b3b2.png), tap Add Caption, enter an image description, then tap Done.

   - _Share your image:_ Tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/748a151e2276ad5df4d03ec8d4506bf7.png), then choose a share option.

   - _Save your image to your photo library:_ Tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/748a151e2276ad5df4d03ec8d4506bf7.png), then tap Save Image.

## Delete an image

_Note:_ If you delete an image from Image Playground, it’s deleted on all your devices where you’re signed in to the same [Apple Account](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/aside/iph39beaede9/26/ios/26).

1. Go to the Image Playground app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/29a4f3117f60fda49acbc1aa49048f90.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap an image in the gallery, then tap ![the Delete button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4674dda5f890754954a91b420bfb1c92.png).

metadata.title: Create original images with Image Playground on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Create original images with Image Playground on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)]

our iPhone.

2. Tap an image in the gallery, then tap ![the Delete button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4674dda5f890754954a91b420bfb1c92.png).

## Create and send original images in Messages

You can create images with Image Playground directly in the Messages app.

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Start a new message or open a conversation.

3. Tap ![the Apps button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e174be8448abb41f13837019b102e688.png), then tap Image Playground.

4. Do one of the following:

   - _Select an image you previously created:_ Tap Gallery at the top, select your image, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

   - _Create a new image:_ Tap Create at the top, follow instructions to [create an image](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/create-original-images-with-image-playground-iph0063238b5/26/ios/26#iph490bc6603), then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).
5. When you’re ready to send your image, tap ![the Send button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f5dabff8f401af1580540b6ab7aedcb8.png).

metadata.title: Create original images with Image Playground on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Create original images with Image Playground on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)]

## Create original images in Freeform

You can create images with Image Playground directly in the Freeform app.

1. Go to the Freeform app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8adc98e6e3712b407ec28063cfd0709b.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a board, or tap ![the New Board button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/678b074491ca5209414815750e75fea1.png) to start a new one.

3. Do one of the following:

   - _Create a new image:_ Tap ![the Insert Media button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/025baf2be112a826d2a87dbb0e230d26.png) in the toolbar, tap Image Playground, then follow instructions to [create an image](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/create-original-images-with-image-playground-iph0063238b5/26/ios/26#iph490bc6603).

   - _Use elements from your board:_ [Select](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/draw-or-handwrite-iphe749c1957/26/ios/26#iph0d8bb647f) up to six images, drawings, text boxes, stickies, or shapes that you want to use, tap them, then tap Add to Playground.

   - _Replace an existing image with an image created in Image Playground:_ [Add an image](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/add-images-scans-links-and-other-files-iphd301a8bf1/26/ios/26), select the image, tap ![the Modify Image button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/297c1374fe7c50f0c764d49e1a413977.png), tap Replace, tap Image Playground, then follow instructions to [create an image](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/create-original-images-with-image-playground-iph0063238b5/26/ios/26#iph490bc6603).
4. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/02981b7364cd7a2928906668ec4494fc.png) to add the image to your board.

You can also use Image Playground in other apps—such as Keynote, Pages, Apple Invites, and supported third-party apps.

You can block access to image creation features, like Image Playground, in Screen Time settings. See [Block access to image creation features](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/block-access-to-apple-intelligence-features-iph3fed3f2c3/26/ios/26#iphb11516d73).

**See also** [Create your own emoji with Genmoji on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/create-your-own-emoji-with-genmoji-iph4e76f5667/26/ios/26) [Block ac

# Adjust HDR camera settings on iPhone

_HDR_ (high dynamic range) helps you get great shots in high-contrast situations. The iPhone camera takes several photos in rapid succession at different exposures and blends them together to bring more highlight and shadow detail to your photos.

By default, iPhone takes photos in HDR (for the rear camera and the front camera) when it’s most effective.

[Supported models](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/aside/iphc1127fd5d/26/ios/26) record video in HDR to capture true-to-life color and contrast.

## Turn off automatic HDR

By default, iPhone automatically uses HDR when it’s most effective. On some iPhone models, you can manually control HDR instead.

On iPhone 11 models, iPhone SE (2nd generation), and iPhone 12, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Camera, then turn off Smart HDR. Then from the camera screen, tap HDR to turn it off or on.

metadata.title: Adjust HDR camera settings on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Adjust HDR camera settings on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)]

com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Camera, then turn off Smart HDR. Then from the camera screen, tap HDR to turn it off or on.

## Turn HDR video off and on

On [supported models](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/aside/iphc1127fd5d/26/ios/26), iPhone records video in Dolby Vision HDR for true-to-life color and contrast. To turn off HDR video recording, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Camera > Record Video, then turn off HDR Video or HDR (depending on your model).

**See also** [Change advanced camera settings on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/change-advanced-camera-settings-iphb362b394e/26/ios/26) [Record a video with your iPhone camera](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/record-a-video-iph61f49e4bb/26/ios/26) [Record ProRes video with your iPhone camera](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/record-prores-video-iphde02c478d/26/ios/26) [Change video recording settings on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/change-video-recording-settings-iphc1827d32f/26/ios/26)

# Sell, give away, or trade in your iPhone

Before you sell, give away, or trade in your iPhone, see the Apple Support article [What to do before you sell, give away, or trade in your iPhone, iPad, or iPod touch](https://support.apple.com/109511), and be sure to perform the following tasks:

- If you paired an Apple Watch with your iPhone, unpair your Apple Watch. (See the Apple Support article [Unpair and erase your Apple Watch](https://support.apple.com/108372).)

- [Back up iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/back-up-iphone-iph3ecf67d29/26/ios/26). If you replace one iPhone with another, you can [use the setup assistant](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/turn-on-and-set-up-iphone-iph1fd7e482f/26/ios/26) to restore the backup to your new iPhone.

If you’re replacing your iPhone with another one you have on hand, you can use extra free storage in iCloud to move your apps and data from one iPhone to another. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f6b5581fdf38013a42e265720f20b597.png) \> General > Transfer or Reset iPhone > Get Started, then follow the onscreen instructions.

- Sign out of iCloud, the iTunes Store, and the App Store. Go to Settings > \[ _your name_\], scroll down, tap Sign Out, enter your Apple Account password, then tap Turn Off.

- [Erase all content and settings](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/erase-iphone-iph7a2a9399b/26/ios/26) that contain personal information. If you previously [turned on Find My](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/locate-a-device-iph09b087eda/26/ios/26) for your iPhone, Activation Lock is removed when you erase iPhone, making it ready for a new owner.

# Get started with FaceTime on iPhone

Learn how to use the FaceTime app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/a09a8a59a86fc2415cda046ee0aaa5fd.png) to visit face-to-face with friends and family—over Wi-Fi or cellular. In a FaceTime call, you can also watch TV shows and movies, listen to music, and even work out together.

![The FaceTime Settings screen, showing the switch to turn FaceTime on or off and the field with your Apple Account for FaceTime.](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/68348a4c879bb4e49b1ae9f41c9f90a1.png)

## Set up FaceTime

To get started with FaceTime, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Apps > FaceTime, then sign in to your Apple Account or turn on FaceTime. If more than one contact method appears below “You can be reached by FaceTime at,” you can tap to select or deselect it.

![The screen for initiating a FaceTime call, showing the Create Link button and the New FaceTime button for starting a FaceTime call.](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/1e1760e351688ca77d44b9aa0fd9e74a.png)

metadata.title: Get started with FaceTime on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Get started with FaceTime on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)]

eate Link button and the New FaceTime button for starting a FaceTime call.](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/1e1760e351688ca77d44b9aa0fd9e74a.png)

## Make a FaceTime call

Open the FaceTime app, tap New FaceTime, then enter the name or phone number of the person (or people) you want to call. Tap ![the FaceTime button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/b26bf8e27b8c7723209c06db9b3eb16a.png) to make a video call or ![the Call button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/e276499ce06e7fadbe47518d9847407d.png) to make an audio call (not available in all countries or regions). You can talk with up to 32 people on a call.

If you want to call someone who doesn’t have an Apple device, you can create and send a link to the call in Messages or Mail. To get started, open FaceTime, then tap Create Link.

![The FaceTime screen showing a call in progress. The caller’s image appears in a small rectangle in the lower right, and the image of the other person fills the rest of the screen. Across the bottom of the screen are the Live Photo, Effects, and Flip to Back Camera buttons. The FaceTime controls are at the top of the screen, including the Speaker, Camera, Mute, Share Content, and End buttons. At the top of the controls are the name or Apple Account of the person you’re talking to.](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/9264f98c32529f60a757f24138eec0c7.png)

## Use the FaceTime controls

While on a FaceTime call, use the FaceTime controls to turn your speaker, camera, or mic on or off, take a Live Photo, and more. If you don’t see the controls, tap your screen.

![A FaceTime call with a SharePlay session, showing Apple TV+ video content being shared in the call. The person sharing the content is shown in the small window, the video fills the rest of the screen, and the playback controls are on top of the video.](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/b98bc38f5b790b55f9123fc960a796d1.png)

metadata.title: Get started with FaceTime on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Get started with FaceTime on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)]

the rest of the screen, and the playback controls are on top of the video.](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/b98bc38f5b790b55f9123fc960a796d1.png)

## Watch, listen, and play together

To get started sharing music or video together, or working out together during a FaceTime call, tap ![the Share Content button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/e6784006b85b19a0d11885ba22958cc1.png) in the FaceTime controls (if you don’t see ![the Share Content button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/e6784006b85b19a0d11885ba22958cc1.png), tap the screen).

Scroll through the apps below Listen and Play Together, then select one (for example, Apple TV app, Music, or Fitness).

Want to learn more?

- [Add people to a FaceTime call](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/make-a-group-facetime-call-iph405ab67de/18.0/ios/18.0#iph1a781542e)

- [Use SharePlay to watch, listen, and play together in FaceTime](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/shareplay-watch-listen-play-iphb657eb791/18.0/ios/18.0)

- [Change your FaceTime video settings](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/change-the-facetime-video-settings-iphfb3d2a12b/18.0/ios/18.0)

- [Change your FaceTime audio settings](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/change-the-facetime-audio-settings-iphb54d5dee2/18.0/ios/18.0)

# Unsend and edit messages on iPhone

You can unsend and edit recent text messages, giving you the opportunity to fix a typo or pull back a message that you accidentally sent to the wrong person.

![A conversation in Messages showing the last message being edited. The rest of the conversation is lighter, except for the specific text message that’s being edited. The conversation shows that a previous message was unsent, and this message was edited before this current edit.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7c3f971db61b0ed6c14b7494f8c1dc15.png)

_Note:_ To unsend or edit text messages, you must be using [iMessage](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/about-imessage-iph4e9799206/26/ios/26) with iOS 16, iPadOS 16.1, macOS 13, visionOS 1, or later. If you unsend a message to someone who’s using an earlier operating system, the original message remains in the conversation, and you’re notified that the recipient may still see the original message. If you edit a message to someone who’s using an earlier operating system, they receive follow-up messages with the preface “Edited to” and your new message in quotation marks. SMS, MMS, or RCS text messages can’t be edited or unsent. However, SMS, MMS, or RCS text messages can be edited in a group conversation as long as there’s at least one other iMessage user in the group.

## Unsend a message

You can undo a recently sent message for up to 2 minutes after sending it.

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Touch and hold the message bubble, then tap Undo Send.

A note confirming that you unsent the message appears in both conversation transcripts: yours and your recipient’s.

Unsending removes the message from your recipient’s device.

metadata.title: Unsend and edit messages on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Unsend and edit messages on iPhone – Apple Support (UK)]

, then tap Undo Send.

A note confirming that you unsent the message appears in both conversation transcripts: yours and your recipient’s.

Unsending removes the message from your recipient’s device.

## Edit a sent message

You can edit a recently sent message up to five times within 15 minutes of sending it.

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Select a conversation with the message you want to edit.

3. Touch and hold the message bubble, then tap Edit.

4. Make any changes, then tap ![the Send Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7c29db565673f106ec2610dbf4a90268.png) to resend with edits or ![the Cancel Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c49333ed954a7b2d7b5ab9f9f3537f12.png) to revert.

_Note:_ The message is marked as Edited in the conversation transcript.

The message bubble updates to reflect your edits on both your iPhone and your recipient’s device, and both of you can tap Edited to see previous versions of your message.

**See also** [Delete messages and attachments in Messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/delete-messages-and-attachments-iph2c9c4bfcb/26/ios/26) [Keep track of messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/keep-track-of-messages-iphe9b48b89e/26/ios/26) [Screen and filter text messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-gb/guide/iphone/screen-and-filter-texts-iph203ab0be4/26/ios/26)

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: About iMessage - Apple Support]

# About iMessage

iMessage is a secure messaging service that you can use to send and receive text messages in the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, and Apple Vision Pro.

If someone sends a message to your email address or phone number using iMessage, you receive the message [on all your Apple devices](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-messages-iph3d039b67/26/ios/26#iph6fc6b7653) that are set up to receive messages sent to that email address or phone number. When you view an iMessage conversation, you see all messages sent from any device, so you can keep in touch with others wherever you are.

You need an [Apple Account](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphfd5021a18/26/ios/26) to use some iMessage features. If you’ve made purchases from the iTunes Store or App Store or you’ve signed in to iCloud, you have an Apple Account.

![A conversation using iMessage.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/d712294a4c21ba6b1d074d315b1dea74.png)

Some things to know about communicating in iMessage:

- You can send texts over Wi-Fi or cellular service.

- Texts you send and receive using iMessage don’t count against your SMS, MMS, or RCS allowances in your cellular messaging plan, but cellular data rates may apply.

- When you communicate with others who also use iMessage on an Apple device, the texts appear in blue bubbles. (SMS, MMS, and RCS messages appear in green bubbles.)

Messages are sent with iMessage when you see a blue ![the Send button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f5dabff8f401af1580540b6ab7aedcb8.png) send button. A green send button indicates the message is sent with SMS, MMS, or RCS.

- In a conversation, you can see if someone’s writing a message. If someone has [read receipts](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-read-receipts-on-or-off-iph5e713a045/26/ios/26) turned on, you can also see if they’ve opened your message.

- You can use a wider range of apps and features, like [text formatting](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/format-text-and-animate-messages-iphe5c5af4d4/26/ios/26), [inline replies](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/send-and-reply-to-messages-iph82fb73ba3/26/ios/26#iph89c62f75e), [editing](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/unsend-and-edit-mess

metadata.title: About iMessage - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: About iMessage - Apple Support]

/ios/26), [inline replies](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/send-and-reply-to-messages-iph82fb73ba3/26/ios/26#iph89c62f75e), [editing](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/unsend-and-edit-messages-iphe67195653/26/ios/26#iph023262dda), [unsending](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/unsend-and-edit-messages-iphe67195653/26/ios/26#iph877f4c34b), [scheduling](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/schedule-a-text-message-to-send-later-iph5ae9a7be6/26/ios/26), [backgrounds](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-backgrounds-iph605fa06e4/26/ios/26), [polls](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/poll-people-in-a-conversation-iphde1787df4/26/ios/26), [Memoji](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/create-and-send-memoji-iphd730f04a7/26/ios/26), [drawings](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/draw-and-handwrite-messages-iph3d4cb79c9/26/ios/26), [collaboration](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/collaborate-on-projects-iphf08c82a16/26/ios/26), [group conversation management](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/group-conversations-iphb10c80fc5/26/ios/26), and more.

- A greater range of options are available when you [react with Tapbacks](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/react-with-tapbacks-iph018d3c336/26/ios/26), [send audio messages](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/send-and-receive-audio-messages-iph2e42d3117/26/ios/26), or [send via satellite](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/text-via-satellite-iphb9262f4dd/26/ios/26).

- When you use iMessage, you can [report spam messages](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/report-spam-and-block-senders-iph3f94d910d/26/ios/26#iph2ad4f25a1) to Apple and [screen unknown senders](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/screen-and-filter-texts-iph203ab0be4/26/ios/26#iphb62e224e4).

- For security, messages sent using iMessage are end-to-end encrypted before they’re sent, and you can also use [Contact Key Verification](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-contact-key-verification-iph654dd8c53/26/ios/26).

![The Add menu in Messages on iPhone, listing several iMessage features: audio messaging, the App Store, GIF images, Check In, Digital Touch, location sharing, and Memoji.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/0304619af6cecf6f20d624cd333b7d47.png)

**See also** [Turn on iMessage](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-messages-iph3d039b67/26/ios/26#iphe50bc4b2a) [Set up Messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/ip

# Access books on other Apple devices in the Books app on iPhone

You can access your books and audiobooks in the Books app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f8320af1656515771e747cbc0eed5bd3.png) on your other Apple devices such as your iPad, Mac, and Apple Vision Pro. You can also sync your reading position, highlights, notes, library collections, and more.

_Note:_ Apple Books isn’t available in all countries or regions. See the Apple Support article [Availability of Apple Media Services](https://support.apple.com/118205).

## Access your books on your other devices

To keep your Books content and information updated across your other Apple devices, sign in to the same [Apple Account](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphfd5021a18/26/ios/26) on each device, then do any of the following:

- _Automatically download purchases made on other devices:_ Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Books, then turn on Purchases from Other Devices.

- _Sync reading position, bookmarks, notes, and highlights:_ Go to Settings > \[ _your name_\] \> iCloud, tap See All next to Saved to iCloud, then turn on Books.

- _Sync Home, Library, and collections:_ Go to Settings > Apps > Books, then below Syncing, turn on Home and iCloud Drive.

metadata.title: Access books on other Apple devices in the Books app on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Access books on other Apple devices in the Books app on iPhone - Apple Support]

name_\] \> iCloud, tap See All next to Saved to iCloud, then turn on Books.

- _Sync Home, Library, and collections:_ Go to Settings > Apps > Books, then below Syncing, turn on Home and iCloud Drive.

## Access your books on your Mac

To see your books, audiobooks, and PDFs on your Mac, do one of the following:

- _macOS 15 or later:_ Choose Apple menu ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b9fde8d26f37cf34243c7d64f3508b17.png) \> System Settings, click your name at the top of the sidebar, then click iCloud. Click iCloud Drive, click Apps Syncing to iCloud Drive, then turn on Books.

- _macOS 13–14.5:_ Choose Apple menu > System Settings, click your name at the top of the sidebar, then click iCloud. Click iCloud Drive, click Apps syncing to iCloud Drive, then select Books. Click Back, then click Done.

- _macOS 10.15–12.5:_ Choose Apple menu > System Preferences, then click Apple ID. Click iCloud in the sidebar, then select iCloud Drive. Click Options, then select Books.

- _macOS 10.14 or earlier:_ Choose Apple menu > System Preferences, then click iCloud. Select iCloud Drive, click Options, then select Books.

To see your collections, bookmarks, notes, and highlights on your Mac, open Books, then do one of the following:

- _macOS 13 or later:_ Choose Books > Settings, click General, then select “Collections, bookmarks, and highlights.”

- _macOS 12.5 or earlier:_ Choose Books > Preferences, click General, then select “Collections, bookmarks, and highlights.”

**See also** [Buy books and audiobooks on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/buy-books-and-audiobooks-iphab21972d/26/ios/26) [Annotate books in the Books app on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/annotate-books-iph17bf340c1/26/ios/26)
