# Add backgrounds in Messages on iPhone

You can add a background to an iMessage conversation. Other people in the conversation see the background on their devices too and can change the background for the group.

_Note:_ To add, view, or change conversation backgrounds, you must use iMessage on iOS 26, iPadOS 26, macOS Tahoe, watchOS 26, visionOS 26, or later.

![A Messages conversation with a background.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9704d790abc952ddc2dcd86f578dafe9.png)

## Use a dynamic background

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Go to the conversation where you want to add a background.

3. Tap the contact or group icon at the top of the conversation, then tap Backgrounds.

4. Tap Color, Sky, Water, or Aurora.

5. Swipe left to choose an option.

**Tip:** For Color, tap the swatches in the bottom corners to adjust the colors.

6. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7c29db565673f106ec2610dbf4a90268.png) to add the background to your conversation.

metadata.title: Add backgrounds in Messages on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Add backgrounds in Messages on iPhone - Apple Support]

ors.

6. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7c29db565673f106ec2610dbf4a90268.png) to add the background to your conversation.

## Use a photo as a background

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Go to the conversation where you want to add a photo to the background.

3. Tap the contact or group icon at the top of the conversation, then tap Backgrounds.

4. Tap Photo, then tap to select a photo from your library.

**Tip:** If someone shared a photo you want to use as a background, touch and hold the photo in the message, then tap Set as Background.

5. Do any of the following:

   - _Crop the image:_ Pinch in or out.

   - _Remove the color:_ Swipe left to Black & White.

   - _Apply a color filter:_ Swipe left to Duotone or Color Wash, then tap the color swatch to change the palette.
6. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7c29db565673f106ec2610dbf4a90268.png) to add the background to your conversation.

You can set conversations to showcase backgrounds when you return to them. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Messages, then turn on Start with Photos Visible.

If Apple Intelligence\* is turned on, you can use Image Playground to create one-of-a-kind images for the background of your conversations. See [Use Apple Intelligence in Messages](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-apple-intelligence-in-messages-iph64709c5c3/26/ios/26).

_Note:_ You can have your (or a family member’s) iPhone receive warnings before a background image containing sensitive content is sent or received. See [Receive warnings about sensitive content](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/receive-warnings-about-sensitive-content-iphede874992/26/ios/26) or [Check for sensitive images and videos](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/check-for-sensitive-images-and-videos-iph0d3607e18/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Add backgrounds in Messages on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Add backgrounds in Messages on iPhone - Apple Support]

rnings-about-sensitive-content-iphede874992/26/ios/26) or [Check for sensitive images and videos](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/check-for-sensitive-images-and-videos-iph0d3607e18/26/ios/26).

## Revert to a plain background

_Note:_ Removing a background affects all participants in a conversation.

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Go to the conversation with a background you want to remove.

3. Tap the contact or group icon at the top of the conversation, then tap Backgrounds.

4. Tap None.

## Turn conversation backgrounds off

When you turn conversation backgrounds off, others in a conversation still see any background chosen for a conversation.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Apps, then tap Messages.

3. Turn Conversation Backgrounds off.

**See also** [Have a group conversation in Messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/group-conversations-iphb10c80fc5/26/ios/26) [Format text and animate messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/format-text-and-animate-messages-iphe5c5af4d4/26/ios/26) [Share content in Messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/share-photos-links-and-more-iphb66cfeaad/26/ios/26) [Collaborate on projects with Messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/collaborate-on-projects-iphf08c82a16/26/ios/26)

# Add music to iPhone and listen offline

As soon as you [subscribe to Apple Music](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/subscribe-to-apple-music-iphcfadf0701/26/ios/26), you can add and download songs and videos from Apple Music to your music library. You can stream music you add to iPhone when you have an internet connection. To play music when you’re not connected to the internet, you need to download it first.

**Siri:** [Ask Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/summon_siri/26/ios/26) something like, “Add this to my library” while playing something. [Learn how to use Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-on-and-activate-siri-iph83aad8922/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Add music to iPhone and listen offline - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Add music to iPhone and listen offline - Apple Support]

mon_siri/26/ios/26) something like, “Add this to my library” while playing something. [Learn how to use Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-on-and-activate-siri-iph83aad8922/26/ios/26).

## Add music from Apple Music to your library

1. Go to the Music app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a8584141e5732119d6e19d28e0fbd248.png) on your iPhone.

2. Do any of the following:

   - Touch and hold a song, album, playlist, or video, then tap Add.

   - While viewing the contents of an album or playlist, tap ![the Add to Library button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/0d07cb7185e2d87af8a9ad8b4ace6995.png) near the top of the screen to add the album or playlist, or tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/df71404327453fcdf3541069ec35064a.png) next to a song, then tap Add to add that song.

   - On the Now Playing screen, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/13942107464b4ee2e279ac5af2292d44.png), then tap Add.

Music you add to iPhone is also added to other devices if you’re signed in to the same Apple Account and have Sync Library turned on (go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Music, then turn on Sync Library).

metadata.title: Add music to iPhone and listen offline - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Add music to iPhone and listen offline - Apple Support]

turned on (go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Music, then turn on Sync Library).

## Download music from Apple Music to your iPhone

Before you can download music from Apple Music to your library, you need to turn on Sync Library. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Music, then turn on Sync Library. Then follow these steps to download music.

1. Go to the Music app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a8584141e5732119d6e19d28e0fbd248.png) on your iPhone.

2. Do any of the following:

   - _Download a song, album, or playlist:_ Touch and hold music you added to your library, then tap Download.

     After you add a playlist or album, you can also tap ![the Download button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ed5a4cfb7484e0e332ba1969a6f9a70e.png) at the top of the screen.

   - _Always download music:_ Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Music, then turn on Automatic Downloads.

     Songs you add are automatically downloaded to iPhone.

   - _See download progress:_ On the Library tab, tap Downloading.

_Note:_ If the music you’re downloading is available in Dolby Atmos, the Dolby icon ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2294baa58e494523831361e0a8cac116.png) appears next to the item, and you can download it either in Dolby Atmos or in stereo. To download music in Dolby Atmos when available, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Music, then turn on Download in Dolby Atmos. See [Play music in Spatial Audio with Dolby Atmos on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/play-spatial-audio-iphac459a29e/26/ios/26).

## Delete music from your library

1. Go to the Music app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a8584141e5732119d6e19d28e0fbd248.png) on your iPhone.

2. Touch and hold the song, album, playlist, or music video, then tap Delete from Library.

metadata.title: Add music to iPhone and listen offline - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Add music to iPhone and listen offline - Apple Support]

F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a8584141e5732119d6e19d28e0fbd248.png) on your iPhone.

2. Touch and hold the song, album, playlist, or music video, then tap Delete from Library.

## Manage storage space

To manage storage space, do any of the following:

- _Remove music and videos stored on iPhone:_ Go to the Music app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a8584141e5732119d6e19d28e0fbd248.png) on your iPhone, touch and hold an item that you downloaded, then tap Remove Download.

The item is removed from iPhone, but not from your music library on your other devices.

- _Free up music storage:_ If iPhone is low on storage space, you can automatically remove downloaded music that you haven’t played in a while. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Music > Optimize Storage.

- _Remove all songs or certain artists from iPhone:_ Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Music > Downloaded Music, tap Edit, then tap ![the Delete button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f49364d4831a585edf6235c7a73db060.png) next to All Songs or the artists whose music you want to delete.

- _Remove all Apple Music songs from iPhone:_ Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Music, then turn off Sync Library.

The songs are removed from iPhone but remain in your music library on your other devices. Music you purchased or synced also remains.

metadata.title: Add music to iPhone and listen offline - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Add music to iPhone and listen offline - Apple Support]

6e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Music, then turn off Sync Library.

The songs are removed from iPhone but remain in your music library on your other devices. Music you purchased or synced also remains.

## Choose cellular data options for Music

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Apps, then tap Music.

3. Turn on Cellular Data, then do any of the following:

   - Turn Download over Cellular on or off.

   - Tap Audio Quality, turn on Lossless Audio, then tap Cellular Streaming to choose a setting (None, High Efficiency, High Quality, Lossless, or High-Resolution Lossless).

     High Quality, Lossless, and High-Resolution Lossless use significantly more cellular data than High Efficiency, which may incur additional charges from your cellular carrier. Songs may also take longer to start playing.

**See also** [Search for music on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/search-for-music-iph4b506b24d/26/ios/26) [Transfer music from other music services to Apple Music on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/transfer-music-from-other-music-services-iph103c19002/26/ios/26) [Apple Support article: Migrate purchases from one Apple Account to another Apple Account](https://support.apple.com/117294)

# Add places and notes to your Places in Maps on iPhone

You can find pins, places, guides, and routes in one place: your Places in the Maps app. Add your own personal notes—visible only to you—to saved places, or pin your most frequently visited places for easier access.

![The Places screen in Maps, which shows a list of place categories like Pinned, Saved Places, and Guides. Below this, a Recently Added section lists viewed locations.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/01ca83d2f97141ad6335d883f85f9d31.png)

## Save a place

1. Go to the Maps app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5c95fac6a0912a04a330864acfcec1fb.png) on your iPhone.

2. While looking at a place card, do one of the following:

   - Tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/0d07cb7185e2d87af8a9ad8b4ace6995.png). It turns to ![the Added button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ae89099ef98753805310a8a593327c52.png).

   - Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/aad05ec99d91fb0497859fc9c0c181fb.png), then tap Add to Places.

metadata.title: Add places and notes to your Places in Maps on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Add places and notes to your Places in Maps on iPhone - Apple Support]

310a8a593327c52.png).

   - Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/aad05ec99d91fb0497859fc9c0c181fb.png), then tap Add to Places.

## Add notes to a place

You can read the notes you add to a place at any time. When you share a place, your note appears as text with the shared link, but it doesn’t save to the other person’s notes for that place.

1. Go to the Maps app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5c95fac6a0912a04a330864acfcec1fb.png) on your iPhone.

2. While looking at a place card, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/aad05ec99d91fb0497859fc9c0c181fb.png), then tap Add a Note.

3. Write something, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

Adding a note to a place saves the place to your Places.

## Add a pin to your Places

You can pin frequently visited places for easier access in Maps and CarPlay. They appear as icons in your Places and also under Pinned.

![The Maps app showing pins in Places and Recents.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/74c8150306fe66ed5f8c57714ec1bf5a.png)

1. Go to the Maps app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5c95fac6a0912a04a330864acfcec1fb.png) on your iPhone.

2. Below Places, tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5bed9fbc900ff3d17706314a2ff67f23.png) (you may need to swipe left), then search for or tap a suggestion. You can also [drop a pin](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/mark-a-location-with-a-pin-iphfa7286b57/26/ios/26#iphc4e0624cf).

3. If the pins are for your Home, Work, or School, you can do any of the following:

   - _Assign it to a type:_ Select Home, Work, or School.

   - _Sync to My Card:_ Tap Add to Contact Card.
4. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

See [Edit a pin](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/mark-a-location-with-a-pin-iphfa7286b57/26/ios/26#iphc3ff17bb4).

metadata.title: Add places and notes to your Places in Maps on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Add places and notes to your Places in Maps on iPhone - Apple Support]

E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

See [Edit a pin](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/mark-a-location-with-a-pin-iphfa7286b57/26/ios/26#iphc3ff17bb4).

## Remove a place from your Places

1. Go to the Maps app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5c95fac6a0912a04a330864acfcec1fb.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Places.

3. Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/aad05ec99d91fb0497859fc9c0c181fb.png), then tap Delete From Places.

If you’re in the place card, tap ![the Added button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ae89099ef98753805310a8a593327c52.png). It returns to ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/0d07cb7185e2d87af8a9ad8b4ace6995.png).

Removing a place from your Places deletes any personal [notes you added](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-places-and-notes-to-your-places-iph6838243e8/26/ios/26#iph128959ca1) to it.

**See also** [Organize places with custom guides in Maps on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/organize-places-with-custom-guides-iph0a53d4d7f/26/ios/26) [Get transit directions in Maps on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/get-transit-directions-ipha44f57caa/26/ios/26)

# Adjust how iPhone responds to your touch

If you have difficulties with hand tremors, dexterity, or fine motor control, you can adjust how the iPhone touchscreen responds to tap, swipe, and touch-and-hold gestures. You can have iPhone recognize faster or slower touches and ignore multiple touches. You can also prevent iPhone from waking when you touch the screen, or turn off Shake to Undo if you unintentionally shake iPhone.

metadata.title: Adjust how iPhone responds to your touch - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Adjust how iPhone responds to your touch - Apple Support]

Phone recognize faster or slower touches and ignore multiple touches. You can also prevent iPhone from waking when you touch the screen, or turn off Shake to Undo if you unintentionally shake iPhone.

## Adjust settings for taps, swipes, and multiple touches

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Touch > Touch Accommodations, then turn on Touch Accommodations.

2. You can configure iPhone to do any of the following:

   - _Respond to longer or shorter touches:_ Turn on Hold Duration, then tap ![the Decrement button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/43215f37ef59b6605cde4d5da99f6161.png) or ![the Increment button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/1c855795afee486e11acd25121b275da.png) to adjust the duration.

   - _Prevent unintended swipe gestures:_ If you turn on Hold Duration (above), you can increase the amount of movement required before a swipe gesture begins. Tap Swipe Gestures, turn on Swipe Gestures, then adjust required movement.

   - _Treat multiple touches as a single touch:_ Turn on Ignore Repeat, then tap ![the Decrement button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/43215f37ef59b6605cde4d5da99f6161.png) or ![the Increment button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/1c855795afee486e11acd25121b275da.png) to adjust the amount of time allowed between multiple touches.

   - _Respond to the first or last place you touch:_ Choose Use Initial Touch Location or Use Final Touch Location.

     If you choose Use Initial Touch Location, iPhone uses the location of your first tap—when you tap an app on the Home Screen, for example.

     If you choose Use Final Touch Location, iPhone registers the tap where you lift your finger. iPhone responds to a tap when you lift your finger within a certain period of time. Tap ![the Decrement button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/43215f37ef59b6605cde4d5da99f6161.png) or ![the Increment button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/1c855795afee486e11acd25121b275da.png) to adjust the timing. Your iPhone can respond to other gestures, such as a drag gesture, if you wait longer than the gesture delay.

metadata.title: Adjust how iPhone responds to your touch - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Adjust how iPhone responds to your touch - Apple Support]

83A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/1c855795afee486e11acd25121b275da.png) to adjust the timing. Your iPhone can respond to other gestures, such as a drag gesture, if you wait longer than the gesture delay.

## Adjust settings for touch-and-hold gestures

You use the touch-and-hold gesture to see additional options or actions you can perform, or show a preview of content. If you have trouble performing this gesture, do the following:

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Touch, then tap Haptic Touch or 3D & Haptic Touch.

2. Choose the touch duration—Fast or Slow.

3. On an [iPhone with 3D Touch](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph945ccc462/18.0/ios/18.0), you can also choose the pressure needed—Light, Medium, or Firm.

4. Test your new settings on the image at the bottom of the screen.

## Turn off Tap to Wake

On a [supported iPhone model](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphca1fc2ad9/18.0/ios/18.0), you can prevent touches on the screen from waking iPhone. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Touch, then turn off Tap to Wake.

## Turn off Shake to Undo

If you tend to unintentionally shake iPhone, you can turn off Shake to Undo. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Touch.

**Tip:** To undo text edits, swipe left with three fingers.

**See also** [Accessibility features for mobility on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/overview-accessibility-features-mobility-iph76d37ce7d/18.0/ios/18.0) [Use AssistiveTouch on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-assistivetouch-iph96b21954/18.0/ios/18.0)

# Adjust your Activity ring goals in Fitness on iPhone

You can change your personal health details, change your Activity goals, and customize your Activity ring goals by the day of the week. You can also pause your Activity rings and resume at any time.

metadata.title: Adjust your Activity ring goals in Fitness on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Adjust your Activity ring goals in Fitness on iPhone - Apple Support]

You can change your personal health details, change your Activity goals, and customize your Activity ring goals by the day of the week. You can also pause your Activity rings and resume at any time.

## Change your Activity ring goals

You can change your daily goals for each of your Activity rings. You can also customize your Activity ring goals by the day of the week.

![The Fitness Move Goal Schedule screen showing buttons to increase or decrease the Move goal by the day of the week.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/893347248464f9e33e6f636929dd7900.png)

1. Go to the Fitness app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4a171706431000fd128f989d724dd5ca.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap the Activity rings, then tap ![the Edit Goal button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3887a375232834d3790d76648695f157.png) next to an Activity ring.

3. Tap one of the following:

   - _Adjust Goal for Today:_ Set a temporary goal for your Activity ring for today.

   - _Change Daily Goal:_ Set a goal based on how active you are, or how active you want to be, each day.

**Tip:** To customize your Activity ring goal by the day of the week, tap Change Daily Goal, then tap ![the Schedule button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ce4c009de0f957b0c88249c5f3885fde.png) in the top right.

4. Tap ![the Delete button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c9919e3723f54a73de9bba2187445f66.png) or ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/443066cf6168833f76d9d7d3a2b80897.png) to adjust the goal.

5. When you’re finished, tap Change \[ _activity_\] Goal for Today, Change \[ _activity_\] Goal, or Set \[ _activity_\] Goal Schedule.

Every Monday, you’re notified about the previous week’s achievements, and you can adjust your goals for the upcoming week. Goals are suggested based on your performance in the previous week.

metadata.title: Adjust your Activity ring goals in Fitness on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Adjust your Activity ring goals in Fitness on iPhone - Apple Support]

hedule.

Every Monday, you’re notified about the previous week’s achievements, and you can adjust your goals for the upcoming week. Goals are suggested based on your performance in the previous week.

## Pause your rings

If you need to take a break, you can pause your Activity rings for up to 90 days without breaking your award streak, and resume at any time.

![The Pause Your Rings screen in Fitness, showing options to pause Activity rings.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5909f8c2b64fd691c1f08774cf9b3c6e.png)

1. Go to the Fitness app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4a171706431000fd128f989d724dd5ca.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap the Activity rings, scroll down, then tap Pause Rings.

3. Tap a duration, or tap Custom to set a custom duration.

To resume your rings, tap the Activity rings, then tap Resume Rings. To edit the duration of your pause, tap Edit Pause, set a custom duration, then tap Set.

**See also** [Fill out your Health Details in Health on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/fill-out-your-health-details-iph08022b194/26/ios/26) [See your activity summary in Fitness on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/see-your-activity-summary-iph4c34a8a95/26/ios/26) [Start a workout in Fitness on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/start-a-workout-in-fitness-iph8475d8510/26/ios/26) [Share your activity in Fitness on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/share-your-activity-iph0b826155d/26/ios/26)

# Automatically clean up your iCloud Mail inbox on iPhone

You can use iCloud Mail Cleanup to help keep your inbox uncluttered and free up storage by deleting old promotions, unsubscribing from mailing lists, and more. When you set up iCloud Mail Cleanup recommendations, they work on any device that has iCloud Mail turned on.

_Note:_ iCloud Mail Cleanup may not be available in all languages.

## Turn on iCloud Mail Cleanup

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap \[ _your name_\], then tap iCloud.

3. Below Saved to iCloud, tap Mail, tap iCloud Mail Cleanup, then turn on Use iCloud Mail Cleanup.

_Note:_ Your inbox isn’t affected until you turn on a cleanup recommendation.

You can also turn on iCloud Mail Cleanup on iCloud.com. See [Automatically clean up your iCloud Mail inbox on iCloud.com](https://support.apple.com/guide/icloud/mma3d15ee93e/).

## Turn on a cleanup recommendation

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap \[ _your name_\], then tap iCloud.

3. Below Saved to iCloud, tap Mail, then tap iCloud Mail Cleanup.

4. Tap Get Recommendation under one of the cleanup recommendations. For example, you can choose Clean up Transactions or Clean Up Promotions.

5. If necessary, tap the pop-up menu for a rule, then choose another option.

   - _Action:_ What happens to the email

   - _Status:_ Whether the action applies to unread emails, read emails, or all emails

   - _Age:_ How old the emails need to be before the action applies

**Tip:** You can edit the rules for a recommendation at any point. Tap an active recommendation, then tap a pop-up menu to choose an option.

6. Tap Turn On.

## Delete a cleanup recommendation

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap \[ _your name_\], then tap iCloud.

3. Below Saved to iCloud, tap Mail, then tap iCloud Mail Cleanup.

4. Tap an active cleanup recommendation, then turn it off.

metadata.title: Automatically clean up your iCloud Mail inbox on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Automatically clean up your iCloud Mail inbox on iPhone - Apple Support]

f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap \[ _your name_\], then tap iCloud.

3. Below Saved to iCloud, tap Mail, then tap iCloud Mail Cleanup.

4. Tap an active cleanup recommendation, then turn it off.

## Use iCloud Mail Cleanup recommendations to unsubscribe

iCloud Mail Cleanup can recommend senders in the Updates and Promotions categories to unsubscribe from in bulk, and automatically move future messages to the Trash.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap \[ _your name_\], then tap iCloud.

3. Below Saved to iCloud, tap Mail, then tap iCloud Mail Cleanup.

4. Tap Get Recommendations under Unsubscribe Recommendations.

5. Select each sender you want to unsubscribe from, then tap Unsubscribe.

_Note:_ If you don’t see any recommendations, iCloud Mail Cleanup doesn’t have any recommendations at this time.

## Remove a sender from the Unsubscribed list

If you change your mind and want to receive emails from a sender you unsubscribed from, you can remove them from the Unsubscribed list.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap \[ _your name_\], then tap iCloud.

3. Below Saved to iCloud, tap Mail, then tap iCloud Mail Cleanup.

4. Tap Unsubscribed, then tap Edit.

5. Tap ![the Delete button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/82682bc6fb9951f244f4c7c7fced97ef.png) next to each sender you want to remove from the Unsubscribed list.

To receive emails again from a sender you were previously unsubscribed from, you can sign up for updates and promotions through their website. After you resubscribe, future messages from that sender will go to your inbox.

metadata.title: Automatically clean up your iCloud Mail inbox on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Automatically clean up your iCloud Mail inbox on iPhone - Apple Support]

n from a sender you were previously unsubscribed from, you can sign up for updates and promotions through their website. After you resubscribe, future messages from that sender will go to your inbox.

## Turn off iCloud Mail Cleanup

When you turn off iCloud Mail Cleanup, iCloud Mail stops managing old messages. It also deletes the list of any senders you unsubscribed from and stops deleting their messages.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap \[ _your name_\], then tap iCloud.

3. Below Saved to iCloud, tap Mail, tap iCloud Mail Cleanup, then turn off Use iCloud Mail Cleanup.

**See also** [Automatically categorize incoming emails in Mail on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-categories-iphfe4a36baf/26/ios/26) [Check your email in Mail on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/check-your-email-iph461684497/26/ios/26) [Flag or block emails in Mail on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/flag-or-block-emails-iph3caefa61/26/ios/26)

# Change how you view events in Calendar on iPhone

You can view events across the span of a month, week, or multiple days, or view a list of upcoming events.

![A calendar in Month view showing events and reminders.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7a167465221d4c1893db79e08a8dbb49.png)

## See events in Month view

You can change your Month view to show event indicators or details.

1. Go to the Calendar app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1b944caed2601c11a4cfa936cfd46342.png) on your iPhone.

2. In Month view, tap the Month view icon, then do one of the following:

   - _Show a single bar indicator for the events on each day:_ Tap Compact ![the Compact button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/384fdba93f8ed6281c4442e2b0a9c605.png).

   - _Show a bar indicator for each event:_ Tap Stacked ![the Stacked button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1a4c4e0d41eb07cd7a69e9fe0cb3e376.png).

   - _Show event titles:_ Tap Details ![the Details button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b612271327c5a824727ded9ceb427fc8.png).

metadata.title: Change how you view events in Calendar on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Change how you view events in Calendar on iPhone - Apple Support]

cb3e376.png).

   - _Show event titles:_ Tap Details ![the Details button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b612271327c5a824727ded9ceb427fc8.png).

## View events across multiple days

1. Go to the Calendar app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1b944caed2601c11a4cfa936cfd46342.png) on your iPhone.

2. Do any of the following:

   - _View multiple days:_ In Day view, tap the Day view icon and choose Multi Day ![the Multi Day button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6fe064faa7799f918dd3200e5f7c7b80.png).

   - _View a weekly calendar:_ In Month or Day view, rotate iPhone sideways.

## View events in a list

1. Go to the Calendar app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1b944caed2601c11a4cfa936cfd46342.png) on your iPhone.

2. Do any of the following:

   - _View a list of events for each day:_ In Month view, tap the Month view icon and choose List ![the List button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/799a4d3e34da7ceaf1bb74e6395e0700.png). Tap each day to see a list of events.

   - _View a complete list of upcoming events:_ In Day view, tap the Day view icon and choose List ![the List button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/cc6667e65b317127653dd19573c8cd59.png).

# Change notification settings on iPhone

Choose when and how notifications appear, which apps can send them, and what sounds they make. You can also set up location-based alerts, allow government alerts, and more. Many notification settings apply to all app notifications, while others can be customized for individual apps.

## Change the appearance of notifications

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Notifications, then choose how you want notifications displayed on the Lock Screen:

   - _View just the number of notifications:_ Tap Count.

   - _View the notifications grouped into stacks by app:_ Tap Stack.

   - _View the notifications in a list:_ Tap List.

When notifications arrive, you can change the layout by pinching the notifications on the Lock Screen.

To turn off notifications for an app, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Notifications, tap the app, then turn off Allow Notifications.

metadata.title: Change notification settings on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Change notification settings on iPhone - Apple Support]

tings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Notifications, tap the app, then turn off Allow Notifications.

## Prevent notification previews from appearing on the Lock Screen

You can choose when to have notification previews from an app appear on your Lock Screen.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Notifications, then tap an app below Notification Style.

3. Scroll down, tap Show Previews, then select one of the following:

   - Always

   - When Unlocked

   - Never

## Change the frequency of notifications

To minimize interruptions from notifications throughout the day, you can schedule a notification summary that includes notifications for several apps at once.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Notifications, then tap Scheduled Summary.

3. Turn on Scheduled Summary, then tap Continue (if it appears).

4. Schedule times for the notification summary.

5. Tap A to Z, turn on the apps to include in your notification summary, then tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/672f0597b3bef6ad7262a337e288ceb4.png) at the top of the screen.

6. To choose when you want notification previews to appear, tap Show Previews, select an option, then tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/672f0597b3bef6ad7262a337e288ceb4.png) at the top of the screen.

Previews can include things like text (from Messages and Mail) and invitation details (from Calendar). You can override this setting for individual apps.

When you use Focus, it delays the delivery of notifications on iPhone to prevent interruptions. You can schedule a time to receive a summary of the notifications you missed. See [Schedule a notification summary](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-and-respond-to-notifications-iph6534c01bc/26/ios/26#iphb9de540c3).

metadata.title: Change notification settings on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Change notification settings on iPhone - Apple Support]

receive a summary of the notifications you missed. See [Schedule a notification summary](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-and-respond-to-notifications-iph6534c01bc/26/ios/26#iphb9de540c3).

## Choose notification sounds for individual apps

For some apps (including Mail, Messages, and Calendar), you can choose the sound that plays when a notification arrives for the app.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Notifications, select an app below Notification Style, then turn on Allow Notifications.

3. Tap Sounds, select a sound (if any appear), then tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/672f0597b3bef6ad7262a337e288ceb4.png) at the top of the screen.

You can choose immediate or scheduled delivery for the app’s notifications and where you want them to appear (on the Lock Screen, in Notification Center, or as Banner at the top of the screen), and more.

## Turn off notifications selectively for individual apps

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Notifications, tap an app, then turn off Allow Notifications.

## Turn off location-based alerts

Some apps use your location to send you relevant alerts based on where you are. For example, the Weather app might send you alerts based on your location.

If you don’t want to see these types of alerts, you can turn them off.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Privacy & Security, tap Location Services, then turn on Location Services.

3. Tap an app (if any appear in the list), then choose whether you want to share your location while using that app.

See the Apple Support article [About privacy and Location Services](https://support.apple.com/102515).

metadata.title: Change notification settings on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Change notification settings on iPhone - Apple Support]

appear in the list), then choose whether you want to share your location while using that app.

See the Apple Support article [About privacy and Location Services](https://support.apple.com/102515).

## Turn government alerts on or off

In some countries or regions, you can turn on alerts in the Government Alerts list. For example, on iPhone in the United States, you can receive National Alerts, and you can turn AMBER, Public Safety, and Emergency Alerts (which include both Severe and Extreme Imminent Threat Alerts) on or off (they’re on by default). On iPhone in Japan, you can receive Emergency Earthquake Alerts from the Japan Meteorological Agency.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Notifications, scroll down to the Government Alerts section, then turn on the alerts you want to receive.
![The Notifications screen, showing the Government Alerts you can turn on to receive government alerts.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2c31369ea548fe854a422aefaf584c42.png)
3. Tap Emergency Alerts if you want to turn on Emergency Alerts and Local Awareness.

_Note:_ In some regions, Local Awareness may improve the timeliness, accuracy, and reliability of certain Emergency Alerts. For example, Earthquake Alerts received on iPhone in California, Oregon, and Washington (including bordering regions) may be more timely or delivered with increased accuracy when Local Awareness is turned on.

![The Emergency Alerts screen, with Emergency Alerts, Local Awareness, and Always Play Sound turned on.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3b79d3ff42156b16adc1cf77608b0e01.png)
4. Turn on Always Play Sound to have Emergency Alerts play a sound even if iPhone is in Silent mode.

Government alerts vary by carrier and iPhone model, and may not work under all conditions. See the Apple Support article [About emergency and government alerts](https://support.apple.com/102516).

**See also** [Allow or silence notifications for a Focus on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/allow-or-silence-notifications-for-a-focus-iph21d43af5b/26/ios/26)

# Change your Wallet & Apple Pay settings on iPhone

In Settings, you can customize your preferences for Wallet app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/00d519c052f4af71b0bd726edc47da5d.png) and [Apple Pay](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph7799f0105/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Change your Wallet & Apple Pay settings on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Change your Wallet & Apple Pay settings on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Customize your Wallet & Apple Pay settings

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Wallet & Apple Pay, then set any of the following:

   - _Location:_ Control the [location information you share](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/control-the-location-information-you-share-iph3dd5f9be/26/ios/26).

   - _Siri & Search:_ Allow [Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-siri-suggestions-on-or-off-iph6f94af287/26/ios/26) to make suggestions across apps based on the way you use Wallet & Apple Pay. You can also allow suggestions and content from Wallet & Apple Pay to appear in [Search](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/search-on-iphone-iph3c511548/26/ios/26) and [widgets](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-edit-and-remove-widgets-iphb8f1bf206/26/ios/26).

   - _Notifications:_ Change [notification settings](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-notification-settings-iph7c3d96bab/26/ios/26).

   - _Live Activities:_ Turn on or off [Live Activities on the Lock Screen](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/access-features-from-the-lock-screen-iphcd5c65ccf/26/ios/26#iph3f3ff5648).

   - _Cellular Data:_ Turn on or off [cellular data usage](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-or-change-cellular-data-settings-iph3dd5f213/26/ios/26#iph66b77f44b).

   - _Apple Pay Defaults:_ [Change your default card, address, and preferences for Apple Pay](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-wallet-apple-pay-settings-iphf8b1fc01e/26/ios/26#iph7d4dbdf50).

   - _Apple Cash:_ Turn on or off [Apple Cash](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-apple-cash-iph385cf0980/26/ios/26#iph024874f75).

   - _AutoFill Cards:_ [Fill in payment information](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/fill-in-payment-information-iphf101498e9/26/ios/26).

   - _Express Transit Card:_ Choose cards to pay for transit automatically without requiring authentication. See [Pay for your ride with Express Transit](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/pay-for-transit-iph3fedf8b29/26/ios/26#iphb09100aa1).

   - _Hide Expired Passes:_ Turn on to [archive old passes](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/remove-cards-and-passes-iph3dd32dffe/26/ios/26#iph0f397db88).

   - _Order tracking:_ Purchases from participating merchants are automatically added to Wallet for [order tracking](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/us

metadata.title: Change your Wallet & Apple Pay settings on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Change your Wallet & Apple Pay settings on iPhone - Apple Support]

es-iph3dd32dffe/26/ios/26#iph0f397db88).

   - _Order tracking:_ Purchases from participating merchants are automatically added to Wallet for [order tracking](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-apple-pay-in-apps-and-on-the-web-iph67e89f7c8/26/ios/26#iphde508e5a2).

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Change your Wallet & Apple Pay settings on iPhone - Apple Support]

lp.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/00d519c052f4af71b0bd726edc47da5d.png) and [Apple Pay](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph7799f0105/26/ios/26).

## Change your default card, address, and preferences for Apple Pay

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Wallet & Apple Pay, then tap Apple Pay Defaults.

3. Set any of the following:

   - _Transaction defaults:_ Set the default card, your shipping address, email, and phone number.

   - _Available payment options:_ Turn on or off Pay Later, Rewards, Offers, and Other Pay Later Options.

   - _Allow Payments on Mac:_ Use Apple Pay on your iPhone to confirm payments on your nearby Mac.

   - _Double-Click Side Button:_(on an iPhone with Face ID) Your cards and passes appear on the screen when you double-click the side button.

   - _Double-Click Home Button:_(on an iPhone with a Home button) Your cards and passes appear on the screen when you double-click the Home button.

## Keep your passes up to date on your other devices

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap your name, tap iCloud, then turn on Wallet.

## Allow access to passes when iPhone is locked

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Face ID & Passcode or Touch ID & Passcode, enter your passcode, then turn on Wallet (below Allow Access When Locked).

**See also** [Set up Apple Pay in Wallet on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-apple-pay-iph9b7f53382/26/ios/26) [Fill in payment information on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/fill-in-payment-information-iphf101498e9/26/ios/26)

# Control access to information in apps on iPhone

You control whether third-party apps have access to information in Contacts, Photos, Calendar, and other apps.

## Review or change access to information in apps

The first time an app wants to use information from another app, you receive a request with an explanation. For example, a messaging app may request access to your contacts to find friends who are using the same app. After you grant or deny access, you can change access later.

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Privacy & Security.

2. Tap a category of information, such as Calendars, Reminders, or Motion & Fitness.

A list appears showing the apps that requested access. You can turn access on or off for any app on the list.

metadata.title: Control access to information in apps on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Control access to information in apps on iPhone - Apple Support]

y.

2. Tap a category of information, such as Calendars, Reminders, or Motion & Fitness.

A list appears showing the apps that requested access. You can turn access on or off for any app on the list.

## Review how apps are using the permissions you grant them

Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Privacy & Security, then tap App Privacy Report.

The App Privacy Report shows you how apps are using the permissions you granted them and shows you their network activity.

To turn off the report and delete its data, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Privacy & Security > App Privacy Report, then tap Turn Off App Privacy Report. You can return to this Settings screen to turn the report on again.

**See also** [Manage information sharing with Safety Check on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/manage-information-sharing-with-safety-check-iph42f0b2d53/26/ios/26) [Apple Support article: About App Privacy Report](https://support.apple.com/102188) [Apple Support article: About privacy and Location Services in iOS, iPadOS, and watchOS](https://support.apple.com/102515)

# Control accessories with Home on iPhone

Use the Home app and Control Center to control accessories in your home.

## Control accessories in the Home app

On the Home tab, tap an accessory’s icon on the left side of the tile—a light, for example—to quickly turn the accessory on or off. Tap the accessory’s name on the right side of the tile to show the accessory’s control.

The available controls depend on the type of accessory. For example, with some lightbulbs, there are controls for changing colors. With your smart TV, you can choose an input source.

## Add accessories to Control Center

You can add Home scenes and accessories to Control Center for easy access to your most used accessories.

1. [Open Control Center](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph7c412b4db/26/ios/26) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![The Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/0d07cb7185e2d87af8a9ad8b4ace6995.png) in the top left, then tap Add A Control at the bottom.

3. Tap the Home Scene or Accessory control.

4. Tap Choose Scene or Accessory, then select a home and device or scene you want to control.

metadata.title: Control accessories with Home on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Control accessories with Home on iPhone - Apple Support]

png) in the top left, then tap Add A Control at the bottom.

3. Tap the Home Scene or Accessory control.

4. Tap Choose Scene or Accessory, then select a home and device or scene you want to control.

## View categories and home status

The Lights, Climate, Security, Speakers and TVs, and Water categories let you quickly access all the relevant accessories organized by room.

1. Go to the Home app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/06707ddf9262fd49e182d8fbea6c3f32.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap the Home tab.

Below your home’s name, buttons show the status of accessories belonging to a category—for example, a Lights category that shows “3 on.”

3. Tap one of the category buttons to show all accessories within that category, organized by room.

4. While viewing a category, tap the icon on the left side of an accessory tile to perform an action—turn a light or group of lights on or off, start playing music on a HomePod, or lock the front door.

## View Activity History

When you have a home hub using the [new version of Apple Home](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/upgrade-to-the-new-version-of-apple-home-iphaeb0ce2d3/26/ios/26) and latest software, all the members of your home (except guests) can view the activity history for accessories like garage doors, locks, security systems, and [adaptive temperature](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/adaptive-temperature-clean-energy-guidance-iphdf6fde3d8/26/ios/26#iph8828aa73f) thermostats. For example, you can learn when a door was unlocked and who unlocked it.

1. Go to the Home app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/06707ddf9262fd49e182d8fbea6c3f32.png) on your iPhone.

2. On the Home tab, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2de35ce63d633769b9bfeb184c874452.png), then tap Home Settings.

3. Go to Activity, then tap One Month to view up to 30 days of activity.

4. In the Home tab, tap the Security or Climate category, then tap Home Activity to show the Activity History.

metadata.title: Control accessories with Home on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Control accessories with Home on iPhone - Apple Support]

ome Settings.

3. Go to Activity, then tap One Month to view up to 30 days of activity.

4. In the Home tab, tap the Security or Climate category, then tap Home Activity to show the Activity History.

## Turn Activity History on and off

Only the home owner or a shared home user with permission to add and edit accessories can turn Activity History on and off. When you turn off Activity History, all previous activity for the home is permanently deleted.

1. Go to the Home app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/06707ddf9262fd49e182d8fbea6c3f32.png) on your iPhone.

2. On the Home tab, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2de35ce63d633769b9bfeb184c874452.png), tap Home Settings, then tap Activity.

3. Choose One Month to turn Activity History on, or choose Off.

## Turn on Adaptive Lighting

Some Apple Home-compatible lights let you adjust their color temperature, from cool blue to warm yellow. You can set supported lights to automatically adjust the color temperature throughout the day. Wake up to warm colors, stay alert and focused midday with cooler ones, and wind down at night by removing blue light. For a light that supports Adaptive Lighting, follow these steps:

1. Go to the Home app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/06707ddf9262fd49e182d8fbea6c3f32.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap the light’s tile to show the controls.

You can also touch and hold the tile, and then choose Accessory Settings.

3. Tap ![the Adaptive Lighting button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/0de000aca1fb89d0163de51e703b0455.png), then turn on Adaptive Lighting.

_Note:_ Adaptive Lighting requires a home hub—a device such as Apple TV 4K or HomePod speakers.

metadata.title: Control accessories with Home on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Control accessories with Home on iPhone - Apple Support]

/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/0de000aca1fb89d0163de51e703b0455.png), then turn on Adaptive Lighting.

_Note:_ Adaptive Lighting requires a home hub—a device such as Apple TV 4K or HomePod speakers.

## Edit home accessories

To edit accessory settings, tap the right side of an accessory tile, tap ![the Settings button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a3f742160163c000f2710f952646cafe.png), then do any of the following:

- _Rename an accessory:_ Tap ![the Delete button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/14ad7e04408a158fcb2ba32511cfe8cf.png) to delete the old name, then type a new one.

- _Change an accessory’s icon:_ Tap the icon next to the accessory’s name, then select a new icon. If you don’t get a choice of other icons, it means the icon can’t be changed for this accessory.

## Group accessories

You can control multiple accessories with the tap of a button by grouping them.

1. Go to the Home app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/06707ddf9262fd49e182d8fbea6c3f32.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap the right side of an accessory tile, tap ![the Settings button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a3f742160163c000f2710f952646cafe.png), then tap Group with Other Accessories.

3. Tap the accessory you want to group with this accessory—another light in the room, for example.

4. In the Group Name field, type a name for the group.

5. Tap Done.

Turn on Include in Favorites to include the group in Favorites on the Home tab.

**See also** [Set up accessories with Home on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-accessories-iph125110541/26/ios/26)

# Create a custom iPhone Lock Screen

![A customized iPhone Lock Screen.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/22e149f75fab72b818e2bb626c6c986a.png)

You can personalize your Lock Screen by choosing a wallpaper, showcasing a favorite photo, changing the font of the time, adding widgets, and more.

You can create more than one Lock Screen, [switch between Lock Screens](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/create-a-custom-lock-screen-iph4d0e6c351/26/ios/26#iph1420848e7), and even link a Lock Screen to a Focus. So, for example, you could set a specific Lock Screen for when you want to focus on work.

metadata.title: Create a custom iPhone Lock Screen - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Create a custom iPhone Lock Screen - Apple Support]

## Edit your Lock Screen or create a new one

1. Press the side button on your iPhone two times.

2. Touch and hold the Lock Screen, then tap ![the Add New LockScreen Wallpaper button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/443066cf6168833f76d9d7d3a2b80897.png) to create a new Lock Screen. (If you don’t see ![the Add NewLockScreen Wallpaper button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/443066cf6168833f76d9d7d3a2b80897.png), touch and hold your Lock Screen again, then enter your passcode.)

To make changes to an existing Lock Screen, swipe to the screen you want to change, then tap Customize.

3. If you’re creating a new Lock Screen, tap one of the wallpaper options. On iPhone 12 and later, you can try a spatial scene for a 3D effect and try options where the time dynamically adapts to your photo wallpaper and notifications.

_Note:_ If you don’t see a wallpaper option you want to use (for example, Weather or Astronomy), scroll down to see more options, then tap Get below the one you like.

![A custom Lock Screen in the process of being edited. Elements available for customization are selected—the date, the time, and a button for adding widgets.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/eeb2d3f4c27e092016c48fec4ea3c81f.png)
4. Here are some ways to customize your Lock Screen (options depend on the wallpaper you choose):

   - _Change the look of the clock:_ Drag the bottom right corner to increase the height, then tap the time to change the font, switch to a more transparent look, and more.

   - _Add a 3D effect:_ If you’ve added a photo that supports spatial effects, tap ![the Spatial Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/597ad7d8421c0d94834d7cc4bc8d96e0.png) (iPhone 12 and later).

   - _Add or change the widgets:_ Tap your existing widgets or tap Add Widgets.

   - _Swap the controls (like the flashlight):_ Tap ![the Remove button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6047ad296fd44fcc70419598cf3da8fa.png) on one of the controls, then tap ![the Add Quick Action button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9c254a4b6c38816051b5f7363d4e9933.png).

   - _Reposition your photo:_ Pinch open to zoom in, drag the image with two fingers to move it, then pinch closed

metadata.title: Create a custom iPhone Lock Screen - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Create a custom iPhone Lock Screen - Apple Support]

B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9c254a4b6c38816051b5f7363d4e9933.png).

   - _Reposition your photo:_ Pinch open to zoom in, drag the image with two fingers to move it, then pinch closed to zoom out.

   - _Create a multilayered effect:_ Tap ![the Depth Effect button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/37165e728561a6ba468860e3cd31706f.png). Or if you have a photo that supports layering, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2de35ce63d633769b9bfeb184c874452.png), then choose Depth Effect.

   - _Change the style:_ Swipe left or right to try different color filters.

   - _Create a motion effect with a Live Photo:_ If you select a Live Photo that can be translated into slow motion, tap ![the Live photo button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/642949b7068524ca7845b2b4f67f838e.png) to play the Live Photo when the device wakes up.

   - _Set the shuffle frequency:_ If you chose Photo Shuffle, tap the photo thumbnail to change the photo selection or change the shuffle frequency by tapping ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2de35ce63d633769b9bfeb184c874452.png).

   - _Switch between a light and dark appearance_ If the background you chose has a light mode and dark mode option, tap ![the appearance button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/972cfb7f1a29974cc263753eacea2967.png).
5. Tap Add or Done, then choose to Set as Wallpaper Pair or Customize Home Screen.

metadata.title: Create a custom iPhone Lock Screen - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Create a custom iPhone Lock Screen - Apple Support]

one/create-a-custom-lock-screen-iph4d0e6c351/26/ios/26#iph1420848e7), and even link a Lock Screen to a Focus. So, for example, you could set a specific Lock Screen for when you want to focus on work.

## Link a Focus to your Lock Screen

Focus helps you concentrate on a task by minimizing distractions. After you [set up a Focus](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-a-focus-iphd6288a67f/26/ios/26), you can link it to your Lock Screen — so your Focus settings apply whenever you use that particular Lock Screen and that Lock Screen appears whenever that Focus is on.

1. Press the side button on your iPhone.

2. Touch and hold the Lock Screen until the Customize button appears at the bottom of the screen.

3. Tap Focus near the bottom of the wallpaper to see the Focus options—for example, Do Not Disturb, Personal, Sleep, and Work.

4. Select a Focus, then tap ![the Close button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8632cceede5d336ff7e8e596c149eec1.png).

## Switch between Lock Screens

You can create multiple custom Lock Screens and switch between them throughout the day.

1. Press the side button on your iPhone.

2. Touch and hold the Lock Screen until the Customize button appears at the bottom of the screen.

3. Swipe to the Lock Screen you want to use, then tap it.

_Note:_ If a Lock Screen links to a specific Focus, switching from that Lock Screen to another also switches your Focus.

## Delete a Lock Screen

You can delete Lock Screens you no longer need.

1. Press the side button on your iPhone.

2. Touch and hold the Lock Screen until the Customize button appears at the bottom of the screen.

3. Swipe to go to the Lock Screen you want to delete, swipe up on the screen, tap ![the Trash button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f1b1c9dd52373c0e242a17c216772bfd.png), then tap Delete This Wallpaper.

**See also** [Keep the iPhone display on longer](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/keep-the-iphone-display-on-longer-iph7117338a8/26/ios/26) [Change the wallpaper on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-the-wallpaper-iph3d267104/26/ios/26)

# Create a password for a website or app on iPhone

Your iPhone can generate a password to use on a website or app.

1. Go to the Passwords app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/10c7d2493aff94aeeba904e260e6c1ec.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap All, then tap ![the Toolbar New Password button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/0d07cb7185e2d87af8a9ad8b4ace6995.png) in the lower-right corner.

3. Enter details about the website or app, and a user name for your account.

4. Tap password.

A strong password is suggested for you.

5. Tap the suggested password (or enter one).

6. Tap ![the Save button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

You can also create a password when you sign up for services on websites and in apps. See [Create a strong password for a new account](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/automatically-fill-in-strong-passwords-iphf9219d8c9/26/ios/26#iph04c1f176b).

**See also** [Change the password for a website or app on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-the-password-for-a-website-or-app-iph9e39791bd/26/ios/26)

# Create and edit events in Calendar on iPhone

Use the Calendar app to create and edit events, appointments, and meetings.

![A calendar in Day view showing the day’s events. The Calendars button is at the bottom center of the screen, and the Inbox button is at the bottom right.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/343265f87c471a5f59200a3d76b75d74.png)

**Siri:** [Ask Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/summon_siri/26/ios/26) something like, “Set up a meeting with Gordon at 9.” [Learn how to use Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-on-and-activate-siri-iph83aad8922/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Create and edit events in Calendar on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Create and edit events in Calendar on iPhone - Apple Support]

one/aside/summon_siri/26/ios/26) something like, “Set up a meeting with Gordon at 9.” [Learn how to use Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-on-and-activate-siri-iph83aad8922/26/ios/26).

## Add an event

1. Go to the Calendar app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1b944caed2601c11a4cfa936cfd46342.png) on your iPhone.

2. In Day view, tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/0d07cb7185e2d87af8a9ad8b4ace6995.png) at the top of the screen.

3. Enter the title of the event.

4. Tap Location or Video Call, then enter a physical location or tap FaceTime to enter a video link for a remote event.

You can also copy a FaceTime link you created or received and paste it in the Location field. See [Create a link to a FaceTime call](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/create-a-facetime-link-iphfc9b58aaa/26/ios/26).

5. Enter the start and end times for the event, the travel time, invitees, attachments, and so on. (Swipe up, if necessary, to enter all the meeting information.)

6. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ba2d818fa1343c4620a862e45c5ba4c2.png).

## Add an alert

You can set an alert to be reminded of an event beforehand.

1. Go to the Calendar app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1b944caed2601c11a4cfa936cfd46342.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap the event, then tap Edit.

3. In the event details, tap Alert.

4. Choose when you want to be reminded.

For example, “At time of event,” “5 minutes before,” or another choice.

_Note:_ If you add the address of the event’s location, Calendar uses Apple Maps to look up locations, traffic conditions, and transit options to tell you when it’s time to leave.

5. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/49fec30705abe44e61a4a36f9658a0a2.png).

metadata.title: Create and edit events in Calendar on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Create and edit events in Calendar on iPhone - Apple Support]

it options to tell you when it’s time to leave.

5. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/49fec30705abe44e61a4a36f9658a0a2.png).

## Add an attachment

You can add an attachment to a calendar event to share with invitees.

1. Go to the Calendar app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1b944caed2601c11a4cfa936cfd46342.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap the event, then tap Edit near the top right.

3. In the event details, tap Add attachment.

4. Locate the file you want to attach.

To find the file, you can enter its name in the search field, scroll, tap folders to open them, tap Browse to look in other locations (such as iCloud Drive), and so on. See [Modify files, folders, and find downloads in Files](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/modify-files-and-folders-iphc61044c11/26/ios/26).

5. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ba2d818fa1343c4620a862e45c5ba4c2.png).

To remove the attachment, tap the event, tap Edit near the top right, swipe left over the attachment, then tap Remove.

## Find events in other apps

Siri can suggest events found in Mail, Messages, and Safari—such as flight reservations and hotel bookings—so you can add them easily in Calendar.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Apps, then tap Calendar.

3. Tap Siri, then turn on Show in App to allow Siri to suggest events found in other apps.

To allow Siri to make suggestions in other apps based on how you use Calendar, turn on Learn from this App.

metadata.title: Create and edit events in Calendar on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Create and edit events in Calendar on iPhone - Apple Support]

3. Tap Siri, then turn on Show in App to allow Siri to suggest events found in other apps.

To allow Siri to make suggestions in other apps based on how you use Calendar, turn on Learn from this App.

## Copy and paste an event

You can copy an event and paste it to another date.

1. Go to the Calendar app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1b944caed2601c11a4cfa936cfd46342.png) on your iPhone.

2. Touch and hold the event, then tap Copy.

3. On another date, touch and hold the time where you want to paste the event.

When you release, the New Event screen appears and the copied event appears below Title with the date and time where you want to paste the event.

**Tip:** You can also tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/819a0a2fa98b0fc0bbc880ebcbb15b7d.png) at the top of the screen after copying an event.

4. Tap the copied event below Title.

5. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ba2d818fa1343c4620a862e45c5ba4c2.png).

## Edit an event

You can change the time of an event and any of the other event details.

1. Go to the Calendar app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1b944caed2601c11a4cfa936cfd46342.png) on your iPhone.

2. Do any of the following:

   - _Quickly change the event time or duration:_ In Day or Week view, touch and hold the event, then drag it to a new time, or adjust the grab points.

   - _Change event details:_ Tap the event, then tap Edit near the top right. In the event details, tap a setting to change it, or tap in a field to type new information.

## Delete an event

1. Go to the Calendar app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1b944caed2601c11a4cfa936cfd46342.png) on your iPhone.

2. In Day view, tap the event, then tap Delete Event at the bottom of the screen.

**See also** [Send invitations in Calendar on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/send-invitations-iph82c5721ca/26/ios/26)
