# Dictate text on iPhone

With Dictation on iPhone, you can dictate text anywhere you can type it. You can also use typing and Dictation together—the keyboard stays open during Dictation so you can easily switch between voice and touch to enter text.

Dictation requests are processed on your device in many languages—no internet connection is required. When dictating in a search box, dictated text may be sent to the search provider in order to process the search.

_Note:_ Dictation may not be available in all languages or in all countries or regions, and features may vary.

When using Dictation, cellular data charges may apply. See [View or change cellular data settings](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-or-change-cellular-data-settings-iph3dd5f213/18.0/ios/18.0).

## Turn on Dictation

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> General > Keyboard.

2. Turn on Enable Dictation. If a prompt appears, tap Enable Dictation.

To learn more about how Apple protects your information and lets you choose what you share, tap About Dictation & Privacy below Dictation, or see the [Apple Privacy website](https://www.apple.com/privacy/).

metadata.title: Dictate text on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Dictate text on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Dictate text

![The onscreen keyboard is open in the Mail app. The Dictation button in the bottom-right corner of the screen is selected and the Dictation button appears below the insertion point in the text field.](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/6f9556e5615d5dae671068c6ae706c20.png)

1. Tap in the text field to place the insertion point where you want to begin dictating text.

2. Tap ![the Dictate button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/321479a268e747d8de5fb2a7db064639.png) on the onscreen keyboard (or in any text field where it appears).

3. When ![the Dictate button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/321479a268e747d8de5fb2a7db064639.png) appears near the insertion point in the text field, begin to dictate your text.

4. To insert an emoji or a punctuation mark, or perform simple formatting tasks, do any of the following:

   - _Add emoji_: Say the name of an emoji, such as “heart emoji” or “smiley face emoji.”

   - _Add punctuation_: Say the name of the punctuation mark, such as “exclamation point.”

     _Note:_ In supported languages, Dictation automatically inserts commas, periods, and question marks as you dictate. To turn off automatic punctuation, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> General > Keyboard, then turn off Auto-Punctuation.

   - _Change formatting_: Say a formatting command such as “new line” or “new paragraph.”

   - _Make text changes_: Say a command such as “select…” or “delete…” to make changes to text.

   - _Undo or repeat a command_: Say “undo” or “redo” if you change your mind or want to repeat a command.
5. When you’re done, tap ![the Dictate button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/321479a268e747d8de5fb2a7db064639.png) or say “Stop dictation”; Dictation stops automatically when you stop speaking for 30 seconds.

For a full list of the commands you can use while dictating, see [Commands for dictating text](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/commands-for-dictating-text-iph3bf19d7b9/18.0/ios/18.0).

When you use a keyboard for another language, you can use Dictation in that language as well. See [Add or change keyboards](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-or-change-keyboards-iph73b71eb/18.0/ios/

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> General > Keyboard.

2. Turn off Enable Dictation.

**See also** [Commands for dictating text on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/commands-for-dictating-text-iph3bf19d7b9/18.0/ios/18.0) [Use the onscreen keyboard with VoiceOver on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-the-onscreen-keyboard-iph3e2e3d1d/18.0/ios/18.0) [Dictate text while using Magic Keyboard with iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/dictate-text-iph24206370d/18.0/ios/18.0)

# Edit and organize a list in Reminders on iPhone

In the Reminders app, you can organize a list with sections and subtasks. You can also view the sections as columns and easily move items to different columns.

_Note:_ All Reminders features described in this guide are available when you use [updated reminders in iCloud](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-or-remove-accounts-iph8739025dd/26/ios/26#iph8bbc4e260). Some features aren’t available when using other accounts.

![The Reminders app showing a list with one section expanded and the others collapsed.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/bc42ae5d566d429d52e14ec3e3f57eab.png)

## Edit a list

1. Go to the Reminders app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ec6119fc5bf93b83f78120434ec8118d.png) on your iPhone.

2. While viewing a list, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/aad05ec99d91fb0497859fc9c0c181fb.png), then do any of the following:

   - _Change the list name, type, color, or icon:_ Tap Show List Info.

   - _Edit multiple items:_ Tap Select Reminders or Select Items, then select the items you want to edit. You can also drag two fingers over the items. Use the buttons at the bottom of the screen to add a date and time, move, delete, assign, complete, tag, or flag the selected items.

   - _Sort items by due date, creation date, priority, or title:_ (not available in all Smart Lists) Tap Sort By, then choose an option.

     When you sort or reorder a list, the new order is applied to the list on your other devices where you’re using [updated reminders](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-or-remove-accounts-iph8739025dd/26/ios/26#iph8bbc4e260). If you sort or reorder a shared list, other participants also see the new order (if they use updated reminders).

   - _Remove the list:_ Tap Delete List.

metadata.title: Edit and organize a list in Reminders on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Edit and organize a list in Reminders on iPhone - Apple Support]

counts-iph8739025dd/26/ios/26#iph8bbc4e260). If you sort or reorder a shared list, other participants also see the new order (if they use updated reminders).

   - _Remove the list:_ Tap Delete List.

## Manually reorder or recategorize items in a list

1. Go to the Reminders app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ec6119fc5bf93b83f78120434ec8118d.png) on your iPhone.

2. While viewing a list, touch and hold an item you want to move, then drag it to a new location.

## Organize with sections

1. Go to the Reminders app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ec6119fc5bf93b83f78120434ec8118d.png) on your iPhone.

2. While viewing a list, do any of the following:

   - _Create a new section:_ Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/aad05ec99d91fb0497859fc9c0c181fb.png), tap New Section, then enter a name.

   - _Collapse a section:_ Tap ![the Collapse button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/d9ef375b37f3e012ba5381d26fd9d6a8.png).

   - _Expand a section:_ Tap ![the Expand button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/465382cdc77d9a7ae9a6a530f3a2e55d.png).

   - _Rename section:_ Tap the section name, then enter a new name.

   - _Move a section:_ Touch and hold the section, then drag it to a new position.

     _Note:_ You can’t move the Others section.

   - _Remove a section:_ Swipe left on the section, then tap Delete.

     _Note:_ Removing a section will also delete the section’s reminders.

If Apple Intelligence\* is turned on, you can automatically categorize related reminders into sections within a list. See [Use Apple Intelligence in Reminders](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-apple-intelligence-in-reminders-iphcb580b580/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Edit and organize a list in Reminders on iPhone - Apple Support

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Edit and organize a list in Reminders on iPhone - Apple Support]

categorize related reminders into sections within a list. See [Use Apple Intelligence in Reminders](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-apple-intelligence-in-reminders-iphcb580b580/26/ios/26).

## View the sections as columns

You can view the list in columns, making it easy to visualize and recategorize the items.

![The Reminders app showing a camping trip list with the categories organized as columns.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/700ca78a5e565cef6111de3260942ea9.png)

1. Go to the Reminders app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ec6119fc5bf93b83f78120434ec8118d.png) on your iPhone.

2. While viewing a list, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/aad05ec99d91fb0497859fc9c0c181fb.png), then tap View as Columns.

3. To recategorize an item, drag it into a different column.

## Create subtasks

1. Go to the Reminders app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ec6119fc5bf93b83f78120434ec8118d.png) on your iPhone.

2. While viewing a list, swipe right on an item, then tap Indent. Or drag an item onto another item.

If you complete a main task, the subtasks are also completed. If you delete or move a main task, the subtasks are also deleted or moved.

**See also** [Organize multiple lists in Reminders on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/organize-multiple-lists-iph2c6cf708e/26/ios/26) [Tag items in Reminders on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/tag-items-iph474a6685c/26/ios/26)

# Edit photos and videos on iPhone

After you take a photo or video, use the tools in the Photos app to edit it on your iPhone. You can adjust the light and color, add a filter, and more.

When you use [iCloud Photos](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/sync-photos-videos-icloud-iph961b96c4d/26/ios/26), any edits you make to your photos and videos are saved across all your devices.

metadata.title: Edit photos and videos on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Edit photos and videos on iPhone - Apple Support]

When you use [iCloud Photos](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/sync-photos-videos-icloud-iph961b96c4d/26/ios/26), any edits you make to your photos and videos are saved across all your devices.

## Edit the light or color of a photo or video

Adjust light and color settings to make your photos and videos look their best.

![The Edit screen with a photo in the center. At the bottom of the screen, from left to right, are the Styles, Portrait, Adjust, Crop, and Clean Up buttons. The Adjust button is selected and there is a slider to adjust the Brilliance levels of the photo. The Cancel button is in the top-left corner of the screen and the Done button is in the top-right corner.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9bf2e9c387e20abd1b3a28e32109e982.png)

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap a photo or video to open it, then tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png).

3. Tap Adjust.

4. Swipe left under the photo to view the settings you can adjust—for example, Exposure, Brilliance, or Highlights.

5. Tap a setting, then drag the slider to make precise adjustments.

The outline around each button indicates the level of change for that setting. Tap the button to compare the edited version to the original.

6. Tap Done to save your edits, or if you don’t like your changes, tap Cancel, then tap Discard Changes.

**Tip:** Tap ![the Enhance button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6fe75b68a1240ae2275fc5be70f65568.png) to automatically enhance the light and color.

metadata.title: Edit photos and videos on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Edit photos and videos on iPhone - Apple Support]

:** Tap ![the Enhance button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6fe75b68a1240ae2275fc5be70f65568.png) to automatically enhance the light and color.

## Edit the Photographic Style of a photo

On [models that support Photographic Styles](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph80f8f71ff/26/ios/26), you can edit the Photographic Style or apply a new style to photos in your library.

_Note:_ To edit Photographic Styles in the Photos app, Camera needs to capture photos with High Efficiency image capture. To turn on High Efficiency, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Camera > Formats, then tap High Efficiency.

![The Edit screen with a photo in the center. At the bottom of the screen, from left to right, are the Styles, Adjust, Crop, and Clean Up buttons. The Styles button is selected. Below the photo are the different style options; Dramatic is selected. The Cancel button is in the top-left corner of the screen and the Done button is in the top-right corner.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4b11bc5a851230dbcbe7ce233641c778.png)

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a photo taken with a Photographic Style, then tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png).

3. Tap Styles, then do any of the following:

   - _Choose a new style:_ Swipe left or right on the style choices below the image.

   - _Adjust the tone, color, and intensity:_ Tap ![the Styles Chooser](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/55392a1c35be33a75747e4568b0c831b.png), then drag your finger on the square to simultaneously adjust the tone and color. Drag your finger left or right on the slider below to adjust the intensity level. When you’re done making adjustments, tap the image.

     _Note:_ As you make adjustments, the values for tone, color, and intensity are shown at the top of the screen. Tap ![the Reset button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b286b5723904022212d1b07955ddfbc1.png) to reset these values.
4. Tap Done.

metadata.title: Edit photos and videos on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Edit photos and videos on iPhone - Apple Support]

of the screen. Tap ![the Reset button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b286b5723904022212d1b07955ddfbc1.png) to reset these values.
4. Tap Done.

## Apply filter effects to a photo or video

Apply filter effects to change the tint of a photo or video.

_Note:_ On iPhone 16 models or later, [use Photographic Styles](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/edit-photos-and-videos-iphb08064d57/26/ios/26#iphc0cdae33a) instead of filters to give your photos a customized look. Filter effects are available on these models if you haven’t turned on Photographic Styles.

![The Edit screen with a photo in the center. At the bottom of the screen, from left to right, are the Adjust, Filters, Crop, and Clean Up buttons. The Filters button is selected. Below the photo are the different filter options. Dramatic Warm is selected and there is a slider to adjust the intensity of the filter. The Cancel button is in the top-left corner of the screen and the Done button is in the top-right corner.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7522d72fcb8fff6551ce1815e76eff52.png)

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a photo or video, then tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png).

3. Tap ![the Filters button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8abec477c96730d2a869fc2928282088.png) to apply filter effects such as Vivid or Dramatic.

To remove a filter that was applied when you took the photo, tap Original.

4. Tap a filter, then drag the slider to adjust the effect.

To compare the edited photo to the original, tap the photo.

5. Tap Done to save your edits, or if you don’t like your changes, tap Cancel, then tap Discard Changes.

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Edit photos and videos on iPhone - Apple Support]

the slider to adjust the effect.

To compare the edited photo to the original, tap the photo.

5. Tap Done to save your edits, or if you don’t like your changes, tap Cancel, then tap Discard Changes.

## Copy and paste edits to multiple photos

You can copy the edits you made to one photo (or video) and paste them onto another photo, or a batch or photos, all at the same time.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open the photo or video that contains the edits you want to copy.

3. Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/53cc5dafac91abd63db83c91b43ef920.png), then tap Copy Edits.

4. Review and turn off any edits you don’t want to copy, then tap Copy.

5. Tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ea56b8edd3ca92d7af8adc2abea59bd5.png) to return to your library.

6. Tap Select, then tap the thumbnails of the photos you want to paste the edits onto, or open a single photo or video.

7. Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/df71404327453fcdf3541069ec35064a.png), then tap Paste Edits.

_Note:_ Photos automatically adjusts the white balance and exposure of the edited photos to create a better match and make the photos look even more similar.

## Add stickers, text, and more

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap a photo to open it.

3. Tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png), then tap ![the Markup button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a8c80f885f6d9785113cd5141123e96c.png).

4. Tap ![the Add Annotations button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e174be8448abb41f13837019b102e688.png) to add stickers, captions, text, shapes, or even your signature.

5. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png) to save your edits.

metadata.title: Edit photos and videos on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Edit photos and videos on iPhone - Apple Support]

pes, or even your signature.

5. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png) to save your edits.

## Set the background color of Edit mode

By default, the background of Edit mode matches the light or dark appearance of your iPhone. You can change the background color of Edit mode—for example, set it to always appear dark if you prefer to edit photos against a dark background.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap a photo or video to open it, then tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png).

3. Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2de35ce63d633769b9bfeb184c874452.png), tap Appearance, then do one of the following:

   - _Match Edit mode to the light or dark appearance of your iPhone:_ Tap System.

   - _Make Edit mode always appear dark:_ Tap Dark.

   - _Make Edit mode always appear light:_ Tap Light.

## Remove distractions using Apple Intelligence

With Apple Intelligence,\* you can use the Clean Up tool to help identify and remove distracting objects in a photo. See [Remove distractions from your photos](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-apple-intelligence-in-photos-iphf7de217f0/26/ios/26#iph4e1fc17b5).

**See also** [Crop, rotate, flip, or straighten photos and videos on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/crop-rotate-flip-straighten-photos-videos-iph0f3ebb1dd/26/ios/26) [Undo and revert photo edits on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/undo-and-revert-photo-edits-iph2413db0ab/26/ios/26) [Use Markup to add text, shapes, signatures, and more to documents on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-text-shapes-stickers-and-more-iphcdb1d0bc7/26/ios/26) [Apple Support article: How to edit with third-party apps and extensions in Photos](https://support.apple.com/102259)

# Enter formulas and equations in Notes on iPhone

With math in the Notes app, you can draw diagrams, solve equations, assign variables, and plot graphs. Mathematical expressions are solved inline when you write or type an equal sign.

_Note:_ The Notes app supports math written using Western Arabic numerals and common mathematical symbols, or typed using Arabic (Eastern & Western) and Devanagari numerals and common mathematical symbols.

![A note for planning a trip, with a calculation of the travel expenses and a photo.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8dd2dd6adeface8e6c9a81c6d8c5af72.png)

## Add mathematical expressions to a note

1. Go to the Notes app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/454094731616c01a657acf1f45a61c55.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a note, or tap ![the New Note button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f1d4a1bdfa3adb69b6fda92686ed7424.png) to start a new note.

3. Do any of the following:

   - Type a math expression, then include an equal sign.

   - Tap ![the Markup button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a4a0abe7e5321eb074fb81c9838bb2ee.png), write a math expression horizontally, then include an equal sign.

   - Tap ![the Markup button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a4a0abe7e5321eb074fb81c9838bb2ee.png), write a stack of numbers, then draw a horizontal line below the numbers to add the numbers in the stack. Write a different symbol to the left of each digit if you want a different operation.
4. Tap the equal sign to see what Notes understood in what you wrote, then tap Solve. To correct mistakes, you can fix any of the following:

   - _Unrecognized math and unsolvable expressions:_ Tap any dotted red lines to see details about the error.

   - _Ambiguous characters:_ Tap any dotted blue lines to clarify what you wrote.

metadata.title: Enter formulas and equations in Notes on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Enter formulas and equations in Notes on iPhone - Apple Support]

wing:

   - _Unrecognized math and unsolvable expressions:_ Tap any dotted red lines to see details about the error.

   - _Ambiguous characters:_ Tap any dotted blue lines to clarify what you wrote.

## Create a graph

1. Go to the Notes app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/454094731616c01a657acf1f45a61c55.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a note, or tap ![the New Note button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f1d4a1bdfa3adb69b6fda92686ed7424.png) to start a new note.

3. Type or write an equation with x and y variables, with the y variable on the left side of the equation. For example, y = 5x + 3.

You can also tap the equal sign in an equation you already wrote.

4. When you finish the equation, a menu appears. Tap Insert Graph.

The y variable is represented on the vertical axis, and the x variable is represented on the horizontal axis.

To add another equation to the same graph, type or write a new equation, then tap Add to Existing Graph.

See also [Work with graphs in Math Notes in Calculator on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/work-with-graphs-in-math-notes-iph09ad8b507/26/ios/26).

## Access Math Notes created in the Calculator app

1. Go to the Notes app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/454094731616c01a657acf1f45a61c55.png) on your iPhone.

2. In the Folders list, tap Math Notes. You may have to tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ea56b8edd3ca92d7af8adc2abea59bd5.png) to see your folders.

See [Solve math with Math Notes in Calculator](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/solve-math-with-math-notes-iph46efa613a/26/ios/26).

**See also** [Add drawings and handwriting in Notes on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-drawings-and-handwriting-iphd939f0a1c/26/ios/26) [Add photos, video, and more to notes on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-photos-video-and-more-iph23f4d9aa9/26/ios/26)

# Export or print notes on iPhone

In the Notes app, you can open a note in the Pages app and continue working. You can also export the note as a PDF or print the note.

## Open a note in Pages

1. Make sure your iPhone has the latest version of [Pages](https://apps.apple.com/app/pages/id409201541).

2. Go to the Notes app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/454094731616c01a657acf1f45a61c55.png) on your iPhone.

3. Open the note you want to export.

4. Tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f641f1d538e27d78e9d83a0d0359a02b.png), then tap Open in Pages.

_Note:_ If the note is locked, you must unlock it before exporting the contents of the note to Pages. You can’t edit drawings in Pages. Content such as scanned documents and PDFs appear as thumbnail images in Pages. Tags, mentions, checklists, and links to notes aren’t active items in Pages. If a note is shared, the resulting Pages document isn’t shared.

## Export a note as a PDF

You can create a PDF of a note, using the note’s current view settings for its attachments. In a note with a multipage PDF or scanned document, the exported PDF contains only the first page of the original PDF or scanned document.

1. Go to the Notes app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/454094731616c01a657acf1f45a61c55.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open the note you want to export.

3. Tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f641f1d538e27d78e9d83a0d0359a02b.png), then tap Markup.

4. Mark up the pages in the exported PDF as needed, then tap Done to save the PDF.

4. Mark up the pages in the exported PDF as needed, then tap Done to save the PDF.

## Export a note as a Markdown file

You can export your notes as Markdown files to integrate your notes into writing, content, and other workflows that support Markdown.

1. Go to the Notes app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/454094731616c01a657acf1f45a61c55.png) on your iPhone.

## Print a note

1. Go to the Notes app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/454094731616c01a657acf1f45a61c55.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open the note you want to print.

3. Tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f641f1d538e27d78e9d83a0d0359a02b.png), then tap Print.

4. Choose the printer and other options, then tap Print.

**See also** [Print from iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/printers-iph92628b8f/26/ios/26) [Share notes and collaborate on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/share-and-collaborate-iphe4d04f674/26/ios/26) [Pages User Guide for iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/pages-iphone/welcome/ios)

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Connect external storage devices to iPhone - Apple Support]

# Connect external storage devices to iPhone

You can use the Files app and other supported apps to access files stored on external devices, such as USB drives and SD cards, connected to your iPhone.

## Connect a USB drive or an SD card reader

1. Attach the USB drive or SD card reader to the charging port on your iPhone using a compatible connector or adapter.

You may need the Lightning to USB Camera Adapter, Lightning to USB 3 Camera Adapter, USB-C to SD Card Camera Reader, or Lightning to SD Card Camera Reader (all sold separately).

_Note:_ An external storage device must have only a single data partition, and it must be formatted as APFS, APFS (encrypted), macOS Extended (HFS+), exFAT (FAT64), FAT32, or FAT. To change the formatting of a storage device, use [the Files app](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/transfer-files-iphone-a-storage-device-server-iphe9aff429a/26/ios/26#iphbb7184396) or a Mac or PC.

2. Do any of the following:

   - _Insert an SD memory card into a card reader:_ Don’t force the card into the slot on the reader; it fits only one way.

     _Note:_ You can import photos and videos from the memory card directly to the Photos app. See [Import and export photos and videos on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/import-and-export-photos-and-videos-iph480caa1f3/26/ios/26).

   - _View the contents of the drive or memory card:_ In a supported app (for example, Files), tap Browse at the bottom of the screen, then tap the name of the device below Locations. If you don’t see Locations, tap Browse again at the bottom of the screen.

   - _Disconnect the drive or card reader:_ Remove it from the charging port on iPhone.

_Note:_ External hard drives often require an external power source when used with iPhone. If an external hard drive doesn’t supply its own power, and your iPhone has a USB-C connector, you can use a powered USB hub. If your iPhone has a Lightning connector, you can use the Lightning to USB 3 Camera Adapter connected to a USB power adapter.

**See also** [Modify files and folders in Files on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/modify-files-and-folders-iphc61044c11/26/ios/26)

# Find and share your Wi-Fi password on iPhone

You can find the password of the Wi-Fi network you’re currently using and see all the Wi-Fi networks and passwords you’ve previously saved. You can share the password of the Wi-Fi network you’re currently using with a nearby friend or family member.

## Find a Wi-Fi password

1. Go to the Passwords app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/10c7d2493aff94aeeba904e260e6c1ec.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Wi-Fi.

If you’re connected to a Wi-Fi network, its name appears at the top of the screen. You can also see all the Wi-Fi networks you’ve previously saved.

3. Tap a Wi-Fi network.

4. Tap the Password field.

To copy the password to use elsewhere, tap Copy Password.

## Share your Wi-Fi network password with a nearby friend or family member

1. Make sure your iPhone (the one sharing the password) is unlocked and connected to the Wi-Fi network.

2. Have your friend of family member go to Wi-Fi settings on their iPhone, iPad, or Mac, then choose the network.

3. When a message asks if you want to share your Wi-Fi password, tap Share Password.

_Note:_ To share your Wi-Fi network password devices must be within Bluetooth and Wi-Fi range, not using Personal Hotspot, and added to each other’s contacts.

You can also go to the Wi-Fi network and tap Show Network QR Code, which friends and family can scan to join the network.

**See also** [View your passwords and related information on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-passwords-and-related-information-iph3ee1dd6e7/26/ios/26)

# Find new music with Apple Music on iPhone

When you [subscribe to Apple Music](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/subscribe-to-apple-music-iphcfadf0701/26/ios/26), you can browse new and noteworthy music, music videos, playlists, and more.

![The New tab showing an updated playlist at the top. You can swipe left to see more featured music and videos. The Latest Songs section appears below. You can swipe up on the screen to explore new and recommended music.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/097a6c388cca356d7c6ecdb2d8052bd5.png)

metadata.title: Find new music with Apple Music on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Find new music with Apple Music on iPhone - Apple Support]

elow. You can swipe up on the screen to explore new and recommended music.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/097a6c388cca356d7c6ecdb2d8052bd5.png)

## Discover new releases, featured music, and more

1. Go to the Music app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a8584141e5732119d6e19d28e0fbd248.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap New to find new music, then do any of the following:

   - _Explore featured music:_ Swipe through featured songs and videos at the top of the screen.

   - _Browse playlists created by music experts:_ Tap one of the many playlists created by music experts.

   - _Explore new music:_ Tap a song below the Latest Songs heading or swipe through the albums listed below New Releases.

   - _See upcoming concerts:_ Go to any Artist page to see upcoming concerts. Scroll down to see details under All Upcoming Concerts. To view all upcoming concerts and update your location in the Concerts Hub, tap the Search tab, then tap Concerts.

     _Note:_ Concerts isn’t available in China.

   - _See what’s hot:_ Tap a song below the Trending Songs heading. Swipe left to see more songs.

   - _Listen to the top songs from around the world:_ Tap one of the Daily Top 100 playlists. These playlists reflect the day’s most popular songs in countries across the globe.

   - _Listen to songs from upcoming albums:_ Tap an album below Coming Soon, then tap an available song to play it.

   - _Watch music videos:_ Tap Music Videos, then tap a featured music video or playlist of music videos.

   - _Play the day’s most popular songs:_ Tap Charts, then tap a song, playlist, album, or music video to play it. Tap All Genres at the top of the screen to see the top songs and music videos in a genre you choose.

   - _Browse your favorite genres:_ Tap Browse by Genre, choose a genre, then tap a featured playlist, song, album, artist, or radio station to enjoy music handpicked by music experts.

   - _Play music that matches your mood:_ Tap Moods and Activities, then tap a playlist such as Feel Good, Romance, or Party.

_Note:_ To see more music, swipe left on the album art. To see all the music in a section—New Releases, for example—tap ![the Forward button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/465382cdc77d9a7ae9a6a530f3a2e55d.png) next to the section’s name.

metadata.title: Find new music with Apple Music on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Find new music with Apple Music on iPhone - Apple Support]

Releases, for example—tap ![the Forward button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/465382cdc77d9a7ae9a6a530f3a2e55d.png) next to the section’s name.

## Discover your most-played music of the year

For every year you’ve subscribed to Apple Music (and played enough music to become eligible), Apple Music creates a Replay personal chart, ranking the songs you’ve played the most.

1. Go to the Music app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a8584141e5732119d6e19d28e0fbd248.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Home, swipe down to Replay: Your Top Music, then tap an option (for example, a year).

You can also see all your insights throughout the year on your iPhone or at [replay.music.apple.com](https://replay.music.apple.com/). See the Apple Support article [How to get your Apple Music Replay](https://support.apple.com/109356).

## Discover music you’ve never played before

Using your listening history and taste profile, Apple Music recommends music you may like that you’ve never played before.

**Siri:** [Ask Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/summon_siri/26/ios/26) something like, “Play my Discovery Station.” [Learn how to use Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-on-and-activate-siri-iph83aad8922/26/ios/26).

1. Go to the Music app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a8584141e5732119d6e19d28e0fbd248.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Home, then swipe down to Stations for You.

3. Tap Discovery Station.

## Turn on or off new music notifications

1. Go to the Music app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a8584141e5732119d6e19d28e0fbd248.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the My Account button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/75c119c299421ca48b9edf9b28eebda5.png) or your profile picture.

3. Tap Notifications, then turn on (or off) New Music.

**See also** [Mark items as favorites in Apple Music on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/mark-items-as-favorites-iphbb0f5ff34/26/ios/26) [Play music on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/play-music-iph0138fb328/26/ios/26)

# Get started with Reminders on iPhone

The Reminders app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ec6119fc5bf93b83f78120434ec8118d.png) helps you remember anything you need to track. You can set reminders, make grocery lists, create checklists for projects, and organize tasks in to-do lists.

![The Reminders app, showing a new task being added to the Reminders list.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/0eeb776c501dbf8e6ae3dca59448aee0.png)

## Set a reminder

Simply type, dictate, or ask Siri when you need to remember something.

![The Reminders app, showing a grocery list with items in categories such as Produce and Seafood.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6c7cafc2cd5938942ec4dcfa22bfa24f.png)

## Create a grocery list

Make your shopping easier with a grocery list that automatically sorts items into categories. To convert a regular list into a grocery list, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/aad05ec99d91fb0497859fc9c0c181fb.png), tap Show List Info, tap List Type, then choose Groceries.

![The Reminders app, showing a grocery list with options for sharing at the bottom.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ee52fbbc2a9473564bcc9c6cbd5ae767.png)

## Collaborate on a list

To invite people to work on a list, tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/748a151e2276ad5df4d03ec8d4506bf7.png), then choose a sharing option.

![The Reminders app, showing multiple lists and the Tag Browser at the bottom.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a679f4f2abfa1a97772ee7008ed99ebc.png)

e Reminders app, showing multiple lists and the Tag Browser at the bottom.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a679f4f2abfa1a97772ee7008ed99ebc.png)

## Organize your reminders

You can create new lists to suit your needs, categorize with tags, and manage your reminders with Smart Lists.

_Note:_ All Reminders features described in this guide are available when you use [updated reminders in iCloud](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-or-remove-accounts-iph8739025dd/26/ios/26#iph8bbc4e260). Some features aren’t available when using other accounts.

# Hand off tasks between your iPhone and your other devices

With Handoff, you can start a task on one device and complete it on another. For example, you can start an email on your iPhone but then finish it on your Mac computer’s bigger screen.

You can use Handoff with many Apple apps—for example, Safari, Mail, FaceTime, and Maps. Some third-party apps also work with Handoff.

You can use Handoff between your iPhone, iPad, and Mac. You can also hand off tasks between your Apple Watch and iPhone.

## Before you begin

Make sure of the following:

- Both devices have [Wi-Fi](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph5f955a936/26/ios/26) and [Bluetooth](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph453f556d6/26/ios/26) ® turned on.

- You’re [signed in to the same Apple Account](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph39beaede9/26/ios/26) on both devices.

- Both devices meet [minimum system requirements for Handoff](https://support.apple.com/108046/#handoff).

If you’re using a VPN, make sure its configuration doesn’t prevent local networking, as that may interfere with some Continuity features.

metadata.title: Hand off tasks between your iPhone and your other devices - Apple Support

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Hand off tasks between your iPhone and your other devices - Apple Support]

nts for Handoff](https://support.apple.com/108046/#handoff).

If you’re using a VPN, make sure its configuration doesn’t prevent local networking, as that may interfere with some Continuity features.

## Turn on Handoff

Turn on Handoff on both your iPhone and your other Apple device.

- _On your iPhone or iPad:_ Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> General > AirPlay & Continuity, then turn on Handoff.

- _On your Mac:_ Choose Apple menu ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b9fde8d26f37cf34243c7d64f3508b17.png) \> System Settings, click General in the sidebar (you may need to scroll). Click AirDrop & Handoff, then turn “Allow Handoff between this Mac and your iCloud devices” on or off.

_Note:_ If your Mac has macOS 12.5 or earlier, choose Apple menu ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b9fde8d26f37cf34243c7d64f3508b17.png) \> System Preferences, click General, then select “Allow Handoff between this Mac and your iCloud devices.”

- _On your Apple Watch:_ Open the Apple Watch app on iPhone, go to My Watch > General, then turn on Enable Handoff.

## Hand off a task between your iPhone and other devices

When your devices are near each other, an icon appears whenever a task can be handed off. Just tap or click the Handoff icon to continue working on your other device.

The Handoff icon appears in the following locations:

- _On an iPhone:_ At the bottom of the [App Switcher](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph956b628e2/26/ios/26) screen.
![The App Switcher on iPhone showing a handoff notification at the bottom of the screen.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f7f931a5386265e54a4ea32aeb46fe63.png)
- _On an iPad:_ At the right end of the Dock.

- _On a Mac:_ Toward the right end of the Dock, if the Dock is at the bottom of the screen. If you set up the Dock to appear on the left or right side of your screen, the Handoff icon appears at the bottom of the Dock.
![The Handoff icon visible in the Dock on a Mac.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3baced3a42e8f9d91780a4921a0fccd2.png)

metadata.title: Hand off tasks between your iPhone and your other devices - Apple Support

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Hand off tasks between your iPhone and your other devices - Apple Support]

t the bottom of the Dock.
![The Handoff icon visible in the Dock on a Mac.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3baced3a42e8f9d91780a4921a0fccd2.png)

## Hand off a task from your Apple Watch

If you have an Apple Watch, you can hand off tasks from certain apps on your Apple Watch to your iPhone. To learn more, see [Handoff tasks from Apple Watch](https://support.apple.com/guide/watch/apdc40081790).

## If Handoff isn’t working

- Make sure that both devices have [Wi-Fi](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph5f955a936/26/ios/26) and [Bluetooth](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph453f556d6/26/ios/26) turned on, and you’re signed in to the same [Apple Account](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphfd5021a18/26/ios/26).

- Check that your app supports Handoff. Calendar, Contacts, FaceTime, Keynote, Mail, Maps, Pages, Numbers, Reminders, and Safari, as well as many third-party apps, allow Handoff.

**Tip:** When Handoff is on, you can use [Universal Clipboard](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/copy-and-paste-between-devices-iph220ea8dca/26/ios/26#iph9e17640f4) to copy and paste text, images, photos, and videos across devices.

**See also** [Use Continuity to work across Apple devices](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/intro-to-continuity-iphf5fa30b66/26/ios/26) [Copy and paste between your iPhone and other Apple devices](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/copy-and-paste-between-devices-iph220ea8dca/26/ios/26) [Use AirDrop to send items to nearby Apple devices](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-airdrop-to-send-items-to-nearby-devices-iphcd8b9f0af/26/ios/26) [Hand off audio from iPhone to HomePod](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/homepod-and-other-wireless-speakers-iph315e0d58d/26/ios/26#iphcdd8b4570) [Hand off a FaceTime call from your iPhone to your other Apple devices](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/hand-a-facetime-call-apple-device-iph74e013a95/26/ios/26)

# Hide people, memories, and holidays in Photos on iPhone

You can set the Photos app to show certain people, days, and holidays less frequently or not at all in your memories, featured photos, and the Photos widget. You can also turn off memories and featured photos in the Photos app and the widget on your iPhone Home Screen.

## Show a person less frequently or not at all

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a photo of the person you want to show less often.

3. Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/53cc5dafac91abd63db83c91b43ef920.png), then tap Don’t Feature a Person.

If there’s more than one person in the photo, tap the person you want to feature less, then tap Next.

4. Choose Feature This Person Less or Never Feature This Person.

5. Tap Confirm.

To feature someone again, tap Collections, tap your account in the top-right corner, then tap Reset People & Pets Suggestions.

Feature This Person Less or Never Feature This Person.

5. Tap Confirm.

To feature someone again, tap Collections, tap your account in the top-right corner, then tap Reset People & Pets Suggestions.

## Turn off memories, holidays, and other featured content

You can turn off memories or featured photos in the Photos app, and prevent holiday events in your home country or region from appearing in memories and featured content.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Collections.

3. Tap your account in the top-right corner.

4. Do any of the following:

   - _Hide featured content and memories:_ Turn off Show Featured Content to prevent Photos from automatically generating featured photos and memories.

   - _Turn off holiday events:_ Turn off Show Holiday Events to prevent Photos from creating memories and featured content about holidays.

**See also** [Personalize your memory movies in Photos on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/personalize-your-memory-movies-iph1a5832438/26/ios/26) [Delete or hide photos and videos on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/delete-or-hide-photos-and-videos-iphb4defbde9/26/ios/26)

# Intro to Health data on iPhone

The Health app securely stores your health information from iPhone, iPad, and Apple Watch, as well as from compatible third-party apps and connected devices. You can also manually enter data for body measurements, symptoms, menstrual cycle information, and more.

**Siri:** On [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph229706d23/26/ios/26), [ask Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/summon_siri/26/ios/26) something like, “What’s my heart rate?” or “How far have I walked today?” (not available in all languages or regions). [Learn how to use Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-on-and-activate-siri-iph83aad8922/26/ios/26).

![The Summary screen in Health. Information about activity, sleep score, state of mind, and medications appears below Pinned.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/d036f67197ad77787aab1e6c8b03819d.png)

metadata.title: Intro to Health data on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Intro to Health data on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Health data from iPhone

iPhone automatically stores and analyzes valuable health data, including the following:

- _Steps, flights of stairs, and other walking data:_ Built-in sensors on your iPhone capture the number of steps you take, the number of flights of stairs you climb, your walking speed, and more. You can [browse highlights, trends, and details](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26) about this data in the Activity category in Health.

- _Mobility data:_ iPhone stores important mobility metrics such as walking asymmetry, double support time, and more. Over time, as you walk with iPhone in your pocket or wear it near your waist, these metrics are used to calculate and record your walking steadiness. You can [browse](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26) this data in the Mobility category. You can also [receive a notification](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/monitor-your-walking-steadiness-iphff1bf03ed/26/ios/26) and [share your notification](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/share-your-health-data-iph5ede58c3d/26/ios/26) with others if your steadiness becomes low enough to put you at increased risk of falling in the next 12 months.

- _Headphone audio levels:_ Audio level measurements from connected AirPods, EarPods, or other compatible headphones are automatically stored in Health. You can [review](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/check-your-headphone-audio-levels-iph0596a9152/26/ios/26) this data in the Hearing category to help you understand your headphone listening habits.

- _Sleep history:_ If you [set up a sleep schedule in Health](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/edit-sleep-schedules-iph304a683a0/26/ios/26), iPhone estimates the periods you’re lying in bed with the intention to sleep. You can [review this data](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26) in the Sleep category to help you meet your sleep goals.

- _Health records from your healthcare providers:_ You can [set up automatic downloads](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/download-health-records-iphc30019594/26/ios/26#iphe103f5a3e) of health records of your allergies, medical conditions, medications, and more from supported healthcare organizations (not available in all countries or regions). You can [browse these records](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-health-records-iph2b3a37ddd/26/ios/26) in Health.

metadata.title: Intro to Health data on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Intro to Health data on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Health data from Apple Watch

After you [pair iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/apple-watch-iph9252a427b/26/ios/26) with Apple Watch, periodic heart rate and blood oxygen measurements are automatically sent from Apple Watch to Health, where you can [browse highlights, trends, and details](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26#iph59b9b077f) in the Heart and Respiratory categories. You can also set up Apple Watch to send additional information to Health, including the following:

- _Exercise minutes and other activity metrics:_ You can [view these metrics](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26#iph59b9b077f) in the Activity category in Health on iPhone.

- _A history of time in daylight (Apple Watch SE 2nd generation, Apple Watch Series 6 or later, and all Apple Watch Ultra models):_ The ambient light sensor in Apple Watch estimates how much time you’ve spent in daylight. You can [view details](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26#iph59b9b077f) in the Other Data category in Health on iPhone.

- _Notifications about high or low heart rates, low cardio fitness (watchOS 7.2 or later), and irregular heart rhythms (not available in all countries or regions):_ You can [view the details](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26#iph59b9b077f) about these notifications in the Heart category.

- _Environmental noise levels and notifications about loud sounds:_ You can [view the details](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26#iph59b9b077f) about noise in your environment in the Hearing category.

- _Overnight health metrics in the Vitals app:_ You can view overnight health metrics—heart rate, respiratory rate, wrist temperature, blood oxygen, and sleep duration—to get a better understanding of your daily health status. If you wear your Apple Watch to sleep for multiple nights, the Vitals app establishes a typical range for each of your health metrics. If multiple metrics are outside your typical range, you’ll receive a notification along with context for factors that may be involved—like medications or illness, for example. You can [view the details](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26#iph59b9b077f) about your overnight vitals in the Vitals category.

- _A history of hard falls (Appl

metadata.title: Intro to Health data on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Intro to Health data on iPhone - Apple Support]

iew the details](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26#iph59b9b077f) about your overnight vitals in the Vitals category.

- _A history of hard falls (Apple Watch SE and Apple Watch Series 4 and later):_ In the Other Data category, you can [view details](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26#iph59b9b077f) about the number of times fallen.

- _Sleep score in the Sleep app:_ You can view a sleep score—a score based on estimates of how long you slept, the consistency of when you fell asleep relative to your recent history, and the amount and duration of awake periods during sleep. Your sleep score appears around 15 minutes after you wake up. You can also view the amount of sleep you had the night before, the time spent in each sleep stage, and your sleep average over the last 14 days. You can [view details](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26#iph59b9b077f) about your sleep score in the Sleep category.

- _Notifications of possible hypertension (Apple Watch Series 9, Apple Watch Ultra 2 and later):_ You can wear Apple Watch to analyze your heart data, which can be used to detect signs of possible hypertension. If your Apple Watch detects patterns of hypertension, you receive a notification to discuss them with your healthcare provider. You are also prompted to use a third-party blood pressure cuff to take measurements over a 7-day period. You can [view details](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26#iph59b9b077f) about hypertension notifications in the Heart category.

To learn more about health data from Apple Watch, see [Get started with health features](https://support.apple.com/guide/watch/apd7941a2f19), [Time spent in daylight](https://support.apple.com/guide/watch/apd3ab22534c), [Measure noise levels](https://support.apple.com/guide/watch/apd00a43a9cb), [Track your vitals](https://support.apple.com/guide/watch/apd15aa7ed96), [Manage Fall Detection](https://support.apple.com/guide/watch/apd34c409704), [View your sleep score and sleep history](https://support.apple.com/guide/watch/apded441a669), and [Receive hypertension notifications](https://support.apple.com/guide/watch/apd7c37bb802) in the Apple Watch User Guide.

metadata.title: Intro to Health data on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Intro to Health data on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Health data from other apps and devices

As you set up health and fitness apps that you download from the App Store, follow their onscreen instructions to allow them to share data with Health.

To store data from devices like blood pressure monitors and weight scales, follow their setup instructions. Bluetooth® devices [need to be paired](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/connect-bluetooth-accessories-iph3c50f191/26/ios/26#iph386380112) with iPhone.

_Note:_ You can [change health data permissions](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/share-your-health-data-iph5ede58c3d/26/ios/26#iph87bdf560d) for apps and devices at any time.

metadata.title: Intro to Health data on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Intro to Health data on iPhone - Apple Support]

12) with iPhone.

_Note:_ You can [change health data permissions](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/share-your-health-data-iph5ede58c3d/26/ios/26#iph87bdf560d) for apps and devices at any time.

## Data that you add manually

You can manually enter data about your menstrual cycles, medications, body measurements, and more.

- _Add information about your menstrual cycles:_ By logging menstrual cycle information, you can get period and fertile window predictions. See [Log menstrual cycle information in Health](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/log-menstrual-cycle-information-iph51a822b18/26/ios/26).

- _Add your medications, vitamins, and supplements:_ By adding these items, you can get reminders to take them and learn more about them. See [Track your medications in Health](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/track-your-medications-iph811670c81/26/ios/26).

- _Set up a sleep schedule:_ Scheduling regular times for getting to bed and waking up can help you meet your sleep goals. See [Set up a Sleep Focus schedule in Health](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-a-sleep-schedule-iphaf56dceb4/26/ios/26).

- _Add information to a Medical ID:_ You can provide information about your medical conditions, medications, emergency contacts, and more in an emergency Medical ID. First responders and others can view this critical information directly on your iPhone, even while it’s locked. See [Set up and view your Medical ID](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-and-view-your-medical-id-iph08022b192/26/ios/26).

- _Add information to other Health categories:_ Tap ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3506e345e882236df240ce6ef039212b.png) at the bottom right of Health, tap a category, tap ![the Details button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/465382cdc77d9a7ae9a6a530f3a2e55d.png) for the data you want to update, then tap Add Data at the top right.

**Important:** iPhone, iPad, and Apple Watch aren’t medical devices. See [Important safety information for iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/important-safety-information-iph301fc905/26/ios/26).

**See also** [View your data in Health on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26) [Share your data in Health on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/share-your-health-data-iph5ede58c3d/26/ios/26)

# Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone

In the Magnifier app, you can use your iPhone like a magnifying glass to zoom in on text or objects around you.

![The Magnifier app showing a close-up of an unripe orange.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4f58a526cefccbeb7f1440dd5659e1e6.png)

metadata.title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

s around you.

![The Magnifier app showing a close-up of an unripe orange.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4f58a526cefccbeb7f1440dd5659e1e6.png)

## Magnify nearby text or objects

1. Go to the Magnifier app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/39aaaabcae6f66284764c8368a31216c.png) on your iPhone.

2. Point the iPhone camera at the text or object you want to magnify, then drag the slider to zoom in or out.

3. You can also use any of the following controls:

   - Tap ![the Activities button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f819c7e25681484175550cdec5b01440.png) to switch between your default controls and custom sets of controls you’ve defined for recurring tasks or situations (called _activities_). See [Set your preferred controls for an activity](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/customize-controls-ipheed8d2fca/26/ios/26#iphef1068634).

   - Tap ![the Freeze frame button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png) to [temporarily freeze the frame](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/magnify-or-describe-things-around-you-iphe867dc99c/26/ios/26#iph2c76e5eaa).

   - Tap ![the Settings button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a3f742160163c000f2710f952646cafe.png) to [add more controls](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/customize-controls-ipheed8d2fca/26/ios/26), including [Detection Mode](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/magnify-or-describe-things-around-you-iphe867dc99c/26/ios/26#iph32deb9296), or edit activities you’ve created.

**Tip:** Set up a shortcut to quickly open Magnifier by clicking or pressing an iPhone button or tapping the back of iPhone, or from Control Center. See [Quickly turn accessibility features on or off](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/quickly-turn-accessibility-features-on-or-off-iph3e2e31a5/26/ios/26).

On [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph345af6a5d/26/ios/26), you can also customize the Camera Control to open Magnifier. See [Use the Camera Control to open another app](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-the-camera-control-to-open-another-app-iph3940f00d2/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Temporarily freeze the frame

You can temporarily capture what’s in the frame, without saving it to Photos.

1. Go to the Magnifier app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/39aaaabcae6f66284764c8368a31216c.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png), then do one of the following:

   - _Take one or more photos:_ Press and hold ![the Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png), then select Single Photo or Multi-Photo to take a photo or multiple photos.

     If you select Multi-Photo, you’ll see a group of photos in the bottom right corner of the screen.

   - _Capture text and reframe it:_ Press and hold ![the Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png), then select Document. Follow the written and spoken instructions to get the text in frame or drag the corners of the box that appear around the text, then tap Confirm.

     After you tap Confirm, tap ![the Reader button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/66c227b33be6046ef0d5bae03157187b.png) to view the text with Accessibility Reader. See [view your captured text in a format for easier reading](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/magnify-or-describe-things-around-you-iphe867dc99c/26/ios/26#iph9891f10ec) or to [listen to your captured text](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/magnify-or-describe-things-around-you-iphe867dc99c/26/ios/26#iph092daeb86).

   - _Customize and listen to text:_ If you’re magnifying text, tap ![the Reader button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/66c227b33be6046ef0d5bae03157187b.png) to [view your captured text in a format for easier reading](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/magnify-or-describe-things-around-you-iphe867dc99c/26/ios/26#iph9891f10ec) or to [listen to your captured text](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/magnify-or-describe-things-around-you-iphe867dc99c/26/ios/26#iph092daeb86).

   - _Share captured images:_ Tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/748a151e2276ad5df4d03ec8d4506bf7.png) for more options, suc

metadata.title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

- _Share captured images:_ Tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/748a151e2276ad5df4d03ec8d4506bf7.png) for more options, such as sharing the captured frame or saving it to your photos.

   - _Stop viewing captured images:_ Tap ![the Close button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4ed21f66913b5f929023d831b52cf74d.png) to unfreeze the frame and return to the live Magnifier lens.

To reset Magnifier, press and hold ![the Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png) anytime and select Document, Multi-Photo, or Single-Photo.

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

## View your captured text in a format for easier reading

If you magnify a frame that contains text, you can view it in a format adapted for easier reading. You can even adjust fonts, layouts, and background colors.

1. Go to the Magnifier app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/39aaaabcae6f66284764c8368a31216c.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png) to capture the frame, then tap ![the Reader button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/66c227b33be6046ef0d5bae03157187b.png).

Magnifier alerts you if no text is detected in the frame you captured.

3. Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38f95724ed0a62ccb8a071d3df51b3b2.png), then tap Customize Reader.

4. Select a theme, then tap Edit.

5. Do any of the following:

   - _Text Color:_ Choose a color or tap Custom Color.

   - _Background Color:_ Choose a color or tap Custom Color.

   - _Text Size:_ Drag the slider left or right to make the text smaller or larger.

   - _Font:_ Choose a font.

   - _High Legibility Characters:_ Turn on High Legibility Characters.

     _Note:_ This option is only available when using System Font.

   - _Bold Text:_ Turn on Bold Text.

   - _Line Spacing:_ Choose ![the Default button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b9fd2d6aa9e92a26672acc6acc9327ab.png), ![the Loose button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/67cae9458848135e5e3a3dec03fe8940.png), or ![the Open button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/72c3dcb90d47f8f65dd0be433f35db6e.png) to customize the space between lines.

   - _Word Spacing:_ Choose ![the Default button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b53ea49e79f5070b4c8308ec966ed508.png), ![the Loose button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e8b94e5c8d74595eb33f4d73c084250c.png), or ![the Open button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1fcbaa4266cbefa684205b42217aa50c.png) to customize the space between words.

   - _Character Spacing:_ Choose ![the Defa

metadata.title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

//help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1fcbaa4266cbefa684205b42217aa50c.png) to customize the space between words.

   - _Character Spacing:_ Choose ![the Default button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/514bdb16f8b93bad3df3861afce490bc.png), ![the Loose button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/34484e8b314237c6c51eaa2fc26d3a60.png), or ![the Open button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/75f9ecab7ddcfcf612ea9427dce92aa2.png) to customize the space between characters.

   - _Hyperlinks:_ Turn Show Links on or off. When turned on, you can customize the color.

   - _Playback:_ Turn Highlighting on or off. When turned on, you can customize the style or highlight color.

   - _Rename:_ Enter a name for the settings you choose.

   - _Reset to Default:_ Reset all themes to their default settings.

     **Tip:** Rename a theme with your custom settings so you can easily find it and reuse it later.

You can also use Accessibility Reader to customize the text in apps. See [Read or listen to text in apps with Accessibility Reader on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/read-listen-text-apps-accessibility-reader-iph406a46ab8/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Listen to your captured text

1. Go to the Magnifier app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/39aaaabcae6f66284764c8368a31216c.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png) to capture the frame, then tap ![the Reader button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/66c227b33be6046ef0d5bae03157187b.png).

Magnifier alerts you if no text is detected in the frame you captured.

3. Do any of the following:

   - _Listen to the text:_ Tap ![the Play button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9fc2474e8f975c92e61abda72cf78910.png).

   - _Pause the text:_ Tap ![the Pause button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a5f086eae69a48bd1373d00365edeef5.png).

   - _Skip backward or forward:_ Tap ![the Rewind button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9530a528673e0d50898c9ff1d9460c41.png) or ![the Forward button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e2ca2b5b7a76fb497f5ca74cc4fc2611.png).

   - _Increase or decrease the speed:_ Tap ![the Speaking Rate One Times button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/74f8bb5dd4579fbe396327e9f3c2478c.png), then select a speed.

   - _Go to a specific time:_ Drag the slider left or right.

     _Note:_ You can hide or show audio controls: Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38f95724ed0a62ccb8a071d3df51b3b2.png), then tap ![the Hide Audio Controls button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/24b79b52ef0508fdefc6154bd64e4384.png).

metadata.title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Get live descriptions of your surroundings

If you’re blind or have low vision, you can use Detection Mode in the Magnifier app on iPhone to scan your surroundings and get live descriptions of the scenes detected in the camera view. Live descriptions are available as text or speech.

**Important:** Detection Mode should not be relied on in high-risk or emergency situations, in circumstances where you may be harmed or injured, or for navigation.

1. Go to the Magnifier app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/39aaaabcae6f66284764c8368a31216c.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Detection Mode button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/db975b0a1433f2f55117411860975371.png) to start Detection Mode.

If you don’t see ![the Detection Mode button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/db975b0a1433f2f55117411860975371.png), you can add it. See [Customize controls](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/customize-controls-ipheed8d2fca/26/ios/26).

3. Touch and hold ![the Detection Mode button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/db975b0a1433f2f55117411860975371.png), select Scenes, then do any of the following:

   - On [supported iPhone models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphc07537a89/26/ios/26), detect [doors, people, and furniture](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/detect-doors-people-and-furniture-around-you-iph35c335575/26/ios/26#iph1320e1f65) near you.

   - On all iPhone models, [detect text in the camera frame and have it read out loud](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/detect-text-read-loud-iph29dbe3fb6/26/ios/26#iph13d9d4f67) or [point your finger at text to have it spoken](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/detect-text-read-loud-iph29dbe3fb6/26/ios/26#iph11ad5bda8).
4. To temporarily pause detection, double-tap the screen with two fingers. Double-tap again with two fingers to resume detection. To stop Detection Mode, tap ![the Detection Mode button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/db975b0a1433f2f55117411860975371.png).

If you use VoiceOver, you can turn on Live Recognition from any screen on iPhone and get descriptions of your surroundings without going to the Magnifier app. See [Get live descriptions of your surroundings with VoiceOver](https://support.apple.com/guide

metadata.title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

from any screen on iPhone and get descriptions of your surroundings without going to the Magnifier app. See [Get live descriptions of your surroundings with VoiceOver](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/get-live-descriptions-of-your-surroundings-iph37e6b3844/26/ios/26).

**See also** [Accessibility features for vision on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/overview-of-accessibility-features-for-vision-iph732dc028d/26/ios/26)
