# Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone

In the Magnifier app, you can use your iPhone like a magnifying glass to zoom in on text or objects around you.

![The Magnifier app showing a close-up of an unripe orange.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4f58a526cefccbeb7f1440dd5659e1e6.png)

metadata.title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

s around you.

![The Magnifier app showing a close-up of an unripe orange.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4f58a526cefccbeb7f1440dd5659e1e6.png)

## Magnify nearby text or objects

1. Go to the Magnifier app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/39aaaabcae6f66284764c8368a31216c.png) on your iPhone.

2. Point the iPhone camera at the text or object you want to magnify, then drag the slider to zoom in or out.

3. You can also use any of the following controls:

   - Tap ![the Activities button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f819c7e25681484175550cdec5b01440.png) to switch between your default controls and custom sets of controls you’ve defined for recurring tasks or situations (called _activities_). See [Set your preferred controls for an activity](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/customize-controls-ipheed8d2fca/26/ios/26#iphef1068634).

   - Tap ![the Freeze frame button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png) to [temporarily freeze the frame](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/magnify-or-describe-things-around-you-iphe867dc99c/26/ios/26#iph2c76e5eaa).

   - Tap ![the Settings button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a3f742160163c000f2710f952646cafe.png) to [add more controls](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/customize-controls-ipheed8d2fca/26/ios/26), including [Detection Mode](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/magnify-or-describe-things-around-you-iphe867dc99c/26/ios/26#iph32deb9296), or edit activities you’ve created.

**Tip:** Set up a shortcut to quickly open Magnifier by clicking or pressing an iPhone button or tapping the back of iPhone, or from Control Center. See [Quickly turn accessibility features on or off](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/quickly-turn-accessibility-features-on-or-off-iph3e2e31a5/26/ios/26).

On [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph345af6a5d/26/ios/26), you can also customize the Camera Control to open Magnifier. See [Use the Camera Control to open another app](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-the-camera-control-to-open-another-app-iph3940f00d2/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Temporarily freeze the frame

You can temporarily capture what’s in the frame, without saving it to Photos.

1. Go to the Magnifier app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/39aaaabcae6f66284764c8368a31216c.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png), then do one of the following:

   - _Take one or more photos:_ Press and hold ![the Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png), then select Single Photo or Multi-Photo to take a photo or multiple photos.

     If you select Multi-Photo, you’ll see a group of photos in the bottom right corner of the screen.

   - _Capture text and reframe it:_ Press and hold ![the Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png), then select Document. Follow the written and spoken instructions to get the text in frame or drag the corners of the box that appear around the text, then tap Confirm.

     After you tap Confirm, tap ![the Reader button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/66c227b33be6046ef0d5bae03157187b.png) to view the text with Accessibility Reader. See [view your captured text in a format for easier reading](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/magnify-or-describe-things-around-you-iphe867dc99c/26/ios/26#iph9891f10ec) or to [listen to your captured text](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/magnify-or-describe-things-around-you-iphe867dc99c/26/ios/26#iph092daeb86).

   - _Customize and listen to text:_ If you’re magnifying text, tap ![the Reader button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/66c227b33be6046ef0d5bae03157187b.png) to [view your captured text in a format for easier reading](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/magnify-or-describe-things-around-you-iphe867dc99c/26/ios/26#iph9891f10ec) or to [listen to your captured text](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/magnify-or-describe-things-around-you-iphe867dc99c/26/ios/26#iph092daeb86).

   - _Share captured images:_ Tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/748a151e2276ad5df4d03ec8d4506bf7.png) for more options, suc

- _Share captured images:_ Tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/748a151e2276ad5df4d03ec8d4506bf7.png) for more options, such as sharing the captured frame or saving it to your photos.

   - _Stop viewing captured images:_ Tap ![the Close button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4ed21f66913b5f929023d831b52cf74d.png) to unfreeze the frame and return to the live Magnifier lens.

To reset Magnifier, press and hold ![the Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png) anytime and select Document, Multi-Photo, or Single-Photo.

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

## View your captured text in a format for easier reading

If you magnify a frame that contains text, you can view it in a format adapted for easier reading. You can even adjust fonts, layouts, and background colors.

1. Go to the Magnifier app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/39aaaabcae6f66284764c8368a31216c.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png) to capture the frame, then tap ![the Reader button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/66c227b33be6046ef0d5bae03157187b.png).

Magnifier alerts you if no text is detected in the frame you captured.

3. Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38f95724ed0a62ccb8a071d3df51b3b2.png), then tap Customize Reader.

4. Select a theme, then tap Edit.

5. Do any of the following:

   - _Text Color:_ Choose a color or tap Custom Color.

   - _Background Color:_ Choose a color or tap Custom Color.

   - _Text Size:_ Drag the slider left or right to make the text smaller or larger.

   - _Font:_ Choose a font.

   - _High Legibility Characters:_ Turn on High Legibility Characters.

     _Note:_ This option is only available when using System Font.

   - _Bold Text:_ Turn on Bold Text.

   - _Line Spacing:_ Choose ![the Default button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b9fd2d6aa9e92a26672acc6acc9327ab.png), ![the Loose button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/67cae9458848135e5e3a3dec03fe8940.png), or ![the Open button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/72c3dcb90d47f8f65dd0be433f35db6e.png) to customize the space between lines.

   - _Word Spacing:_ Choose ![the Default button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b53ea49e79f5070b4c8308ec966ed508.png), ![the Loose button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e8b94e5c8d74595eb33f4d73c084250c.png), or ![the Open button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1fcbaa4266cbefa684205b42217aa50c.png) to customize the space between words.

   - _Character Spacing:_ Choose ![the Defa

metadata.title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

//help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1fcbaa4266cbefa684205b42217aa50c.png) to customize the space between words.

   - _Character Spacing:_ Choose ![the Default button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/514bdb16f8b93bad3df3861afce490bc.png), ![the Loose button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/34484e8b314237c6c51eaa2fc26d3a60.png), or ![the Open button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/75f9ecab7ddcfcf612ea9427dce92aa2.png) to customize the space between characters.

   - _Hyperlinks:_ Turn Show Links on or off. When turned on, you can customize the color.

   - _Playback:_ Turn Highlighting on or off. When turned on, you can customize the style or highlight color.

   - _Rename:_ Enter a name for the settings you choose.

   - _Reset to Default:_ Reset all themes to their default settings.

     **Tip:** Rename a theme with your custom settings so you can easily find it and reuse it later.

You can also use Accessibility Reader to customize the text in apps. See [Read or listen to text in apps with Accessibility Reader on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/read-listen-text-apps-accessibility-reader-iph406a46ab8/26/ios/26).

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Listen to your captured text

1. Go to the Magnifier app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/39aaaabcae6f66284764c8368a31216c.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Capture button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c790ddb8a706a9d6fd724ec5908b853f.png) to capture the frame, then tap ![the Reader button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/66c227b33be6046ef0d5bae03157187b.png).

Magnifier alerts you if no text is detected in the frame you captured.

3. Do any of the following:

   - _Listen to the text:_ Tap ![the Play button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9fc2474e8f975c92e61abda72cf78910.png).

   - _Pause the text:_ Tap ![the Pause button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a5f086eae69a48bd1373d00365edeef5.png).

   - _Skip backward or forward:_ Tap ![the Rewind button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9530a528673e0d50898c9ff1d9460c41.png) or ![the Forward button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e2ca2b5b7a76fb497f5ca74cc4fc2611.png).

   - _Increase or decrease the speed:_ Tap ![the Speaking Rate One Times button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/74f8bb5dd4579fbe396327e9f3c2478c.png), then select a speed.

   - _Go to a specific time:_ Drag the slider left or right.

     _Note:_ You can hide or show audio controls: Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38f95724ed0a62ccb8a071d3df51b3b2.png), then tap ![the Hide Audio Controls button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/24b79b52ef0508fdefc6154bd64e4384.png).

metadata.title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Magnify or describe things around you with Magnifier on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Get live descriptions of your surroundings

If you’re blind or have low vision, you can use Detection Mode in the Magnifier app on iPhone to scan your surroundings and get live descriptions of the scenes detected in the camera view. Live descriptions are available as text or speech.

**Important:** Detection Mode should not be relied on in high-risk or emergency situations, in circumstances where you may be harmed or injured, or for navigation.

1. Go to the Magnifier app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/39aaaabcae6f66284764c8368a31216c.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Detection Mode button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/db975b0a1433f2f55117411860975371.png) to start Detection Mode.

If you don’t see ![the Detection Mode button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/db975b0a1433f2f55117411860975371.png), you can add it. See [Customize controls](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/customize-controls-ipheed8d2fca/26/ios/26).

3. Touch and hold ![the Detection Mode button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/db975b0a1433f2f55117411860975371.png), select Scenes, then do any of the following:

   - On [supported iPhone models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphc07537a89/26/ios/26), detect [doors, people, and furniture](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/detect-doors-people-and-furniture-around-you-iph35c335575/26/ios/26#iph1320e1f65) near you.

   - On all iPhone models, [detect text in the camera frame and have it read out loud](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/detect-text-read-loud-iph29dbe3fb6/26/ios/26#iph13d9d4f67) or [point your finger at text to have it spoken](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/detect-text-read-loud-iph29dbe3fb6/26/ios/26#iph11ad5bda8).
4. To temporarily pause detection, double-tap the screen with two fingers. Double-tap again with two fingers to resume detection. To stop Detection Mode, tap ![the Detection Mode button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/db975b0a1433f2f55117411860975371.png).

If you use VoiceOver, you can turn on Live Recognition from any screen on iPhone and get descriptions of your surroundings without going to the Magnifier app. See [Get live descriptions of your surroundings with VoiceOver](https://support.apple.com/guide

**See also** [Accessibility features for vision on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/overview-of-accessibility-features-for-vision-iph732dc028d/26/ios/26)

# Make a call on iPhone

There are a number of ways you can make a phone call on your iPhone: dial the number on the keypad, tap a favorite or recent call, or choose a number in your contacts list.

![The Calls tab in the Phone app in Unified layout. Favorite contacts appear at the top. Below the Favorites is a list of recent calls and voicemail. Buttons to view the Calls, Contacts, and Keypad tabs appear at the bottom. The Search button is in the bottom-right corner.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4928857c56295cf9a514caafdb9a3053.png)

metadata.title: Make a call on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Make a call on iPhone - Apple Support]

abs appear at the bottom. The Search button is in the bottom-right corner.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4928857c56295cf9a514caafdb9a3053.png)

## Dial a number

**Siri:** [Ask Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/summon_siri/26/ios/26) to call or dial a number. Speak each digit separately—for example, “four one five, five five five….” For the 800 area code in the U.S., you can say “eight hundred.” [Learn how to use Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/how-to-use-siri-ipha48873ed6/26/ios/26).

Or without using Siri:

1. Go to the Phone app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4651af24ad17cb6ac594c96c6641ba6f.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Keypad at the bottom of the screen, then do any of the following:

   - _Use a different line:_ On [models with Dual SIM](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphb1759eccd/26/ios/26), tap the line at the top, then choose a line.

   - _Enter the number using the keypad:_ If you make a mistake, tap ![the Delete button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/bcba02d7aaf21d0066f1fa548a494d02.png).

   - _Redial the last number:_ Tap ![the Call button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ccd56b2c36a80b69e63079a6319c6690.png) to see the last number you dialed, then tap ![the Call button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ccd56b2c36a80b69e63079a6319c6690.png) to call that number.

   - _Paste a number you’ve copied:_ Tap the phone number field above the keypad, then tap Paste.

   - _Enter a soft (2-second) pause:_ Touch and hold the star (\*) key until a comma appears.

   - _Enter a hard pause (to pause dialing until you tap the Dial button):_ Touch and hold the pound (#) key until a semicolon appears.

   - _Enter a “+” for international calls:_ Touch and hold the “0” key until “+” appears.
3. Tap ![the Call button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ccd56b2c36a80b69e63079a6319c6690.png) to start the call.

To end the call, tap ![the End Call button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e5dd68f3f3e1af9bde771d8cd250f866.png).

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Make a call on iPhone - Apple Support]

c6690.png) to start the call.

To end the call, tap ![the End Call button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e5dd68f3f3e1af9bde771d8cd250f866.png).

## Add or remove contacts from your Favorites

1. Go to the Phone app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4651af24ad17cb6ac594c96c6641ba6f.png) on your iPhone.

2. Do one of the following, depending on which [Phone app layout](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph74958a4e6/26/ios/26) you’re using:

   - _Unified layout:_ Tap Calls at the bottom of the screen, tap Edit, then tap Add Favorite. If you’ve already added a Favorite, tap Edit, tap Edit Favorites, then tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2c6dba0cb486a1dee023b7a6ce137565.png).

   - _Classic layout:_ Tap Favorites at the bottom of the screen, then tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2c6dba0cb486a1dee023b7a6ce137565.png).
3. Tap a contact, choose the default method of communication (for example, message, call, or video) and the preferred number, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

4. To remove a contact from your Favorites, do one of the following:

   - _Unified layout:_ Tap Calls at the bottom of the screen, tap Edit, tap Edit Favorites, tap ![the Remove button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6047ad296fd44fcc70419598cf3da8fa.png) in the upper-left corner of the contact you want to remove, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

   - _Classic layout:_ Tap Favorites at the bottom of the screen, then swipe their name all the way to the left.

To contact someone in your Favorites, tap their name.

metadata.title: Make a call on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Make a call on iPhone - Apple Support]

68e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

   - _Classic layout:_ Tap Favorites at the bottom of the screen, then swipe their name all the way to the left.

To contact someone in your Favorites, tap their name.

## Redial or return a recent call

1. Go to the Phone app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4651af24ad17cb6ac594c96c6641ba6f.png) on your iPhone.

2. Do one of the following, depending on which [Phone app layout](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph74958a4e6/26/ios/26) you’re using:

   - _Unified layout:_ Tap Calls at the bottom of the screen, tap the name or number you want to call, tap ![the Call button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b7501530b3cfc58f2be0f17b95f30b72.png), then tap the number you want to call.

     _Note:_ To start a call as soon as you tap a name or number in the Recents list, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f6b5581fdf38013a42e265720f20b597.png) \> Apps > Phone, then turn on Tap Recents to Call.

   - _Classic layout:_ Tap Recents at the bottom of the screen, then tap the name or number you want to call.
3. To get more info about a call and the caller, do one of the following:

   - _Unified layout:_ Tap the name or number in the Recents list.

   - _Classic layout:_ Tap ![the Info button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c365a2ba2af247ead4528d734ba840f4.png) next to the name or phone number in Recents.

metadata.title: Make a call on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Make a call on iPhone - Apple Support]

ayout:_ Tap ![the Info button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c365a2ba2af247ead4528d734ba840f4.png) next to the name or phone number in Recents.

## Call someone on your contacts list

**Siri:** [Ask Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/summon_siri/26/ios/26) something like, “Call Eliza’s mobile.” [Learn how to use Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-on-and-activate-siri-iph83aad8922/26/ios/26).

Or without using Siri:

1. Go to the Phone app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4651af24ad17cb6ac594c96c6641ba6f.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Contacts at the bottom of the screen, then tap the contact you want to call.

3. Tap ![the Call button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b7501530b3cfc58f2be0f17b95f30b72.png), then tap the phone number you want to call.

On [models with Dual SIM](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphb1759eccd/26/ios/26), the default voice line is used for the call unless you set a preferred line for this contact.

**WARNING:** For important information about avoiding distractions that could lead to dangerous situations, see [Important safety information for iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/important-safety-information-iph301fc905/26/ios/26).

**See also** [Answer or decline incoming calls on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/answer-or-decline-incoming-calls-iph3c9947bf/26/ios/26)

# Open and view PDFs and images in Preview on iPhone

You can use the Preview app to open and view PDF documents and images.

![The main screen of the Preview app, showing PDFs and images. At the bottom of the screen are the Recents, Shared, and Browse tabs.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/843a37f313390e42400d280036d8dd4d.png)

## Open a PDF or image

You can browse and open PDFs and images directly in Preview, without opening the Files app first.

1. Go to the Preview app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ca7f2c2acd3368373c4d43c309574cf1.png) on your iPhone.

2. Do any of the following to find a file:

   - _View files you opened recently:_ Tap Recents.

   - _View shared files:_ Tap Shared.

   - _Browse available files:_ Tap Browse.

   - _Choose a location to browse:_ Tap Browse, tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ea56b8edd3ca92d7af8adc2abea59bd5.png), then tap an option under Locations.

   - _Browse by tag:_ Tap Browse, tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ea56b8edd3ca92d7af8adc2abea59bd5.png), then tap an option under Tags.
3. Optionally, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38f95724ed0a62ccb8a071d3df51b3b2.png), then select how items appear (icons or list) or how items are sorted.

4. Tap a file to open it.

**Tip:** If someone emails you a PDF form to fill out, you can tap the attached file in the Mail app to open and complete the form in Preview. You can then reply to the sender and attach the completed form. See [Reply to an email with a PDF attachment](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/reply-to-an-email-with-a-pdf-attachment-iph5be45d7f0/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Open and view PDFs and images in Preview on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Open and view PDFs and images in Preview on iPhone - Apple Support]

eply to the sender and attach the completed form. See [Reply to an email with a PDF attachment](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/reply-to-an-email-with-a-pdf-attachment-iph5be45d7f0/26/ios/26).

## View a PDF or image

1. Go to the Preview app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ca7f2c2acd3368373c4d43c309574cf1.png) on your iPhone.

2. [Open the PDF or image](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/open-and-view-pdfs-and-images-iph7a0cdad0c/26/ios/26#iph47b7f742f) you want to view.

3. Do any of the following:

   - _Zoom in or out:_ Pinch two fingers open or closed on a page or image.

   - _Show thumbnails for a PDF:_ Tap the page count. (If the page count doesn’t appear, tap the screen.)

   - _Hide thumbnails for a PDF:_ Pinch two fingers open on a page until the thumbnails disappear.

   - _Scroll pages in a PDF:_ Swipe up or down.

   - _Go to a specific page in a PDF:_ Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38f95724ed0a62ccb8a071d3df51b3b2.png), then tap Go to Page. Enter a page number, then tap Go.

metadata.title: Open and view PDFs and images in Preview on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Open and view PDFs and images in Preview on iPhone - Apple Support]

![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38f95724ed0a62ccb8a071d3df51b3b2.png), then tap Go to Page. Enter a page number, then tap Go.

## Find text in a PDF

1. Go to the Preview app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ca7f2c2acd3368373c4d43c309574cf1.png) on your iPhone.

2. [Open a PDF](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/open-and-view-pdfs-and-images-iph7a0cdad0c/26/ios/26#iph47b7f742f) you want to search.

3. Tap ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3506e345e882236df240ce6ef039212b.png).

4. Optionally, tap ![the Search Options button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/bc9621b70009a1ef45b0454cc056116e.png) in the search field and do any of the following:

   - _Exclude matches where the search text is part of another word:_ Select Whole Word.

   - _Only include results that match the capitalization of the search text:_ Select Match Case.
5. Enter a word or phrase in the search field.

If you enter a phrase, Preview searches for that exact phrase.

6. Tap ![the Next Result button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/d9ef375b37f3e012ba5381d26fd9d6a8.png) or ![the Previous Result button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b63d64e291d5e3d92005d1e487609e13.png) to go to the next or previous search result.

7. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7c29db565673f106ec2610dbf4a90268.png) to close the search results.

metadata.title: Open and view PDFs and images in Preview on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Open and view PDFs and images in Preview on iPhone - Apple Support]

ious search result.

7. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7c29db565673f106ec2610dbf4a90268.png) to close the search results.

## View information about a PDF or image

You can get information about PDF documents and images, such as creation date, filename, and more. When location information is available for a photo, you can also view the latitude and longitude where the photo was taken.

1. Go to the Preview app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ca7f2c2acd3368373c4d43c309574cf1.png) on your iPhone.

2. [Open a PDF or image](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/open-and-view-pdfs-and-images-iph7a0cdad0c/26/ios/26#iph47b7f742f).

3. Tap ![the Info button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/681b1fd7f3f687b717fd1b699fcc723c.png).

**See also** [Annotate a PDF or image in Preview on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/annotate-a-pdf-or-image-iph73ca5c8e6/26/ios/26) [Fill out and sign PDF forms in Preview on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/fill-out-and-sign-pdf-forms-iphd7e3c0c74/26/ios/26)

# Organize places with custom guides in Maps on iPhone

You can organize places into your own guides for easy reference, and you can share your guides with others.

![A list of several custom guides in Maps, including a Favorites category, and the New Guide button at the bottom right.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/79c71d23df4a2861e2cbc3a52407b9f9.png)

## See your favorites

A guide called Favorites always appears in your guides. This makes it easier for you to save places to a guide.

1. Go to the Maps app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5c95fac6a0912a04a330864acfcec1fb.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Your Guides, then tap Favorites.

**Tip:** You can pin any places that you want to see in your Places when you open Maps. See [Add a pin to your Places](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-places-and-notes-to-your-places-iph6838243e8/26/ios/26#iphe5d4c6f40).

metadata.title: Organize places with custom guides in Maps on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Organize places with custom guides in Maps on iPhone - Apple Support]

that you want to see in your Places when you open Maps. See [Add a pin to your Places](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-places-and-notes-to-your-places-iph6838243e8/26/ios/26#iphe5d4c6f40).

## Create a guide

1. Go to the Maps app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5c95fac6a0912a04a330864acfcec1fb.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Your Guides, then tap ![the Add to List button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/058005053f0d2261b9f5c7e7a50141c5.png).

3. Enter a name for the guide, then tap ![the Add Image button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/63b6ec96d982c274cb4c9a812fc89c48.png) to add an image from your photo library (optional).

4. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

## Add a place to a guide

1. Go to the Maps app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5c95fac6a0912a04a330864acfcec1fb.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap a place on the map or a search result.

3. In the place card, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/aad05ec99d91fb0497859fc9c0c181fb.png), then tap Add to Guides.

4. Choose one of your guides, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

You can also add Publisher Guides to your guides. See [Explore places with guides](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/explore-places-with-guides-iph4c213d62d/26/ios/26).

## Share a guide

1. Go to the Maps app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5c95fac6a0912a04a330864acfcec1fb.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Your Guides, tap the guide you want to share.

3. Tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/748a151e2276ad5df4d03ec8d4506bf7.png), then choose an option.

metadata.title: Organize places with custom guides in Maps on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Organize places with custom guides in Maps on iPhone - Apple Support]

uide you want to share.

3. Tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/748a151e2276ad5df4d03ec8d4506bf7.png), then choose an option.

## Edit a guide that you created

For any guide that you create, you can supply a cover image, change the title, and remove places.

1. Go to the Maps app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5c95fac6a0912a04a330864acfcec1fb.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Your Guides, then tap the guide you want to edit.

3. Tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e8c6ffaa6db04ef3fbeca71a9b0b0ecc.png), make your changes, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

## Remove a guide

1. Go to the Maps app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5c95fac6a0912a04a330864acfcec1fb.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Your Guides, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/aad05ec99d91fb0497859fc9c0c181fb.png) on the guide you want to remove, then tap Delete from Places.

**See also** [Set your location and map view in Maps on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-your-location-and-map-view-iph10d7bdf26/26/ios/26) [Search for places in Maps on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/search-for-places-iph1df24639/26/ios/26) [Add places and notes to your Places in Maps on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-places-and-notes-to-your-places-iph6838243e8/26/ios/26)

# Personalize your memory movies in Photos on iPhone

You can edit your memory movies to make them even more personal. Try out Memory mixes, which let you apply different songs with a matching photographic look. You can also choose new songs, edit the title of a memory movie, change the length, and add, reorder, or remove photos. Apple Music subscribers can add songs from the millions of songs available in the Apple Music catalog.

![A memory is playing in the middle of the screen. At the bottom of the screen are the Memory Mixes and Browse buttons. At the top of the screen are the Back, Mute, and More buttons. At the top center of the screen are the location of the photo and the name of the song playing.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/bd3919799419712b28db4b62958ff5ab.png)

## Add a Memory mix

Memory mixes are curated combinations of different songs, pacing, and styles that change the look and feel of a memory movie.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Collections.

3. Swipe left under Memories to scroll through a selection of your memories, or tap Memories to view them all.

4. Tap ![the Play button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2de4289510e31551c9b519237047a4ca.png) in the bottom-right corner of a memory to play it.

5. Tap the screen, then tap ![the Memory Mixes button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/309496ba56eb4ffc1549edc5e4a84a32.png).

6. Swipe left to view different Memory mixes.

7. Tap the screen to apply a Memory mix.

6. Swipe left to view different Memory mixes.

7. Tap the screen to apply a Memory mix.

## Change the look of a memory movie

Use Memory looks to apply consistent color and style adjustments to all the photos in a memory movie.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Collections.

3. Swipe left under Memories to scroll through a selection of your memories, or tap Memories to view them all.

4. Tap ![the Play button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2de4289510e31551c9b519237047a4ca.png) in the bottom-right corner of a memory to play it.

5. Tap the screen, then tap ![the Memory Mixes button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/309496ba56eb4ffc1549edc5e4a84a32.png).

6. Tap ![the Filters button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8abec477c96730d2a869fc2928282088.png), tap a look, then tap Done.

dc5e4a84a32.png).

6. Tap ![the Filters button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8abec477c96730d2a869fc2928282088.png), tap a look, then tap Done.

## Change the music of a memory movie

You can change a memory movie’s music by choosing from memory soundtracks or suggested songs in Apple Music. Apple Music subscribers can also access their Apple Music library and view suggested songs based on musical preferences or the content of the memory.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Collections.

3. Swipe left under Memories to scroll through a selection of your memories, or tap Memories to view them all.

4. Tap ![the Play button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2de4289510e31551c9b519237047a4ca.png) in the bottom-right corner of a memory to play it.

5. Tap the screen, then tap ![the Memory Mixes button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/309496ba56eb4ffc1549edc5e4a84a32.png).

6. Tap ![the Music button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f7ebafec367305e17c1b03b90cf50e4a.png), tap a song, then tap Done.

Apple Music subscribers can tap ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3506e345e882236df240ce6ef039212b.png) to search for songs in the Apple Music library.

an tap ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3506e345e882236df240ce6ef039212b.png) to search for songs in the Apple Music library.

## Add or remove photos in a memory movie

You can choose the photos that appear in a memory movie—add or remove suggested photos, or choose photos and videos directly from your library.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Collections.

3. Swipe left under Memories to scroll through a selection of your memories, or tap Memories to view them all.

4. Tap ![the Play button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2de4289510e31551c9b519237047a4ca.png) in the bottom-right corner of a memory to play it.

5. Tap the screen, then tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8a1f0a8df86eb1c80021954b0f092e6e.png).

6. Tap Manage Photos, then do any of the following:

   - _Add a photo from your photo library:_ Tap All, scroll through your library, select the photos you want to add, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

   - _Remove photos:_ Deselect a photo to remove it from the memory, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

   - _Search for photos in your photo library:_ Tap All, tap ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3506e345e882236df240ce6ef039212b.png), then type the name of a person, place, or date. Tap a search result, select the photos you want to add, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).
7. Tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6a2ce9f8f356d0a4dcdcfc384ef38ef9.png) to close the memory movie.

## Reorder photos in a memory movie

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

## Change the length of a memory movie

Depending on the number of photos in a memory movie, you can change its length to short, medium, or long. Your choice automatically adjusts the number of photos included.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

3. Swipe left under Memories to scroll through a selection of your memories, or tap Memories to view them all.

4. Tap ![the Play button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2de4289510e31551c9b519237047a4ca.png) in the bottom-right corner of a memory to play it.

5. Tap the screen as the memory plays, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8a1f0a8df86eb1c80021954b0f092e6e.png), then tap Short, Medium, or Long.

You can edit the title or subtitle of a memory and change the key photo that appears with the title of the memory.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

6. Tap Done.

# Poll people in Messages on iPhone

You can send a poll in any iMessage conversation to decide what to eat, which movie to see, where to travel, when to meet, and more. Everyone in the conversation can contribute options and watch as votes come in.

_Note:_ You must use iMessage on iOS 26, iPadOS 26, macOS Tahoe, watchOS 26, visionOS 26, or later to start, see, or vote in a poll. Those who can’t see it receive a message that a poll was sent to the group.

## Start a poll

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. In a conversation, tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2c6dba0cb486a1dee023b7a6ce137565.png), then tap Polls.

3. Tap each row to add options. A poll can have up to 12 options.

4. Tap ![the Send button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f5dabff8f401af1580540b6ab7aedcb8.png).

**Tip:** Turn on Apple Intelligence\* to get suggestions for polls.

## Vote in a poll

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a conversation with a poll in it.

3. Tap an option to add your vote. Tap again to remove your vote. You can vote for none or all of the options.

## Add an option to a poll

Anyone participating in a poll can add an option to it.

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a conversation with a poll in it.

3. Tap Add Choice, then enter another option.

4. Tap ![the Send button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f5dabff8f401af1580540b6ab7aedcb8.png) to add the option to the poll.

_Note:_ Options are added without votes. Tap to vote for any option you add.

_Note:_ Options are added without votes. Tap to vote for any option you add.

## Check on the results of a poll

You can see how people voted or who hasn’t voted yet.

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a conversation with a poll in it.

3. Touch and hold the poll, then tap Poll Details.

# Power adapters for iPhone

You can connect iPhone to a power outlet [using its charging cable](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/charging-cable-ipheb425c7c4/26/ios/26) (included) and a compatible power adapter (sold separately).

You can use the following Apple USB power adapters to charge iPhone. The size and style may vary depending on the country or region.

## Apple 20W USB-C power adapter

![The Apple 20-watt USB-C power adapter.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/70f2adccb9e9159299d9d4aae184cc24.png)

_Note:_ For fast charging, iPhone 12, iPhone SE (3rd generation), and later models require a power adapter with a minimum power output of 20 watts, such as the Apple 20W USB power adapter. If you use a third-party power adapter, it should meet these recommended specifications:

- _Frequency:_ 50 to 60 Hz, single phase

- _Line Voltage:_ 100 to 240 VAC

- _Output Voltage/Current:_ 9 VDC/2.2 A

- _Minimum Power Output:_ 20 W

- _Output Port:_ USB-C

## Apple 18W USB-C power adapter

![The Apple 18-watt USB-C power adapter.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8bb58c79c1e7b880bd7a44a3b4cc4521.png)

## Apple 5W USB power adapter

![The Apple 5-watt USB power adapter.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8bb58c79c1e7b880bd7a44a3b4cc4521.png)

You can also use Apple USB power adapters for iPad and Mac notebooks to charge iPhone, and you can use third-party power adapters that are compliant with applicable country regulations and international and regional safety standards.

**See also** [“Charging” in Important safety information](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/important-safety-information-iph301fc905/26/ios/26) [Apple Support article: About Apple USB power adapters](https://support.apple.com/120548) [Apple Support article: Fast charge your iPhone](https://support.apple.com/102574)

# Protect your web browsing with iCloud Private Relay on iPhone

When you [subscribe to iCloud+](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/subscribe-to-icloud-iphe9af54d75/26/ios/26), you can use iCloud Private Relay to help prevent websites and network providers from creating a detailed profile about you. When iCloud Private Relay is on, the traffic leaving your iPhone is encrypted and sent through two separate internet relays. This prevents websites from seeing your IP address and exact location while preventing network providers from collecting your browsing activity in Safari.

_Note:_ iCloud Private Relay isn’t available in all countries or regions. For more information, see the [iOS and iPadOS Feature Availability website](https://www.apple.com/ios/feature-availability/#icloud-icloud-plus).

## Turn iCloud Private Relay on

Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> \[ _your name_\] \> iCloud > Private Relay, then tap Private Relay.

_Note:_ You need to turn on iCloud Private Relay on each device where you want to use it.

metadata.title: Protect your web browsing with iCloud Private Relay on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Protect your web browsing with iCloud Private Relay on iPhone - Apple Support]

610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> \[ _your name_\] \> iCloud > Private Relay, then tap Private Relay.

_Note:_ You need to turn on iCloud Private Relay on each device where you want to use it.

## Turn iCloud Private Relay off

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> \[ _your name_\] \> iCloud > Private Relay, then tap Private Relay.

2. Do one of the following:

   - _Turn off iCloud Private Relay temporarily:_ Tap Turn Off Until Tomorrow.

     Within 24 hours, iCloud Private Relay will turn back on automatically. If you want it to resume sooner, follow the instructions for turning it on at any time.

   - _Turn off iCloud Private Relay completely:_ Tap Turn Off Private Relay.

For information about turning off iCloud Private Relay temporarily for a specific website, see [Temporarily allow a website to see your IP address](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/browse-the-web-privately-iphb01fc3c85/26/ios/26#iph313b54b9d).

## Turn iCloud Private Relay on or off for a Wi-Fi network

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Wi-Fi.

2. Tap ![the Actions Available button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c365a2ba2af247ead4528d734ba840f4.png), then turn Limit IP Address Tracking on or off.

If you turn off Limit IP Address Tracking for a Wi-Fi network on your iPhone, iCloud Private Relay is turned off for this network across all your devices where you’re [signed in to the same Apple Account](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph39beaede9/26/ios/26).

## Turn iCloud Private Relay on or off for a cellular network

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Cellular, then do one of the following:

   - _If your iPhone has a single line:_ Tap Cellular Data Options.

   - _If your iPhone has multiple lines:_ Select a line (below SIMs).
2. Turn Limit IP Address Tracking on or off.

The network setting is specific to a physical SIM or eSim in your iPhone (eSIM not available in all countries or regions). See [View or change cellular data settings](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-or-change-cellular-data-settings-iph3dd5f213/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Protect your web browsing with iCloud Private Relay on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Protect your web browsing with iCloud Private Relay on iPhone - Apple Support]

hone (eSIM not available in all countries or regions). See [View or change cellular data settings](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-or-change-cellular-data-settings-iph3dd5f213/26/ios/26).

## Set the specificity of your IP address location

Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> \[ _your name_\] \> iCloud > Private Relay > IP Address Location, then choose one of the following:

- Maintain General Location (for example, to see local content in Safari)

- Use Country and Time Zone (to make your location more obscure)

**See also** [Use Hide My Email in Safari on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-hide-my-email-iphbb888fde2/26/ios/26) [Apple Support article: About iCloud Private Relay](https://support.apple.com/102602)

# Record and transcribe a call on iPhone

In some countries and regions, you can record a phone call when you’re talking to one other person. In select regions and languages, you can view the transcript of the call in the Notes app. See the [iOS and iPadOS Feature Availability website](https://www.apple.com/ios/feature-availability/).

_Note:_ Call recording isn’t currently available in Azerbaijan, Bahrain, Egypt, the European Union, Iran, Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Morocco, Nigeria, Oman, Pakistan, Qatar, Russia, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, Turkey, United Arab Emirates, and Yemen.

## Record a call

Before you begin, check the [iOS and iPadOS Feature Availability website](https://www.apple.com/ios/feature-availability/#phone-call-recording) to see if call recording is available in your language and region, and make sure the other call participant is willing to be recorded.

1. Go to the Phone app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4651af24ad17cb6ac594c96c6641ba6f.png) on your iPhone.

2. Make a call.

3. During the call, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/53cc5dafac91abd63db83c91b43ef920.png), then tap Call Recording.

Both call participants hear an audio notice that the call is being recorded.

4. To stop recording, tap ![the Stop button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/34f8bfcee6d4b0941c899ec860f65540.png) or just hang up.

iPhone automatically saves call recordings to a Call Recordings folder in the Notes app.

5. To see the call in the Notes app, tap View Saved Call.

## Play a recorded call

1. Go to the Notes app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/752a4ee174395508df3192592b50ef60.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap a note in the Call Recordings folder, then tap ![the Play button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4696932698f5c2e6bec182493b0cd1df.png).

metadata.title: Record and transcribe a call on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Record and transcribe a call on iPhone - Apple Support]

## View a call transcript

In [select regions and languages](https://www.apple.com/ios/feature-availability/#phone-call-transcription), you can view the transcript of a recorded call in the Notes app. The transcript may not be available immediately—you may see a message that the transcription is in progress.

_Note:_ Verify the accuracy of a transcription before relying on it. It might not be a perfect representation of the conversation.

1. Go to the Notes app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/752a4ee174395508df3192592b50ef60.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap a note in the Call Recordings folder, then tap ![the Show Transcript and Summary button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/d019d5839aebef39d812641691725de6.png).

_Note:_ Spoken words in supported languages are automatically transcribed. If you don’t see a transcript, your iPhone doesn’t meet the minimum requirements or detect a supported language.

In the transcript, the speaker of each statement is identified.

3. While viewing the transcript, you can do any of the following:

   - _Play the audio from a specific point:_ Select the text you want to hear.

   - _Search the transcript:_ Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5319bdf46371a4f82e24e84aa7dee3a9.png), then tap Find in Transcript.

   - _Copy the transcript:_ Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5319bdf46371a4f82e24e84aa7dee3a9.png), then choose Add Transcript to Note or Copy Transcript.

   - _Save the audio recording:_ Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5319bdf46371a4f82e24e84aa7dee3a9.png), tap Save Audio Files, then select where you want to save the recording.

   - _Share the audio recording:_ Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5319bdf46371a4f82e24e84aa7dee3a9.png), tap Share Audio, then select how you want to share the recording.

   - _Delete the audio recording:_ Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5319bdf46371a4f82e24e84aa7dee3a9.png), then tap Delete.

     _Note:_ Deleting the audio recording also deletes the transcript.

   - _View the waveform of the recording:_ Tap ![

862495245036393/en_US/5319bdf46371a4f82e24e84aa7dee3a9.png), then tap Delete.

     _Note:_ Deleting the audio recording also deletes the transcript.

   - _View the waveform of the recording:_ Tap ![the Hide Transcript button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/773f3d2550c9c0e38dd1d90387192693.png).

If Apple Intelligence\* is turned on, you can get a summary of a phone call you record in the Phone app. See [Use Apple Intelligence in Phone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-apple-intelligence-in-phone-iph2571a9780/26/ios/26).

2. Tap a note in the Call Recordings folder, then tap ![the Play button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4696932698f5c2e6bec182493b0cd1df.png).

## Delete a recorded call

1. Go to the Notes app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/752a4ee174395508df3192592b50ef60.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap a note in the Call Recordings folder, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/5319bdf46371a4f82e24e84aa7dee3a9.png), then tap Delete.

_Note:_ Deleting the audio recording also deletes the transcript.

## Turn off Call Recording

Call Recording is on by default, but you can turn it off.

1. Go to the Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) app on your iPhone.

2. Tap Apps, then tap Phone.

3. Tap Call Recording, then turn off Call Recording.

**See also** [Record and transcribe audio in Notes on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/record-and-transcribe-audio-iphbe11247b5/26/ios/26)

# Schedule a text message on iPhone to send later

If it’s too late at night or too important to forget, you can schedule a text message to be sent at a later time.

_Note:_ You must be using [iMessage](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/about-imessage-iph4e9799206/26/ios/26). Your recipient can use any device, and they won’t know that the message was scheduled. You need to be online to reschedule, edit, or delete a scheduled message.

![A Messages conversation with messages scheduled to be sent later.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/99e95d576f0d9e322d19d37568463056.png)

## Schedule a message

You can schedule a message up to 14 days into the future.

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Go to the conversation where you want to schedule a message or [start a conversation](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/send-and-reply-to-messages-iph82fb73ba3/26/ios/26#ipha07bfaf56).

3. Tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2c6dba0cb486a1dee023b7a6ce137565.png), then tap Send Later.

4. Tap the time to open the scheduler, then choose when you want to send your message.

The border of the text field turns into a dashed line, and the time your message will be sent appears at the top of the message.

5. Enter a message, then tap ![the Send button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9c754fcfdaf85ad3a4580697d1190816.png).

The message appears with a dashed line until it’s time to send it, and it’s delivered even if all your devices are offline.

_Note:_ Scheduled messages are encrypted and stored on Apple servers only until they’re sent. When a message is sent, it’s removed from Apple servers, the balloon becomes a solid color, and its dashed line disappears.

metadata.title: Schedule a text message on iPhone to send later - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Schedule a text message on iPhone to send later - Apple Support]

messages are encrypted and stored on Apple servers only until they’re sent. When a message is sent, it’s removed from Apple servers, the balloon becomes a solid color, and its dashed line disappears.

## Change the time to send a message

You can reschedule a message until its delivery time.

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Go to the conversation with the message you want to reschedule.

If you don’t see your message, scroll down to the bottom of the conversation. Scheduled messages may appear below sent messages.

3. Tap Edit beside the date (above your scheduled message), then do any of the following:

   - _Reschedule it:_ Tap Edit Time, choose a new time, then tap away from the schedule to commit the new time.

   - _Send it immediately:_ Tap Send Message.

## Edit a scheduled message

You can edit a scheduled message until its delivery time.

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Go to the conversation with the scheduled message you want to edit.

If you don’t see your message, scroll down to the bottom of the conversation. Scheduled messages may appear below sent messages.

3. Touch and hold the message bubble, then tap Edit.

4. Make any changes, then tap ![the Send Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7c29db565673f106ec2610dbf4a90268.png) to resend with edits or ![the Cancel Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c49333ed954a7b2d7b5ab9f9f3537f12.png) to revert.

metadata.title: Schedule a text message on iPhone to send later - Apple Support

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Schedule a text message on iPhone to send later - Apple Support]

bf4a90268.png) to resend with edits or ![the Cancel Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c49333ed954a7b2d7b5ab9f9f3537f12.png) to revert.

## Delete a scheduled message

When you delete a message before its scheduled time, it’s canceled and not delivered to the recipient.

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Go to the conversation with the scheduled message you want to delete.

If you don’t see your message, scroll down to the bottom of the conversation. Scheduled messages may appear below sent messages.

3. Touch and hold the message you want to cancel, then tap Delete.

**See also** [Unsend and edit messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/unsend-and-edit-messages-iphe67195653/26/ios/26) [Turn read receipts on or off in Messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-read-receipts-on-or-off-iph5e713a045/26/ios/26) [Delete messages and attachments in Messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/delete-messages-and-attachments-iph2c9c4bfcb/26/ios/26)

# Send and save GIFs in Messages on iPhone

You can search #images to find hundreds of trending GIFs you can add to your text messages and save GIFs others send to you (not available in all countries or regions).

## Add a GIF from \#images

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. In a conversation, tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2c6dba0cb486a1dee023b7a6ce137565.png), then tap #images. (You may have to swipe up.)

3. To search for a specific GIF, tap “Find images,” enter a keyword (like “birthday”), then tap a search suggestion or ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6b6ef36774e0b221f5803b448d3fdbf6.png).

4. Tap a GIF to add it to your message, then tap ![the Send button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f5dabff8f401af1580540b6ab7aedcb8.png).

_Note:_ GIFs from #images animate only in the Messages app on Apple devices.

metadata.title: Send and save GIFs in Messages on iPhone - Apple Support

ps://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f5dabff8f401af1580540b6ab7aedcb8.png).

_Note:_ GIFs from #images animate only in the Messages app on Apple devices.

## Save a GIF

You can save a GIF that you receive in a message to use again later.

1. Go to the Messages app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ad1f1e874b79cd34473c266e98f9cbd0.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open the conversation that includes the GIF that you want to save.

3. Touch and hold the GIF, then tap Save.

The GIF is saved to the Photos app. See [Send a photo or video](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/take-and-edit-photos-or-videos-iph3d039f23/26/ios/26#iph19b7ebee1).

**See also** [Take and edit photos or videos in Messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/take-and-edit-photos-or-videos-iph3d039f23/26/ios/26) [Send stickers in Messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/send-stickers-iph37b0bfe7b/26/ios/26) [Draw and handwrite messages on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/draw-and-handwrite-messages-iph3d4cb79c9/26/ios/26) [Customize onscreen motion on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/customize-onscreen-motion-iph0b691d3ed/26/ios/26)

# Set an alarm in Clock on iPhone

You can turn your iPhone into an alarm clock, or simply ask Siri to set one for you. Alarms can be set for any time and repeated on specific days of the week. You can also adjust how long the snooze lasts.

**Siri:** [Ask Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/summon_siri/26/ios/26) something like, “Set an alarm for 7 a.m.” [Learn how to use Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-on-and-activate-siri-iph83aad8922/26/ios/26).

_Note:_ You can also set up a wake up alarm as part of a full [sleep schedule](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-a-sleep-schedule-iphaf56dceb4/26/ios/26) (including bedtimes, wake up times, and more) in the Health app. If you don’t want to set up a sleep schedule, you can set a regular alarm in Clock for the time you want to wake up.

![A screen for changing an alarm, with settings to change the time of the alarm, choose whether the alarm repeats, add an alarm label, select an alarm sound, turn on snooze, and change the snooze duration.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1cc62f1e6439891ae090e3945a4287aa.png)

metadata.title: Set an alarm in Clock on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set an alarm in Clock on iPhone - Apple Support]

el, select an alarm sound, turn on snooze, and change the snooze duration.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1cc62f1e6439891ae090e3945a4287aa.png)

## Set an alarm

You can set alarms for any time, including one for the time you want to wake up. Any alarm you set up in Clock is unrelated to a sleep schedule.

1. Go to the Clock app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38d6e8b6bb0cf0e0575d007c6d53fb29.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Alarms, then tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/1a9bda2390113800474d880037fe3324.png).

3. Set the time, then choose any of the following options:

   - _Repeat:_ Choose the days of the week you want the alarm to repeat.

   - _Label:_ Give the alarm a name, like “Water the plants.”

   - _Sound:_ Choose a vibration, song, or ringtone.

   - _Snooze:_ Turn on to see a Snooze button with your alarm.

   - _Snooze Duration:_ Choose how long to pause the alarm.
4. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/780f7463300e41a2f875f912ab290a0c.png).

To change the alarm, tap the alarm time. Or tap Edit at the top left, then tap the alarm time.

## Change the alarm’s volume

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Sounds & Haptics.

3. Under Ringtone and Alerts, drag the volume slider.

**Tip:** To use the volume buttons on your device to change the alarm volume, turn on Change with Buttons.

Your alarm sounds even when you:

- Turn on [Silent mode](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/silence-iphone-iph81c7fd7d1/26/ios/26#iphcc98fdd04).

- Turn on Do Not Disturb (or another [Focus](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-a-focus-iphd6288a67f/26/ios/26)).

- Connect headphones to your iPhone. The alarm plays at a set volume through both the built-in speakers on your iPhone as well as your headphones.

## Turn off an alarm

1. Go to the Clock app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38d6e8b6bb0cf0e0575d007c6d53fb29.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Alarms.

3. Tap the button next to the alarm time.

metadata.title: Set an alarm in Clock on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set an alarm in Clock on iPhone - Apple Support]

(https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38d6e8b6bb0cf0e0575d007c6d53fb29.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Alarms.

3. Tap the button next to the alarm time.

## Remove an alarm

1. Go to the Clock app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/38d6e8b6bb0cf0e0575d007c6d53fb29.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Alarms.

3. Swipe left on the alarm, then tap ![the Trash button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9d1c99a52b6a3e69d129929f0235e09a.png).

**See also** [Change your wake up alarm in Clock on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-your-wake-up-alarm-iphf2a780f81/26/ios/26) [Set up your first recurring sleep schedule](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-a-sleep-schedule-iphaf56dceb4/26/ios/26#iph2d7daf6fc) [Turn off alarms and delete sleep schedules in Health on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-off-alarms-and-delete-sleep-schedules-iph35e7e0e5f/26/ios/26)
