# Set schedules with Screen Time on iPhone

Screen Time helps you manage when and how you use iPhone. You can create schedules and set limits that work best for you. Here are some ways you can customize your Screen Time schedule:

- _Schedule downtime:_ Take regular breaks from your screen by setting specific times when most apps are unavailable—like during dinner or bedtime.

- _Set limits for app use:_ Control how much time you spend in apps by setting daily time limits.

- _Allow certain apps and contacts:_ Select apps and contacts that stay available during downtime and after you reach time limits.

You can [Block apps during scheduled Downtime](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-schedules-with-screen-time-iphb0c7313c9/26/ios/26#iph749c2c574) so that limits can’t be ignored once they’re reached.

metadata.title: Set schedules with Screen Time on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set schedules with Screen Time on iPhone - Apple Support]

[Block apps during scheduled Downtime](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-schedules-with-screen-time-iphb0c7313c9/26/ios/26#iph749c2c574) so that limits can’t be ignored once they’re reached.

## Schedule time away from the screen

Block apps and notifications for periods when you want time away from your devices. During downtime, only calls, messages, and apps you choose to allow are available. To learn how to allow certain apps and contacts, see [Allow or block calls and messages from specific contacts](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/block-calls-and-messages-with-screen-time-iph4df1c0dad/26/ios/26#iphff45567ed) and [Choose apps and contacts to allow at all times](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-schedules-with-screen-time-iphb0c7313c9/26/ios/26#iph8a312fcd5).

_Note:_ By default, Screen Time limits can be ignored once reached. To learn how to prevent limits from being ignored, see [Block apps during scheduled Downtime](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-schedules-with-screen-time-iphb0c7313c9/26/ios/26#iph749c2c574).

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) app on your iPhone.

2. Tap Screen Time.

If you want to turn on Downtime for your child, tap your child's name.

3. Tap App & Website Activity, then turn on App & Website Activity if you haven't already.

4. Tap Downtime, then do one of the following:

   - Tap Turn On Downtime Until Tomorrow.

   - Tap Scheduled to schedule downtime in advance.

When you schedule downtime, a 5-minute reminder is sent before downtime begins. You can ignore the reminder, or turn on downtime until the beginning of the scheduled downtime.

5. Select Every Day or Customize Days, then set the start and end times.

_Note:_ When you select Customize Days, you can set schedules for different days of the week—like weekends.

You can turn off the Downtime schedule at any time by turning off Scheduled.

metadata.title: Set schedules with Screen Time on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set schedules with Screen Time on iPhone - Apple Support]

d end times.

_Note:_ When you select Customize Days, you can set schedules for different days of the week—like weekends.

You can turn off the Downtime schedule at any time by turning off Scheduled.

## Block apps during scheduled Downtime

If you set a Screen Time passcode for yourself or your child, you can choose whether apps are completely blocked or just dimmed during downtime.

1. Go to the Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) app on your iPhone.

2. Tap Screen Time.

If you want to turn on this feature for your child, tap your child's name.

3. Tap Downtime, then turn on Block at Downtime.

When Block at Downtime is turned on, restricted apps can't be opened during downtime—they appear dimmed with an hourglass icon. When Block at Downtime is off, you see a reminder that it's downtime, but you can choose to continue using the apps.

## Set limits for app use

Set a time limit for a category of apps (for example, Games or Social Networking) and for individual apps.

1. Go to the Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) app on your iPhone.

2. Tap Screen Time.

If you want to set app limits for your child, tap your child's name.

3. Tap App Limits, then tap Add Limit.

4. Select one or more app categories.

To set limits for individual apps, tap the category name to see all the apps in that category, then select the apps you want to limit. If you select multiple categories or apps, the time limit you set applies to all of them.

5. Tap Next at the top right, then set the amount of time allowed.

To set an amount of time for each day, tap Customize Days, then set limits for specific days.

6. When you finish setting limits, tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

metadata.title: Set schedules with Screen Time on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set schedules with Screen Time on iPhone - Apple Support]

specific days.

6. When you finish setting limits, tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

## Choose apps and contacts to allow at all times

Specify apps that can be used and contacts you can communicate with at all times (for example, in the event of an emergency), even during downtime.

1. Go to the Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) app on your iPhone.

2. Tap Screen Time.

If you want to turn this feature on for your child, tap your child's name.

3. Tap Always Allowed.

4. To specify contacts you want to allow communication with, tap Contacts.

The option you selected in [communication limits](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/block-apps-app-downloads-websites-purchases-iph3ff83f3b1/26/ios/26) appears here. You can change this setting to Specific Contacts, then choose one of the following:

   - _Choose From My Contacts:_ To select specific people to allow communication with.

   - _Add New Contact:_ To add a new contact and allow communication with that person.
5. Tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ea56b8edd3ca92d7af8adc2abea59bd5.png) at the top left, then tap ![the Remove button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f49364d4831a585edf6235c7a73db060.png) below Allowed Apps or ![the Insert button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e567b2e0439c66eb44b64cf9663c305a.png) below Choose Apps.

6. Tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ea56b8edd3ca92d7af8adc2abea59bd5.png) at the top left.

**See also** [Set up Screen Time for a child on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-screen-time-for-a-child-ipha200da319/26/ios/26) [Check for sensitive images and videos in Screen Time on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/check-for-sensitive-images-and-videos-iph0d3607e18/26/ios/26)

# Set up a Focus on iPhone

Focus is a feature that helps you concentrate on a task by minimizing distractions. When you want to concentrate on a specific activity, you can customize one of the provided Focus options—for example Work, Personal, or Sleep—or [create a custom Focus](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-a-focus-iphd6288a67f/18.0/ios/18.0#iphf9f22c88c). You can use Focus to temporarily silence all notifications, or allow only specific notifications—ones that apply to your task, for example—and let other people and apps know you’re busy.

**Tip:** To quickly silence all notifications, [open Control Center](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph7c412b4db/18.0/ios/18.0), tap Focus, then turn on Do Not Disturb.

metadata.title: Set up a Focus on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up a Focus on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Set up a Focus

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Focus, then tap a Focus—for example, Do Not Disturb, Personal, or Work.

For your Focus, you can set up the options described in the steps below, but you don’t have to set up all of them.
![A screen showing five provided Focus options—Do Not Disturb, No messages during calls, Sleep, Personal, and Work. The Share Across Devices option is on, which allows the same Focus settings to be used across your Apple devices.](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/e966b88c21db42304c7d61758d3e3330.png)
2. Specify which people and apps to allow notifications from during your Focus:

   - _People:_ Tap People, then tap Allow Notifications From. Tap ![the Add People button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/27bb9655dccf819f6b80069b98f42598.png), select contacts, tap Done, then tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/672f0597b3bef6ad7262a337e288ceb4.png) at the top of the screen. You can also silence specific people by tapping Silence Notification From instead.

   - _Apps:_ Tap Apps, then tap Allow Notifications From. Tap ![the Add Apps button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/27bb9655dccf819f6b80069b98f42598.png), select apps, tap Done, then tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/672f0597b3bef6ad7262a337e288ceb4.png) at the top of the screen. You can also silence specific apps by tapping Silence Notifications From instead.
3. Tap Options, then select any of the following:

   - _Show silenced notifications in Notification Center instead of on the Lock Screen:_ Turn off Show On Lock Screen.

   - _Hide notification badges on your silenced apps to prevent the distraction:_ Turn on Hide Notification Badges.

   - _Silence calls and notifications:_ Tap Silence Notifications, then choose Always or While Locked.

   - _Dim the Lock Screen during this Focus:_ Turn on Dim Lock Screen.

   - _Intelligently allow important notifications to interrupt you:_ If you have any iPhone 16 model, iPhone 15 Pro, or iPhone 15 Pro Max, turn on Intelligent Breakthrough & Silencing. Any notifications specifically allowed or silenced will alwa

metadata.title: Set up a Focus on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up a Focus on iPhone - Apple Support]

ions to interrupt you:_ If you have any iPhone 16 model, iPhone 15 Pro, or iPhone 15 Pro Max, turn on Intelligent Breakthrough & Silencing. Any notifications specifically allowed or silenced will always be allowed or silenced.
4. When you’re finished selecting options, tap ![the Back button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/672f0597b3bef6ad7262a337e288ceb4.png) at the top of the screen.

5. Specify the Lock Screen and Home Screen options:

   - _Choose the Lock Screen for this Focus:_ Tap the Lock Screen preview below Customize Screens, select a Lock Screen, then tap Done at the top of the screen.

   - _Choose the Home Screen for this Focus:_ Tap the Home Screen preview below Customize Screens, select a Home Screen, then tap Done at the top of the screen.

     _Note:_ To make changes to the Home Screen to customize it further for your Focus, see [Move apps and widgets on the Home Screen](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/move-apps-and-widgets-on-the-home-screen-iphd2fc8ce30/18.0/ios/18.0).

After setting up your Focus, you can return to Settings > Focus at any time and change any of the options you chose above.

metadata.title: Set up a Focus on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up a Focus on iPhone - Apple Support]

ow you’re busy.

**Tip:** To quickly silence all notifications, [open Control Center](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph7c412b4db/18.0/ios/18.0), tap Focus, then turn on Do Not Disturb.

## Turn a Focus on or off

You can [turn a Focus on or off manually in Control Center](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-a-focus-on-or-off-iph5c3f5b77b/18.0/ios/18.0) or [schedule the Focus to turn on automatically](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-a-focus-on-or-off-iph5c3f5b77b/18.0/ios/18.0#iph219573264).

## Add Focus filters

When you set up a Focus, you can add app filters that determine what information apps will show during the Focus. For example, you can choose which mail account or which calendar to use during the Focus.

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Focus, then tap the Focus you want to add filters to.

2. Scroll down to Focus filters, then tap Add Filter.

3. Tap an app, then select the information from that app you want to use during the Focus:

   - _Calendar:_ Choose which calendars you want to show during the Focus.

   - _Mail:_ Choose which mail accounts you want to use during the Focus.

   - _Messages:_ Choose which message conversations you want to see during the Focus—for example, only conversations from people you’ve allowed notifications from during this Focus.

   - _Safari:_ Choose which Tab Group you want to use during the Focus.
4. Tap Add to add the filter to the Focus.

_Note:_ Focus filters don’t get synced across devices when you have [Share Across Devices](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-a-focus-iphd6288a67f/18.0/ios/18.0#iphd5eeb63ff) turned on in Settings > Focus.

metadata.title: Set up a Focus on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up a Focus on iPhone - Apple Support]

ters don’t get synced across devices when you have [Share Across Devices](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-a-focus-iphd6288a67f/18.0/ios/18.0#iphd5eeb63ff) turned on in Settings > Focus.

## Create a custom Focus

If you want to focus on an activity that’s different from any of the provided Focus options, you can create a custom Focus.

![A Focus setup screen for the additional provided Focus options, including Custom, Driving, Fitness, Gaming, Mindfulness, and Reading.](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/86c7dec606ea6379c4de7e58edb3025a.png)

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Focus.

2. Tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/12d48fa9a82b5d7e291da08f1769ba33.png) at the top right, then tap Custom.

3. Enter a name for your Focus, then tap Return.

4. Choose a color and an icon to represent your Focus, then tap Next.

5. Tap Customize Focus, then set up the options for your custom Focus, following the instructions in [Set up a Focus](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-a-focus-iphd6288a67f/18.0/ios/18.0#ipheb6ee12a0).

metadata.title: Set up a Focus on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up a Focus on iPhone - Apple Support]

ze Focus, then set up the options for your custom Focus, following the instructions in [Set up a Focus](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-a-focus-iphd6288a67f/18.0/ios/18.0#ipheb6ee12a0).

## Keep your Focus settings up to date across all your Apple devices

You can use the same Focus settings on all your Apple devices where you’re [signed in to the same Apple Account](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph39beaede9/18.0/ios/18.0).

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Focus.

2. Turn on Share Across Devices.

_Note:_ When you turn on Share Across Devices and turn on Share Focus Status, your Focus can show that you have notifications silenced. For example, if you silence notifications when you [Set up a Focus](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-a-focus-iphd6288a67f/18.0/ios/18.0#ipheb6ee12a0), people who text you will see that your notifications are silenced. You can change this setting for specific people on their contact card.

**See also** [Turn on or schedule a Focus on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-a-focus-on-or-off-iph5c3f5b77b/18.0/ios/18.0) [Link a Focus to your Lock Screen](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/create-a-custom-lock-screen-iph4d0e6c351/18.0/ios/18.0#ipheaae24c57) [Move apps and widgets on the iPhone Home Screen](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/move-apps-and-widgets-on-the-home-screen-iphd2fc8ce30/18.0/ios/18.0)

# Set up and turn on Switch Control on iPhone

To set up [Switch Control](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/intro-to-switch-control-iphc9d32b862/18.0/ios/18.0), you need to connect and add one or more [switches](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/intro-to-switch-control-iphc9d32b862/18.0/ios/18.0#iph5aeefee2c), then assign an action to each switch. You can choose a scanning style and fine-tune other settings to suit your needs. After you turn on Switch Control, you may need to change the assigned actions or readjust the settings. If you have difficulty performing these steps, you can get personalized help at your nearest Apple Store.

## Step 1: Connect an external switch (optional)

If you want to use an adaptive accessory, joystick, game controller, or [MFi](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph6553e7a63/18.0/ios/18.0) device for Switch Control, you need to first connect it to iPhone by following the manufacturer’s instructions. If the switch connects using Bluetooth®, you need to pair it with iPhone.

1. Turn on the switch.

2. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Bluetooth, turn on Bluetooth, tap the name of the switch, then follow the onscreen instructions.

metadata.title: Set up and turn on Switch Control on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up and turn on Switch Control on iPhone - Apple Support]

ssets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Bluetooth, turn on Bluetooth, tap the name of the switch, then follow the onscreen instructions.

## Step 2: Add switches and assign actions

You can use only one switch, or you can use multiple switches to accommodate different actions. Each switch performs an assigned action, such as selecting an item or moving the cursor to the next item.

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Switch Control > Switches.

2. Tap Add New Switch, then choose External, Screen, Camera, Back Tap, or Sound.

If you’re trying to add an external switch but it isn’t shown in the list, make sure it’s connected to your iPhone as described above in [Step 1: Connect an external switch (optional)](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-and-turn-on-switch-control-iph400b2f114/18.0/ios/18.0#iph5ec880cf4).

3. Assign an action to the switch.

The action for the switch depends on the number of switches you have and the scanning style you choose in [Step 3: Choose a scanning style](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-and-turn-on-switch-control-iph400b2f114/18.0/ios/18.0#iph3ae3637e7), below. If you don’t know which action to assign, choose Select Item, which is compatible with Auto Scanning (the default scanning style). You can return to this setting later to change the assigned action.

**Tip:** Turn your iPhone into a game controller that uses gestures you’ve already set up, like the same switch (or alternative input method) that you navigate and control your iPhone with. Or you may decide to reuse your [Back Tap](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/back-tap-iphaa57e7885/18.0/ios/18.0) gestures instead.

metadata.title: Set up and turn on Switch Control on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up and turn on Switch Control on iPhone - Apple Support]

input method) that you navigate and control your iPhone with. Or you may decide to reuse your [Back Tap](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/back-tap-iphaa57e7885/18.0/ios/18.0) gestures instead.

## Step 3: Choose a scanning style

By default, the cursor automatically moves from one item to the next after a specified duration. If you prefer to move the cursor manually, you can choose a different scanning style.

Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Switch Control > Scanning Style, then choose one of the following:

- _Auto Scanning:_ The cursor automatically moves to the next item after a specified duration. This is the default scanning style.

If you use Auto Scanning with just one switch, make sure the Select Item action is assigned to the switch.

- _Manual Scanning:_ When you activate your Move to Next Item switch, the cursor moves to the next item.

Manual Scanning requires at least two switches. Make sure the Move to Next Item action is assigned to one switch, and the Select Item action is assigned to another switch.

- _Single Switch Step Scanning:_ When you activate the Move to Next Item switch, the cursor moves to the next item; if no action is taken within a specified duration, the current item is automatically selected.

This scanning style requires only one switch. Make sure the Move to Next Item action is assigned to the switch.

metadata.title: Set up and turn on Switch Control on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up and turn on Switch Control on iPhone - Apple Support]

tion is taken within a specified duration, the current item is automatically selected.

This scanning style requires only one switch. Make sure the Move to Next Item action is assigned to the switch.

## Step 4: Fine-tune Switch Control settings

You can adjust the behavior of Switch Control in a variety of ways, to suit your specific needs and style. For example, you can change the scanning speed or have iPhone speak the highlighted items.

Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Switch Control, then adjust any of the following:

- _Auto Scanning Time:_ Adjust the item scanning speed.

- _Pause on First Item:_ Set scanning to pause on the first item in a group.

- _Loops:_ Choose how many times to cycle through the screen before hiding Switch Control.

- _Move Repeat:_ Set the delay before moving to the previous or next item while a switch is pressed.

- _Long Press:_ Set whether a different action occurs when you press and hold a switch, and how long to wait before performing that action.

- _Tap Behavior:_ Choose a tap behavior and set the interval for performing a second switch action to show the Scanner menu.

- _Focused Item After Tap:_ Choose whether Switch Control resumes scanning at an item you tap or from the beginning.

- _Hold Duration:_ Specify how long you need to press and hold a switch before it’s accepted as a switch action.

- _Ignore Repeat:_ Ignore accidental repeated switch triggers.

- _Sound Effects:_ Turn on sound effects.

- _Speech:_ Speak items as they’re scanned.

- _Menu Items:_ Choose the actions shown in the Scanner menu and the order in which they appear.

- _Group Items:_ Group items for faster navigation.

- _Large Cursor:_ Make the highlight more prominent.

- _Cursor Color:_ Choose a different highlight color.

metadata.title: Set up and turn on Switch Control on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up and turn on Switch Control on iPhone - Apple Support]

u and the order in which they appear.

- _Group Items:_ Group items for faster navigation.

- _Large Cursor:_ Make the highlight more prominent.

- _Cursor Color:_ Choose a different highlight color.

## Step 5: Turn on Switch Control

**Important:** When Switch Control is turned on, you can’t use the standard gestures on the touchscreen to exit Switch Control.

Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Switch Control, then turn on Switch Control.

**Tip:** You can add Switch Control to [Accessibility Shortcut](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/quickly-turn-accessibility-features-on-or-off-iph3e2e31a5/18.0/ios/18.0), then quickly turn it on or off by triple-clicking the side button (on an iPhone with Face ID) or the Home button (on other iPhone models).

**See also** [Intro to Switch Control on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/intro-to-switch-control-iphc9d32b862/18.0/ios/18.0) [Use Switch Control on iPhone to select items, perform actions, and more](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/select-items-perform-actions-and-more-iph8de250c54/18.0/ios/18.0) [Control several devices with one switch on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/control-several-devices-with-one-switch-iph626dd390a/18.0/ios/18.0) [Accessibility features for mobility on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/overview-accessibility-features-mobility-iph76d37ce7d/18.0/ios/18.0)

# Set up cellular service on iPhone

Your iPhone needs a physical SIM or an eSIM to connect to a cellular network. (Not all options are available on all models or in all countries and regions. On iPhone 14 and later, some models only use eSIM.) Contact your carrier to get a SIM and set up cellular service.

metadata.title: Set up cellular service on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up cellular service on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Set up an eSIM

[Supported iPhone models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph97b3e34cd/26/ios/26) can digitally store an eSIM provided by your carrier. If your carrier supports either eSIM Carrier Activation or eSIM Quick Transfer, you can turn on your iPhone and follow the instructions to activate your eSIM during setup.

If you already completed setup, do the following:

1. Make sure your iPhone is [connected to an available Wi-Fi or cellular network](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/connect-to-the-internet-iphd1cf4268/26/ios/26). eSIM setup requires an internet connection.

2. Add an eSIM using one of the following methods:

   - _eSIM Carrier Activation:_ Some carriers can assign a new eSIM directly to your iPhone; contact your carrier to initiate this process. When you receive the “Finish Setting Up Cellular” notification, tap it. Or go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f6b5581fdf38013a42e265720f20b597.png) \> Cellular, then tap Set Up Cellular or Add eSIM.

   - _eSIM Quick Transfer:_ Some carriers support transferring a phone number from your previous iPhone to your new iPhone without needing to contact them (requires iOS 16 or later on both devices).

     On your new iPhone, go to Settings > Cellular, tap Set Up Cellular or Add eSIM, then tap Transfer From Nearby iPhone or choose a phone number. On your previous iPhone, follow the instructions to confirm the transfer.

     _Note:_ After your phone number is transferred to your new iPhone, it stops working on your previous iPhone.

   - _Scan a QR Code provided by your carrier:_ Go to Settings > Cellular, tap Set Up Cellular or Add eSIM, then tap Use QR Code. (You may need to tap Other Options first.) Position iPhone so the QR code appears in the frame, or enter the details manually. You may be asked to enter a confirmation code provided by your carrier.

   - _Transfer from a different smartphone:_ If your previous phone isn’t an Apple iPhone, contact your carrier to transfer the phone number.

   - _Activate service through a participating carrier’s app:_ Go to the App Store, download the carrier’s app, then use the app to activate cellular service.

   - Travel eSIM: A travel eSIM can maintain coverage and may help you avoid international rates. Make sure you have cellular service, then go to Settings > Cellular, tap Add eSIM, then tap View Travel Options. Follow the on-screen instructions. See Apple Supp

metadata.title: Set up cellular service on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up cellular service on iPhone - Apple Support]

y help you avoid international rates. Make sure you have cellular service, then go to Settings > Cellular, tap Add eSIM, then tap View Travel Options. Follow the on-screen instructions. See Apple Support article [Use eSIM while traveling internationally with your iPhone](https://support.apple.com/en-us/118227).
3. To verify the eSIM is activated, go to Settings > Cellular, then make sure the new line (below eSIMs) is turned on.

For troubleshooting steps, see the Apple Support article [If you can’t set up an eSIM on your iPhone](https://support.apple.com/102478).

metadata.title: Set up cellular service on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up cellular service on iPhone - Apple Support]

twork. (Not all options are available on all models or in all countries and regions. On iPhone 14 and later, some models only use eSIM.) Contact your carrier to get a SIM and set up cellular service.

## Install a physical SIM

You can get a nano-SIM card from a carrier or move it from your previous iPhone.

_Note:_ Physical SIM isn’t supported on all models.

1. Insert a paper clip or SIM eject tool into the small hole of the SIM tray, then push in toward iPhone to eject the tray.
![A paper clip or SIM eject tool is inserted into the small hole of the tray on the left side of iPhone to eject and remove the tray.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e6af7dc07de78dc75fb695746b1b4cfb.png)

_Note:_ The shape and orientation of the SIM tray depend on the iPhone model and your country or region.

2. Remove the tray from iPhone.

3. Place the SIM in the tray. The angled corner determines the correct orientation.
![A SIM being inserted into the tray on iPhone; the angled corner is in the upper left.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4e1901fd03d564c53b46361f62f41e0c.png)
4. Insert the tray back into iPhone.

5. If you previously set up a PIN on the SIM, carefully enter the PIN when prompted.

**WARNING:** Never try to guess a SIM PIN. An incorrect guess can permanently lock your SIM, and you won’t be able to make phone calls or use cellular data through your carrier until you get a new SIM. See the Apple Support article [Use a SIM PIN for your iPhone or iPad](https://support.apple.com/118228).

metadata.title: Set up cellular service on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Set up cellular service on iPhone - Apple Support]

be able to make phone calls or use cellular data through your carrier until you get a new SIM. See the Apple Support article [Use a SIM PIN for your iPhone or iPad](https://support.apple.com/118228).

## Convert a physical SIM to an eSIM

If your carrier supports it, you can convert a physical SIM to an eSIM on a [supported iPhone model](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph97b3e34cd/26/ios/26).

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f6b5581fdf38013a42e265720f20b597.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Cellular, then tap Set Up Cellular or Add eSIM.

3. Choose the phone number with a physical SIM.

4. Tap Convert to eSIM, then follow the onscreen instructions.

**Important:** The availability of cellular capabilities depends on the wireless network, your iPhone model, and your location.

Consider applicable data, voice, and roaming charges when managing your cellular plans, especially when you travel with iPhone. See [View or change cellular data settings](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-or-change-cellular-data-settings-iph3dd5f213/26/ios/26).

Some carriers let you unlock iPhone for use with another carrier (additional fees may apply). Contact your carrier for authorization and setup information. See the Apple Support article [How to unlock your iPhone for use with a different carrier](https://support.apple.com/109316).

**See also** [Connect to a cellular network if Wi-Fi isn’t available](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/connect-to-the-internet-iphd1cf4268/26/ios/26#iphbba3b6ccd) [Use Dual SIM on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-dual-sim-iph9c5776d3c/26/ios/26) [Apple Support article: About eSIM on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/118669) [Apple Support article: Use eSIM while traveling internationally with your iPhone](https://support.apple.com/118227)

# Share your location in Find My on iPhone

Before you can use the Find My app to share your location with friends, you need to set up location sharing.

_Note:_ If you set up location sharing and others still can’t see your location, make sure Location Services is on in Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Privacy & Security > Location Services > Find My. See [Control the location information you share](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/control-the-location-information-you-share-iph3dd5f9be/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Share your location in Find My on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Share your location in Find My on iPhone - Apple Support]

cy & Security > Location Services > Find My. See [Control the location information you share](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/control-the-location-information-you-share-iph3dd5f9be/26/ios/26).

## Set up location sharing

1. Go to the Find My app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3ef46077881e8558ba33ff4dc013dbbe.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Me at the bottom of the screen, then turn on Share My Location.

The device sharing your location appears next to Sharing From.

3. If your iPhone isn’t currently sharing your location, you can tap Use This iPhone as My Location.

_Note:_ To share your location from another device, open Find My on the device and change your location to that device. If you share your location from an iPhone that’s paired with Apple Watch (GPS + Cellular models), your location is shared from your Apple Watch when you’re out of range of your iPhone and Apple Watch is on your wrist.

You can also change your location sharing settings in Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> \[ _your name_\] \> Find My.

## Set a label for your location

You can set a label for your current location to make it more meaningful (like Home or Work). When you tap Me, you see the label in addition to your location.

1. Go to the Find My app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3ef46077881e8558ba33ff4dc013dbbe.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Me at the bottom of the screen, then tap Location Label.

3. Select a label.

To add a new label, tap Add Custom Label, enter a name, then tap Done.

metadata.title: Share your location in Find My on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Share your location in Find My on iPhone - Apple Support]

e8558ba33ff4dc013dbbe.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Me at the bottom of the screen, then tap Location Label.

3. Select a label.

To add a new label, tap Add Custom Label, enter a name, then tap Done.

## Share your location with a friend

1. Go to the Find My app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3ef46077881e8558ba33ff4dc013dbbe.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap People at the bottom of the screen, tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/12d48fa9a82b5d7e291da08f1769ba33.png), then choose Share My Location.
![The Find My screen open to the People list. There are four people in the list: Olivia Rico, Dawn Ramirez, Ashley Rico, and Will Rico. Tap the Add button at the top of the People list to share your location.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a91e773fa08c67fad2411fc4eaa9fa22.png)
3. In the With field, type the name of a friend you want to share your location with (or tap ![the Add Contact button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a5ea01967d4192105230259d32bcba54.png) and select a contact).

4. Tap Send and choose how long you want to share your location.

You can also [notify a friend or family member when your location changes](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/notify-a-friend-when-your-location-changes-iph9bfec93b1/26/ios/26).

If you’re a member of a Family Sharing group, see [Share your location with family members](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/share-locations-family-locate-lost-devices-iph6231f621a/26/ios/26#iph74f3e3974).

## Stop sharing your location

You can stop sharing your location with a specific friend or hide your location from everyone.

- _Stop sharing with a friend:_ Tap People at the bottom of the screen, then tap the name of the person you don’t want to share your location with. Tap Stop Sharing My Location, then tap Stop Sharing Location.

- _Hide your location from everyone:_ Tap Me at the bottom of the screen, then turn off Share My Location.

metadata.title: Share your location in Find My on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Share your location in Find My on iPhone - Apple Support]

to share your location with. Tap Stop Sharing My Location, then tap Stop Sharing Location.

- _Hide your location from everyone:_ Tap Me at the bottom of the screen, then turn off Share My Location.

## Respond to a location sharing request

1. Go to the Find My app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3ef46077881e8558ba33ff4dc013dbbe.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap People at the bottom of the screen.

3. Tap Share below the name of the friend who sent the request and choose how long you want to share your location.

If you don’t want to share your location, tap Cancel.

## Stop receiving new location sharing requests

1. Go to the Find My app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3ef46077881e8558ba33ff4dc013dbbe.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Me at the bottom of the screen, then turn off Allow Friend Requests.

**See also** [Use Precision Finding on iPhone to meet up with a friend](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/meet-up-with-a-friend-iph3effd0ed6/26/ios/26)

# Sign in and manage your Apple Account on iPhone

Your Apple Account gives you access to Apple services such as the App Store, the Apple Games app, the iTunes Store, Apple Books, Apple Music, FaceTime, iCloud, iMessage, and more.

When you create a new Apple Account in China mainland or India, you can use your mobile phone number as your Apple Account user name. See the Apple Support article [Create and manage an Apple Account using a phone number instead of an email address](https://support.apple.com/105034).

## Sign in to your Apple Account

If you didn’t sign in during setup, do the following:

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f6b5581fdf38013a42e265720f20b597.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Apple Account.

3. Tap one of the following:

   - _Use Another Apple Device:_ Bring your other iPhone or iPad (with iOS 17, iPadOS 17, or later) nearby, then follow the onscreen instructions.

   - _Sign in Manually:_ Enter your email address or phone number, then enter your password. If you don’t have an Apple Account, you can create one.
4. If you protect your account with two-factor authentication, enter the six-digit verification code.

If you forgot your Apple Account password, see the [Reset your password website](https://iforgot.apple.com/).

metadata.title: Sign in and manage your Apple Account on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Sign in and manage your Apple Account on iPhone - Apple Support]

tect your account with two-factor authentication, enter the six-digit verification code.

If you forgot your Apple Account password, see the [Reset your password website](https://iforgot.apple.com/).

## Change your Apple Account settings

![The Apple Account Settings shown with a list of options including personal information, subscriptions, Find My, and more.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/937d8d89ada7237bba10087443bf279f.png)

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f6b5581fdf38013a42e265720f20b597.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap your name, then do any of the following:

   - _Update Personal Information:_ Update your name, birthdate, and Memoji or photo associated with your Apple Account. You can also set up your preferences for communication from Apple, and see how your data is managed.

   - _Change Sign-In & Security:_ Change your Apple Account password, turn on [two-factor authentication](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphd321ccd59/26/ios/26), add or remove phone numbers and emails you can use to sign in, and [set up Account Recovery or Legacy Contact](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/keep-your-apple-account-secure-iph904b71f28/26/ios/26).

   - _Manage Payment & Shipping:_ Select your payment methods and shipping address for your Apple Account and purchases from the Apple Store.

   - _View and manage your subscriptions:_ [View and manage your subscriptions](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/see-your-purchases-and-subscriptions-iph4e3e7324f/26/ios/26) from Apple and subscriptions that you purchased in apps from the App Store.

   - [Use iCloud](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-icloud-iphde0f868fd/26/ios/26).

   - [Manage Family Sharing](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-family-sharing-iph223f61318/26/ios/26).

   - Use [Find My](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/share-your-location-iph01954dc44/26/ios/26).

   - _Manage Media & Purchases:_ Manage the accounts linked to Apple Music, Apple Podcasts, Apple TV, and Apple Books and select purchasing settings.

   - Use [Sign in with Apple](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/sign-in-with-apple-iph238921d37/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Sign in and manage your Apple Account on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Sign in and manage your Apple Account on iPhone - Apple Support]

pple Music, Apple Podcasts, Apple TV, and Apple Books and select purchasing settings.

   - Use [Sign in with Apple](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/sign-in-with-apple-iph238921d37/26/ios/26).

## Add or update the payment method associated with your Apple Account

The payment information associated with your Apple Account is used for Apple media purchases and services.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f6b5581fdf38013a42e265720f20b597.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap \[ _your name_\], then tap Payment & Shipping. You may need to authenticate with Face ID or Touch ID.

3. Tap Add Payment Method or tap an existing payment method.

See the Apple Support article [If you need to change or update your Apple Account payment method](https://support.apple.com/118293).

**See also** [Keep your Apple Account secure on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/keep-your-apple-account-secure-iph904b71f28/26/ios/26) [Apple Support article: Manage and use your Apple Account](https://support.apple.com/105023) [Apple Account website](https://account.apple.com/)

# Silence iPhone

With the push of a button or flip of a switch, you can quickly silence your iPhone.

## Turn Silent mode on or off

When Silent mode is on, iPhone doesn’t ring or play alerts or other sound effects (but iPhone may still vibrate). When Silent mode is off, iPhone plays all sounds.

- On [models with an Action button](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph44c8b4227/26/ios/26): Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Sounds & Haptics, then turn Silent mode on or off.

You can also configure the Action button to turn Silent mode on or off, in addition to other functions. See [Use and customize the Action button on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-and-customize-the-action-button-iphe89d61d66/26/ios/26).

- On other models: Flip the Ring/Silent switch toward the back of your iPhone to turn Silent mode on. Flip it toward the front to turn Silent mode off.

**Important:** Clock alarms, audio apps such as Music, and many games play sounds through the built-in speaker, even when Silent mode is on. In some countries or regions, the sound effects for Camera, Voice Memos, and Emergency Alerts are played, even when the Ring/Silent switch is set to silent.

metadata.title: Silence iPhone - Apple Support

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Silence iPhone - Apple Support]

t-in speaker, even when Silent mode is on. In some countries or regions, the sound effects for Camera, Voice Memos, and Emergency Alerts are played, even when the Ring/Silent switch is set to silent.

## Silence iPhone by turning on Do Not Disturb

You can temporarily silence calls, alerts, and notifications by turning on Do Not Disturb.

1. [Open Control Center](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph7c412b4db/26/ios/26).

2. Tap Focus, then tap Do Not Disturb.

When Do Not Disturb is turned on, you won’t hear ringtones or text tones for incoming calls, texts, or notifications. Also ![the Do Not Disturb icon](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b26597fdb9cbbc61c9e6dd3789968f2a.png) appears in the [status bar](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/learn-the-meaning-of-iphone-status-icons-iphef7bb57dc/26/ios/26) at the top of the screen.

See [Turn on or schedule a Focus](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-a-focus-on-or-off-iph5c3f5b77b/26/ios/26).

# Start a workout in Fitness on iPhone

You can start individual workout sessions—from cardio-focused options such as High Intensity Interval Training (HIIT), Outdoor Run, and Outdoor Cycle, to strength-based workouts like Functional Strength Training, Core Training, and Kickboxing. You can also set media to automatically play when you begin a workout type: If you subscribe to Apple Music, Apple Music selects media based on the workout type and the music you love. Or you can choose media yourself.

If you have just an iPhone, you can do a workout with the following workout types:

- Outdoor Walk

- Outdoor Run

- Hiking

- Outdoor Push Walking Pace

- Outdoor Push Running Pace

- Outdoor Cycle

When you connect an Apple Watch, AirPods Pro 3, Powerbeats Pro 2, or a compatible Bluetooth enabled third-party heart rate monitor, you can do a workout with workout metrics displayed on the screen.

_Note:_ If you have iPhone connected to AirPods Pro 3 or a third-party heart rate monitor, you have access to more workout types like Kickboxing and Pilates. If you have an Apple Watch, you have access to all workout types.

![The Fitness workout screen showing three workout types. Buttons to log a workout and connect a heart rate monitor are at the top of the screen.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/71e6469ad109509943527ae1a72c411e.png)

metadata.title: Start a workout in Fitness on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Start a workout in Fitness on iPhone - Apple Support]

g a workout and connect a heart rate monitor are at the top of the screen.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/71e6469ad109509943527ae1a72c411e.png)

## Connect a heart rate device

Before you start a workout on your iPhone, make sure you’ve connected an Apple Watch, AirPods Pro 3, Powerbeats Pro 2, or Bluetooth®-enabled third-party heart rate monitors that connect to the Health app. For more information on AirPods Pro 3, see [Track your heart rate during workouts with AirPods Pro 3](https://support.apple.com/guide/airpods/dev1b40fb47d) in the AirPods User Guide.

If you do not have a heart rate device connected, do the following:

1. Go to the Fitness app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4a171706431000fd128f989d724dd5ca.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Workout, then tap ![the Heart Rate Devices button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7493f45b28c115223f51d17eb3433aaa.png) in the top-right corner.

3. Tap Bluetooth Settings, then [pair your iPhone with a Bluetooth accessory](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/connect-bluetooth-accessories-iph3c50f191/26/ios/26#iph386380112).

## Start a workout

1. Go to the Fitness app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4a171706431000fd128f989d724dd5ca.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Workout, then scroll to the workout type you want to do.

To see more workout types, tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/443066cf6168833f76d9d7d3a2b80897.png) at the bottom of the tab.

3. To start the workout, tap ![the Start button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/083d349f844bc6723f9159d4fbf3d0a9.png).

metadata.title: Start a workout in Fitness on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Start a workout in Fitness on iPhone - Apple Support]

t the bottom of the tab.

3. To start the workout, tap ![the Start button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/083d349f844bc6723f9159d4fbf3d0a9.png).

## Choose audio for a workout

You can set media to automatically play when you begin a workout type—either media selected for you based on what you normally listen to, or media you choose yourself.

1. Go to the Fitness app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4a171706431000fd128f989d724dd5ca.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Workout, scroll to a workout type, then tap ![the Media button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/960aa53985e64fde7d76889d4f7e9507.png).

3. Turn on Autoplay Media.

4. If you subscribe to Apple Music, you can have media play automatically based on the workout type and the music you listen to. Tap Picked for You.

5. To choose your own media, tap Choose Media, tap Add Media, then do one of the following:

   - _Choose a suggested option:_ Tap an option below Suggestions.

     **Tip:** To view more suggestions, tap Music or Podcasts below Sources.

   - _Choose a playlist:_ Tap Music, tap Playlists, then choose a playlist.
6. When you’re finished, tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8bcd220bb69165d0a7a2c11d4a868121.png).

_Note:_ Music or other audio you’re already listening to before you begin your workout continues to play.

metadata.title: Start a workout in Fitness on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Start a workout in Fitness on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Use the workout controls

During a workout, you can pause, mark a segment, [send a Check In](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-check-in-iphc143bb7e9/26/ios/26#iph83285f205), lock controls, and more. When you finish a workout, you can view a summary of how you did.

1. Go to the Fitness app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4a171706431000fd128f989d724dd5ca.png) on your iPhone.

2. [Start a workout](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/start-a-workout-in-fitness-iph8475d8510/26/ios/26#iph2d72baf57), then do any of the following:

   - _Pause the workout:_ Tap ![the Pause button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b6aad128109b0598370d877b45a0f41d.png).

   - _Resume the workout:_ Tap ![the Resume button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/12a02f28edef20c789378cbdd5794a86.png).

   - _Mark a segment:_ Tap ![the Segments button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/28f5451ed77382293fde5c4aaee87226.png).

   - _Connect a heart rate device:_ Tap ![the Heart Rate Devices button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7493f45b28c115223f51d17eb3433aaa.png).

   - _End the workout:_ Swipe up from the bottom of the screen, then tap End Workout. Review the workout summary, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8bcd220bb69165d0a7a2c11d4a868121.png).

   - _Mute Workout Buddy:_ Swipe up from the bottom of the screen, then tap Mute.

   - _Start a Check-In:_ Swipe up from the bottom of the screen, tap Check-In, then follow the onscreen instructions.

   - _Stop viewing metrics on iPhone (Apple Watch required):_ Swipe up from the bottom of the screen, then tap Stop Viewing.

     _Note:_ To start viewing again, swipe right on your Apple Watch, then tap View.

   - _Lock the workout controls:_ Swipe up from the bottom of the screen, then tap Lock Controls.

**See also** [See your activity summary in Fitness on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/see-your-activity-summary-iph4c34a8a95/26/ios/26) [Customize a workout in Fitness on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/customize-a-workout-iphbcd56be45/26/ios/26)

# Start watching and control playback in the Apple TV app on iPhone

In the Apple TV app, you can stream TV shows, movies, sports, and more. You can also download movies and episodes or a full season of a TV show.

Content from Apple TV and Apple TV channels plays in the Apple TV app, while content from other providers plays in their video app. Use the playback controls to play or pause what you’re watching, skip backward or forward, and more.

When you watch with your friends using SharePlay, the playback controls are shared by everyone on the FaceTime call. To learn more about using SharePlay in the Apple TV app, see [Use SharePlay to watch, listen, and play together in FaceTime](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/shareplay-watch-listen-play-iphb657eb791/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Start watching and control playback in the Apple TV app on iPhone - Apple Support

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Start watching and control playback in the Apple TV app on iPhone - Apple Support]

ut using SharePlay in the Apple TV app, see [Use SharePlay to watch, listen, and play together in FaceTime](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/shareplay-watch-listen-play-iphb657eb791/26/ios/26).

## Play a TV show or movie

![The Home tab showing a featured Apple Original in the center, above the Continue Watching row. At the bottom, from left to right, are the Home, Apple TV, Store, Library, and Search tabs.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/08e5599a6c8e1fc14d6a2dbdc34f8f59.png)[Stream _The Buccaneers_ on the Apple TV app with a subscription](https://tv.apple.com/us/show/the-buccaneers/umc.cmc.g28hcpusm23xbs9zwvnwn9re)

1. Go to the Apple TV app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/abed9ba23a400d9fcb746e6d2e1c3a59.png) on your iPhone.

2. Do any of the following:

   - Swipe left or right at the top of the screen to browse featured items, then tap the Play button (if available).

   - Tap Home, then tap an item in the [Continue Watching](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/personalize-the-home-tab-iphbf1137ac0/26/ios/26#iph97c4169ed) row to immediately pick up where you left off.

   - Scroll down to view more rows, then tap an item you want to watch. When the page for that item opens, tap the Play button (if available) or tap a specific episode or trailer you want to watch.

     **Tip:** Tap a row title to view all items in a category or view more information about a collection.

tap the Play button (if available) or tap a specific episode or trailer you want to watch.

     **Tip:** Tap a row title to view all items in a category or view more information about a collection.

## Download content

![The Apple TV tab showing a list of episodes of an Apple Original. Buttons to download the entire season and each episode appear on the right side of the screen.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2d689bf9343c1943d9fd1175176a58f5.png)[Stream _Hijack_ on the Apple TV app with a subscription](https://tv.apple.com/us/show/hijack/umc.cmc.1dg08zn0g3zx52hs8npoj5qe3)

In the Apple TV app, you can download movies and episodes of a TV show. You can also download a full season of a TV show.

1. Go to the Apple TV app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/abed9ba23a400d9fcb746e6d2e1c3a59.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap an item to see its details.

3. Do any of the following (not all options are available for all titles):

   - _Download movies:_ Tap ![the Download button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/96cc67f38140da9b3bb0e8f3dcedcb2e.png) in the top-right corner.

   - _Download an episode of a TV show:_ Tap ![the Download button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/96cc67f38140da9b3bb0e8f3dcedcb2e.png) in the top-right corner, tap a season, then tap ![the Download button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a2bc1b532bdbca3dd86ef6d8380c53e0.png) next to the episode you want to download.

   - _Download a full season of a TV show:_ Tap ![the Download button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/96cc67f38140da9b3bb0e8f3dcedcb2e.png) in the top-right corner, tap a season, then tap the season button above the list of episodes.

You can [find the downloaded item in your library](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/manage-your-library-iph3ecbb37ad/26/ios/26) and watch it even when iPhone isn’t connected to the internet.

| ![The Close button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7e0676994a0e6a73eafc448515ed6ada.png) | Stop playback |

When the audio is muted, subtitles automatically appear. And if you skip backward 10 seconds, subtitles appear. To turn off subtitles, tap ![the Legible Options button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a5f47277938d4cfcc33bd5ec4a2aeb09.png), then tap Off.

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Start watching and control playback in the Apple TV app on iPhone - Apple Support]

.

You can [find the downloaded item in your library](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/manage-your-library-iph3ecbb37ad/26/ios/26) and watch it even when iPhone isn’t connected to the internet.

## Change audio options during playback

1. During playback, tap the screen to show the playback controls.

2. Tap ![the Audio Track button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ebd585f0d6bc186a983b8830cfd99f24.png), then tap Enhance Dialogue.

3. Tap one of the following:

   - _Enhance More:_ Makes dialogue very prominent in the mix.

   - _Enhance:_ Makes dialogue more prominent in the mix.

   - _Off:_ Turns Enhance Dialogue off.

metadata.title: Start watching and control playback in the Apple TV app on iPhone - Apple Support

ogue.

3. Tap one of the following:

   - _Enhance More:_ Makes dialogue very prominent in the mix.

   - _Enhance:_ Makes dialogue more prominent in the mix.

   - _Off:_ Turns Enhance Dialogue off.

## Get information about shows, characters, actors, and music

During playback, you can get more information about the movie or TV show you’re watching or go directly to Continue Watching.

With InSight, you can get information in real time about actors, characters, and music currently onscreen while you’re watching an Apple TV movie or TV show (Apple TV subscription required). You can tap an actor to view their background and filmography page, or quickly view a song that’s playing in the background and add it to an Apple Music playlist (Apple Music subscription required).

1. During playback, tap the screen to show the playback controls.

2. Tap any of the following options:

   - _Info:_ See more information about what you’re watching.

   - _InSight:_ Tap an actor or character to see more info. If music information appears, tap the song to add it to a playlist in the Music app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a8584141e5732119d6e19d28e0fbd248.png) (Apple TV and Apple Music subscriptions required).

   - _Continue Watching:_ Go directly to Continue Watching, then tap an item to start watching it.

   - _Live Sports:_ View live and upcoming sports events.

# Summarize notifications and reduce interruptions with Apple Intelligence on iPhone

With its deep understanding of language, Apple Intelligence\* can help condense the information most important to you. Notifications are summarized so you can scan them for key details, such as when a group chat is particularly active. Your iPhone can also determine which notifications have priority over others. And the Reduce Interruptions Focus shows you only the notifications that might need immediate attention, like a text about an early pickup from daycare.

![An iPhone showing the Lock Screen. A summarized time-sensitive notification appears at the bottom of the screen.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2f5bd600415c21fea99417c85d48aec8.png)

_Note:_ Apple Intelligence is not available on all [iPhone models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph275f4d617/26/ios/26) or in all languages or regions.\*\* To access the most recent available features, make sure you’re using the [latest version of iOS](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/update_os/26/ios/26) and have [Apple Intelligence turned on](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph3212e4900/26/ios/26).

## Customize notification summaries

Your iPhone can summarize long or stacked notifications, making them easier to understand at a glance.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Notifications.

3. Tap Summarize Notifications, turn on Summarize Notifications, then tap Choose Notifications to Summarize.

4. Tap the notifications you want to summarize, then tap Summarize Selected Notifications.

To change which notifications you want to summarize, tap the button next to an app.

metadata.title: Summarize notifications and reduce interruptions with Apple Intelligence on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Summarize notifications and reduce interruptions with Apple Intelligence on iPhone - Apple Support]

ications to Summarize.

4. Tap the notifications you want to summarize, then tap Summarize Selected Notifications.

To change which notifications you want to summarize, tap the button next to an app.

## Turn notification summaries on or off

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Notifications.

3. Tap Summarize Notifications, then turn Summarize Notifications on or off.

## Turn priority notifications on

Your iPhone can determine which notifications have priority over others. Priority notifications appear at the top of the stack, so you can catch up on what you may have missed.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Notifications.

3. Tap Prioritize Notifications, then turn Prioritize Notifications on.

To turn off priority notifications, turn Prioritize Notifications off.

To change which notifications you want to prioritize, tap the button next to an app.

You can also change settings when you receive a priority notification on the Lock Screen. When a priority notification appears, do one of the following:

- _Turn off Priority Notifications for a specific app:_ Swipe left, tap Options, then tap Turn Off All \[ _app_\] Notifications.

- _Turn off all Priority Notifications:_ Swipe left, tap Options, then tap Turn Off Prioritization.

metadata.title: Summarize notifications and reduce interruptions with Apple Intelligence on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Summarize notifications and reduce interruptions with Apple Intelligence on iPhone - Apple Support]

ions for a specific app:_ Swipe left, tap Options, then tap Turn Off All \[ _app_\] Notifications.

- _Turn off all Priority Notifications:_ Swipe left, tap Options, then tap Turn Off Prioritization.

## Use the Reduce Interruptions Focus

The Reduce Interruptions Focus understands the content of your notifications and shows you the most important ones, but silences notifications that are less important.

You can customize a Reduced Interruptions Focus.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Focus.

3. Tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/12d48fa9a82b5d7e291da08f1769ba33.png) at the top right, then tap Reduce Interruptions.

4. Tap Customize Focus, then tap People or Apps.

5. Choose the people and apps to allow notifications from.

_Note:_ When you turn on Intelligent Breakthrough & Silencing for any Focus, any notifications specifically allowed or silenced will always be allowed or silenced.

**See also** [Change notification settings on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-notification-settings-iph7c3d96bab/26/ios/26) [Turn on or schedule a Focus on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-a-focus-on-or-off-iph5c3f5b77b/26/ios/26)

# Sync a third-party workout app to Fitness on iPhone

You can sync a compatible third-party workout app to the Fitness app on iPhone to help you meet your fitness goals. Fitness suggests workout apps that you can download from the App Store.

1. Go to the Fitness app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4a171706431000fd128f989d724dd5ca.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap the Activity rings, then scroll down to Add To Your Rings.

_Note:_ You can also browse apps in the App Store. See [Get apps in the App Store](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/get-apps-iphc90580097/26/ios/26).

3. Choose an app, then download it from the App Store.

4. Open the app and if prompted, grant permission to share data with the Health app.

Any workout you complete in a compatible third-party app appears in your activity summary and contributes to the progress toward closing your Move ring in the Fitness app.

To find out if a workout app you’ve downloaded is compatible with the Fitness and Health apps, see the Apple Support article [Manage Health data on your iPhone or Apple Watch](https://support.apple.com/108779).

**See also** [Get apps in the App Store on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/get-apps-iphc90580097/26/ios/26) [Share health and fitness data with apps and devices](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/share-your-health-data-iph5ede58c3d/26/ios/26#iph87bdf560d) [Change Fitness settings on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-fitness-settings-iph5f0e22170/26/ios/26)

# Back up and sync your photos and videos with iCloud

You can use iCloud Photos to keep your photos and videos securely stored in iCloud. If you turn on iCloud Photos on all your devices, you can see the photos and videos you took on iPhone on any device. You can also access your iCloud photos and videos in a web browser.

When iCloud Photos is turned on, the photos and videos you take are uploaded automatically and stored in their original format at full resolution. Any changes you make to your photo collection on one device are reflected on your other devices, too.

_Note:_ Make sure that you’re signed in to the same [Apple Account](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphfd5021a18/26/ios/26) on all your devices, and that your devices meet these minimum system requirements: iOS 8.3, iPadOS 13.1, macOS 10.10.3, or later, or a Windows device with iCloud for Windows 7.x or later.

To learn more, see [Set up iCloud Photos on all your devices](https://support.apple.com/guide/icloud/mm7e90c1ba48/).

## Turn on iCloud Photos

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap your name, then tap iCloud.

3. Tap Photos, then turn on Sync this iPhone.

iCloud automatically uploads every photo and video you take to iCloud, so your photos are backed up and in sync, and you can access your library from any device, anytime.

metadata.title: Back up and sync your photos and videos with iCloud - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Back up and sync your photos and videos with iCloud - Apple Support]

n turn on Sync this iPhone.

iCloud automatically uploads every photo and video you take to iCloud, so your photos are backed up and in sync, and you can access your library from any device, anytime.

## Check if your photos and videos are backed up to iCloud

You can check that iCloud Photos is syncing your photos to iCloud, so that you have copies if something happens to your iPhone.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Collections.

3. In the top-right corner of the screen, you can see a preview of your syncing status. You may see any of the following:

   - _Yellow badge:_ iCloud syncing is paused. Tap your account icon to see the details.

   - _Yellow warning icon:_ Your iCloud storage is almost full. Tap your account icon to see the details.

   - _Red exclamation mark:_ Your iCloud storage is full. See [Get more iCloud storage](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/sync-photos-videos-icloud-iph961b96c4d/26/ios/26#iph9ebd883f3).

   - _Blue badge:_ You have notifications from a shared library or shared album. Tap your account icon to see the details.

   - _Partially filled blue circle around your account icon:_ Photos are actively uploading to iCloud. Tap your account icon to see the details.

   - _Partially filled gray outline around your account icon:_ Uploads are paused. Tap your account icon to see the details.
4. Tap your account to see more details. The last time your iPhone synced with iCloud appears below the account profile.

_Note:_ If iCloud sync is in progress or paused, a notification also appears in the upper-left corner of the library.

## Turn off Optimize Storage on your iPhone

iCloud Photos can help you make the most of the storage space on your iPhone. When Optimize iPhone Storage is turned on, all your full‑resolution photos and videos are stored in iCloud in their original formats, with storage-saving versions kept on your iPhone. Optimize iPhone Storage is turned on by default, but you can turn it off.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap your name, then tap iCloud.

3. Tap Photos, then tap Download and Keep Originals.

metadata.title: Back up and sync your photos and videos with iCloud - Apple Support

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Back up and sync your photos and videos with iCloud - Apple Support]

69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap your name, then tap iCloud.

3. Tap Photos, then tap Download and Keep Originals.

## Get more iCloud storage

If your uploaded photos and videos exceed your storage plan, you can upgrade to iCloud+ for more storage and additional features. See [Subscribe to iCloud+](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/subscribe-to-icloud-iphe9af54d75/26/ios/26).

**See also** [Use iCloud on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-icloud-iphde0f868fd/26/ios/26) [iCloud User Guide: How iCloud keeps information up to date across all your devices](https://support.apple.com/guide/icloud/mmd44763ead3) [iCloud User Guide: Set up iCloud Photos on all your devices](https://support.apple.com/guide/icloud/mm7e90c1ba48)
