# Take portraits with your iPhone camera

You can apply a depth-of-field effect that keeps your subject—people, pets, or objects—sharp while creating a beautifully blurred foreground and background. You can also apply and adjust different lighting effects to your portraits.

metadata.title: Take portraits with your iPhone camera - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Take portraits with your iPhone camera - Apple Support]

## Take a portrait

![The Camera screen in Portrait mode; in the viewfinder, the subject is sharp and the background is blurred. The dial to select Portrait Lighting is open in the bottom of the frame and Studio Light is selected.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/df76d3049b2fdb62131de066b2bb1a5e.png)

1. Open Camera ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/71ce3044f246f5f1f828b819f1c395b6.png) on your iPhone.

2. Do any of the following:

   - Swipe left to select Portrait mode.

   - Tap ![the Depth button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/25cdab1e14eff2b794ff28c2bccd2ba7.png) (if a face is detected in the camera frame; on [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphde3d86c94/26/ios/26)).

   - Use the Camera Control to select Portrait mode; see [Select and adjust the Camera Control settings](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-the-camera-control-iph0c397b154/26/ios/26#iphf10d97496).
3. Drag ![the Portrait Lighting control](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3d400598bc5159bfe3fc68d86d0a0f2e.png) to choose a lighting effect:

   - _Natural Light:_ Sharp focus with a blurred background

   - _Studio Light:_ Bright, clean lighting

   - _Contour Light:_ Dramatic shadows with highlights and lowlights

   - _Stage Light:_ Spotlight effect against a black background

   - _Stage Light Mono:_ Stage Light effect in black and white

   - _High-Key Light Mono:_ Grayscale subject on a white background
4. Tap the Shutter button or click the Camera Control on ( [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph345af6a5d/26/ios/26)) to take the portrait.

After you take the portrait, you can remove the effect if you want to see the image without it. In the Photos app, open the photo, tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png), then tap Portrait to turn the effect on or off.

Your iPhone captures depth information when ![the Depth button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/25cdab1e14eff2b794ff28c2bccd2ba7.png) appears while taking photos in Photo mode, so you can apply the portrait effect later even if you didn’t use it when taking the original photo. See [Apply portr

metadata.title: Take portraits with your iPhone camera - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Take portraits with your iPhone camera - Apple Support]

S/25cdab1e14eff2b794ff28c2bccd2ba7.png) appears while taking photos in Photo mode, so you can apply the portrait effect later even if you didn’t use it when taking the original photo. See [Apply portrait effects to photos taken in Photo mode](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/edit-portrait-mode-photos-iph310a9a220/26/ios/26#ipha7919eb64).

On [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphbaefb01b6/26/ios/26), Night mode turns on when you take portraits in low-light situations with the wide (1x) lens. To learn more about Night mode, see [Take Night mode photos](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/take-night-mode-photos-iph1a3c5b4c3/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Take portraits with your iPhone camera - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Take portraits with your iPhone camera - Apple Support]

fect that keeps your subject—people, pets, or objects—sharp while creating a beautifully blurred foreground and background. You can also apply and adjust different lighting effects to your portraits.

## Adjust the background blur

Use the Depth Control slider to adjust the level of background blur in your portraits.

1. Open Camera ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/71ce3044f246f5f1f828b819f1c395b6.png) on your iPhone.

2. Swipe left to select [Portrait mode](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/camera-basics-iph263472f78/26/ios/26#iphac6618d52) or stay in Photo mode and tap ![the Depth Off button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/25cdab1e14eff2b794ff28c2bccd2ba7.png) on ( [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphde3d86c94/26/ios/26)).

3. Tap ![the Camera Controls button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8ed35ca4bcbec2cdd97804e0495a881b.png) or swipe up from Portrait mode at the bottom of the screen.

4. Tap Aperture, then drag the slider below the frame right or left to adjust the effect.

5. Tap ![the Camera Control Status button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6b95c3060c7ea35acdffad3d68c54277.png) to close the Depth Control slider.

On [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph345af6a5d/26/ios/26), you can also adjust the depth control using the Camera Control. See [Select and adjust the Camera Control settings](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-the-camera-control-iph0c397b154/26/ios/26#iphf10d97496).

After you capture a portrait, you can use the Depth Control slider in the Photos app to further adjust the background blur effect. See [Edit Portrait mode photos](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/edit-portrait-mode-photos-iph310a9a220/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Take portraits with your iPhone camera - Apple Support

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Take portraits with your iPhone camera - Apple Support]

## Adjust the Portrait Lighting

Virtually adjust the position and intensity of the Portrait Lighting to sharpen eyes or brighten and smooth facial features.

1. Open Camera ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/71ce3044f246f5f1f828b819f1c395b6.png) on your iPhone.

2. Swipe left to select [Portrait mode](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/camera-basics-iph263472f78/26/ios/26#iphac6618d52) or stay in Photo mode and tap ![the Depth Off button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/25cdab1e14eff2b794ff28c2bccd2ba7.png) on ( [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iphde3d86c94/26/ios/26)).

3. Drag ![the Portrait Lighting control](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3d400598bc5159bfe3fc68d86d0a0f2e.png) to choose a lighting effect.

4. Tap ![the Camera Controls button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8ed35ca4bcbec2cdd97804e0495a881b.png) or swipe up from Portrait mode at the bottom of the screen.

5. Tap Intensity, then drag the slider below the frame right or left to adjust the effect.

6. Tap ![the Dismiss Portrait Effect Intensity Control button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6b95c3060c7ea35acdffad3d68c54277.png) to close the Portrait Lighting slider.

After you capture a portrait, you can edit the Portrait Lighting levels in the Photos app. See [Edit Portrait mode photos](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/edit-portrait-mode-photos-iph310a9a220/26/ios/26).

**See also** [Edit Portrait mode photos on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/edit-portrait-mode-photos-iph310a9a220/26/ios/26) [Take Night mode photos with your iPhone camera](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/take-night-mode-photos-iph1a3c5b4c3/26/ios/26) [Take macro photos and videos with your iPhone camera](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/take-macro-photos-and-videos-iphfaacf2eb0/26/ios/26)

# Track your medications in Health on iPhone

You can track and manage the medications, vitamins, and supplements you take.

**Siri:** On [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph229706d23/26/ios/26), [ask Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/summon_siri/26/ios/26) something like, “Log my 6AM medications as taken” (not available in all languages or regions). [Learn how to use Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-on-and-activate-siri-iph83aad8922/26/ios/26).

![The Medications screen in Health showing the date and a log for medications.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/c5d7918ee80f53ba5ba3c1ad4ef77829.png)

_Note:_ Some features are not available in all countries or regions. The Medications feature is not a substitute for professional medical judgment. Additional information is available on the labels of your medications. Consult your healthcare provider prior to making any decisions related to your health.

metadata.title: Track your medications in Health on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Track your medications in Health on iPhone - Apple Support]

titute for professional medical judgment. Additional information is available on the labels of your medications. Consult your healthcare provider prior to making any decisions related to your health.

## Add and schedule a new medication

1. Go to the Health app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9778c09654aad90365a961606974a385.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3506e345e882236df240ce6ef039212b.png) at the bottom right, then tap Medications.

3. Tap Add a Medication (to start your list) or Add Medication (to add to your list).

4. To identify the medication, do one of the following:

   - _Type the name:_ Tap the text field, enter the name, then tap Add.

     In the U.S. only, suggestions appear as you begin typing. You can select a suggestion, or finish typing the name, then tap Add.

   - _Use the camera:_(U.S. only) Tap ![the Camera button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/0a48037583b372df7dc38ebcba6ec542.png) next to the text field, then follow the onscreen instructions.

     If a match isn’t found, tap Search by Name, then type the name (as described above).
5. Do the following:

   - _Choose the medication type:_ Tap an option, then tap Next.

   - _Choose the medication strength:_ Tap an option, then tap Next.

   - _Set a schedule:_ Follow the onscreen instructions to set the frequency and duration of your scheduled medication, then tap Next.

     Tap Change under “When will you take this?” to choose a different schedule type, like On a Cyclical Schedule, or Every Few Days.

   - _Choose the shape (optional):_ Tap an option, then tap Next.

   - _Choose the colors (optional):_ Tap an option, then tap Next.
6. When you’re finished, tap Done.

metadata.title: Track your medications in Health on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Track your medications in Health on iPhone - Apple Support]

Schedule, or Every Few Days.

   - _Choose the shape (optional):_ Tap an option, then tap Next.

   - _Choose the colors (optional):_ Tap an option, then tap Next.
6. When you’re finished, tap Done.

## Turn on Follow Up Reminders and Critical Alerts

If you set a medication schedule, you [receive notifications](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-and-respond-to-notifications-iph6534c01bc/26/ios/26) from the Health app reminding you to log the medication. You can receive follow-up reminders if a medication hasn’t been logged 30 minutes after the scheduled time.

1. Go to the Health app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9778c09654aad90365a961606974a385.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3506e345e882236df240ce6ef039212b.png) at the bottom right, then tap Medications.

3. Tap Options at the bottom of the screen, then turn on Follow Up Reminders.

When you turn on Follow Up Reminders, you can also turn on Critical Alerts for each of your medications. Critical Alerts appear on the Lock Screen and play a sound (even if a Focus is on or iPhone is muted).

4. Tap Critical Alerts, tap the button next to a medication, then tap Allow.

To turn off Critical Alerts, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Health > Notifications, then tap the button next to Critical Alerts.

metadata.title: Track your medications in Health on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Track your medications in Health on iPhone - Apple Support]

ps://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Apps > Health > Notifications, then tap the button next to Critical Alerts.

## Change the schedule for a medication or update other information

![The Medications screen in Health showing options to change the schedule for a medication.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/a34aaeb1639e3562d75e3d255b611a7c.png)

1. Go to the Health app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9778c09654aad90365a961606974a385.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3506e345e882236df240ce6ef039212b.png) at the bottom right, then tap Medications.

3. Scroll down to Your Medications, then do any of the following:

   - _Change the schedule for a medication:_ Tap a medication, scroll down to Schedule, tap Edit, then tap a field to change it.

   - _Change the icon, nickname, or notes for a medication:_ Tap a medication, scroll down to Details, tap Edit to change the icon or tap either of the text fields, then make your changes.

   - _Reorder your list:_ Tap Edit above the list of your medications, touch and hold ![the Reorder button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/705a0755aace2f1e01e2a1ffe986c224.png) for a medication, drag it to a new position, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

   - _Archive a medication:_ Swipe left on its name in the list of your medications, then tap Archive.

   - _Make an archived medication reappear in your list of medications:_ Tap Edit above the list of your medications, then tap ![the Add button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/7c24a87974c9f3d927f73dd5edc48c38.png) next to the medication name, then tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png). Any schedule you previously set is not preserved.

   - _Delete a medication:_ Tap a medication, scroll down, then tap Delete Medication.

   - _Change how you receive notifications about taking medications:_ See [View and respond to notifications](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-and-respond-to-notifications-iph6534c01bc/26/ios/26) and [Change notification settings](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-notification-settings-iph7c3d96bab/26/ios/26).

/guide/iphone/view-and-respond-to-notifications-iph6534c01bc/26/ios/26) and [Change notification settings](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-notification-settings-iph7c3d96bab/26/ios/26).

## Log when you take a medication

You can log a medication, scheduled or unscheduled, at any time. By default, your iPhone will remind you when it’s time to log your medications based on the schedule you’ve created in the Health app.

_Note:_ For your Health app data to sync across devices, make sure they’re signed in to the same Apple Account, are connected to the internet, and are updated to the latest OS version.

![The Medications screen in Health showing a log of medications.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/28e3f03f9e83689dce261f8dc9e4429e.png)

1. Go to the Health app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9778c09654aad90365a961606974a385.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3506e345e882236df240ce6ef039212b.png) at the bottom right, then tap Medications.

3. Select a day at the top of the screen.

4. Do one of the following:

   - _Log whether you took a scheduled medication:_ Tap the name of a medication in the list below Log, then below the medication, tap Taken or Skipped.

   - _Log an unscheduled medication:_ Tap As Needed Medications in the list below Log, then below the medication, tap Taken.
5. When you’re finished, tap Done.

**Tip:** When you receive a notification to log a scheduled medication, press and hold the notification, then choose an option.

To review your history of taking the medication, tap ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3506e345e882236df240ce6ef039212b.png) at the bottom right, tap Medications, then tap the medication (in the list of your medications).

## Share your medication list

1. Go to the Health app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9778c09654aad90365a961606974a385.png) on your iPhone.

2. Do one of the following:

   - _Share your list (or just specific medications) with a loved one:_ See [Share your data in Health](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/share-your-health-data-iph5ede58c3d/26/ios/26).

     **Tip:** If you add a new medication after you start sharing, add it to your shared list.

   - _Share your list as a PDF:_ Tap ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/3506e345e882236df240ce6ef039212b.png) at the bottom right, tap Medications, scroll down, tap Export PDF, tap ![the Share button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/748a151e2276ad5df4d03ec8d4506bf7.png), then choose an option.

**See also** [Learn more about your medications in Health on iPhone (U.S. only)](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/learn-more-about-your-medications-iph2fcefa8d6/26/ios/26) [View your data in Health on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-your-health-data-iphe3d379c32/26/ios/26)

# Trim video length, adjust speed, and edit audio on iPhone

In the Photos app, you can trim a video you recorded on your iPhone to change where it starts and stops. You can also adjust the playback speed, and change the portion of a video that appears in slow motion when you record in Slo-mo mode.

When you record a video with Spatial Audio, you can use Audio Mix to edit the sound in the Photos app (available on [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph5da84b7f8/26/ios/26)).

## Trim a video

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a video, then tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png).

3. Drag either end of the frame viewer below the video to adjust the start and stop times, then tap Done.

4. Tap Save Video to save only the trimmed video, or Save Video as New Clip to save both versions of the video.
![The Edit screen in the Photos app with a video playing in the center. In the frame viewer below the video, frames to include in the trimmed video are selected and outlined in yellow. On the bottom of the screen are the Video, Audio Mix, Adjust, Filters, and Crop buttons; the Video button is selected.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/d219779de7a63de68f26df7b37890d7a.png)

To undo edits after you save, open the video, tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png), then tap Revert.

_Note:_ A video saved as a new clip can’t be reverted to the original.

metadata.title: Trim video length, adjust speed, and edit audio on iPhone - Apple Support

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Trim video length, adjust speed, and edit audio on iPhone - Apple Support]

pple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png), then tap Revert.

_Note:_ A video saved as a new clip can’t be reverted to the original.

## Edit the video playback speed

You can slow down videos that were captured at a high frame rate for a dramatic effect.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a video, tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png), then tap Video.

3. Tap ![the Playback Speed button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/582c79b06ab384d1f07b1be35236eeb8.png), then tap a playback speed.

_Note:_ The playback options available depend on the format of the video you captured. The larger the format you capture, the more options are available.

## Edit the slow-motion section of a video shot in Slo-mo mode

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a video shot in Slo-mo mode, then tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png).

3. Drag the black vertical bars beneath the frame viewer to set where the video is played in slow motion.

metadata.title: Trim video length, adjust speed, and edit audio on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Trim video length, adjust speed, and edit audio on iPhone - Apple Support]

F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png).

3. Drag the black vertical bars beneath the frame viewer to set where the video is played in slow motion.

## Edit sound with Audio Mix

When you record a video with iPhone 16 or later, you can [choose between three recording options](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-sound-recording-options-iph31c1ca6c7/26/ios/26): Spatial Audio, Studio, and Mono. When you record with Spatial Audio, you can use Audio Mix to customize the sound in your video.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6653ad847aeffaa071397f0b0db8c03d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a video, then tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png).

3. Tap Audio Mix, then choose one of the following:

   - _Standard:_ Play the original audio you recorded.

   - _In-Frame:_ Reduce sounds and voices from sources not visible in the video frame.

   - _Studio:_ Reduce background sounds and reverb, to make it sound more like you’re recording in a professional studio.

   - _Cinematic:_ Put all the voices onto a front-facing track and leave environmental noises in surround, like the audio in movies.
4. Drag the slider below the mix options to increase or decrease the effect and background noise levels.

5. Tap Done.

**See also** [Edit photos and videos on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/edit-photos-and-videos-iphb08064d57/26/ios/26) [Edit Cinematic mode videos on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/edit-cinematic-mode-videos-iph5e602f6d6/26/ios/26) [Undo and revert photo edits on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/undo-and-revert-photo-edits-iph2413db0ab/26/ios/26)

# Use AirPods with iPhone

After you pair AirPods with iPhone, you can use AirPods to listen to audio playing on iPhone, make and answer calls, listen and respond to messages, hear reminders when you need them, listen to conversations more easily in noisy environments, and more.

## Pair AirPods with your iPhone

1. On iPhone, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Bluetooth, then turn on Bluetooth.

2. Go to the Home Screen on your iPhone.

3. Do one of the following:

   - _AirPods (1, 2, 3, and 4) and AirPods Pro:_ Open the case with your AirPods inside, then hold it next to your iPhone.

   - _AirPods Max:_ Take your AirPods Max out of the Smart Case, then hold AirPods Max next to your iPhone.
4. Follow the onscreen instructions, then tap Done.

_Note:_ If onscreen instructions for pairing your AirPods Max don’t appear, go to Settings > Bluetooth, then select your AirPods Max. If the status light doesn’t flash white on AirPods Max, press and hold the noise control button until it does.

Your AirPods are automatically paired with all of your supported devices where you’re [signed in to your Apple Account](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph39beaede9/26/ios/26) (iOS 10, iPadOS 13, macOS 10.12, watchOS 3, or later required).

_Note:_ If you can’t pair your AirPods, see the Apple Support article [If your AirPods won’t connect](https://support.apple.com/118576).

# Use Apple Intelligence in Mail on iPhone

Apple Intelligence\* makes it easier than ever to stay on top of your email. In addition to [using Writing Tools](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/find-the-right-words-with-writing-tools-iph6f08da1d2/26/ios/26) to craft your outgoing messages, you can use Priority Messages to quickly view your most urgent emails, like a same-day dinner invitation or boarding pass. Smart Reply helps you compose a fast email response to make sure you covered everything. You can also view summaries that automatically appear in your inbox, and generate summaries of complex emails and long email threads to view all the pertinent details.

![An iPhone screen showing an email. A summary of the email appears at the top of the screen.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f570d145daf366e11a45454d34eb83cd.png)

_Note:_ Apple Intelligence is not available on all [iPhone models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph275f4d617/26/ios/26) or in all languages or regions.\*\* To access the most recent available features, make sure you’re using the [latest version of iOS](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/update_os/26/ios/26) and have [Apple Intelligence turned on](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph3212e4900/26/ios/26).

## View Priority Messages in your inbox

1. Go to the Mail app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/352a64cda8fca90aa29ad6f4f6185ee5.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap your inbox, tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8a1f0a8df86eb1c80021954b0f092e6e.png), then tap Show Priority.

Time-sensitive emails appear at the top of your inbox.

_Note:_ Priority Messages appear in the Primary category or in List View.

metadata.title: Use Apple Intelligence in Mail on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Apple Intelligence in Mail on iPhone - Apple Support]

en_US/8a1f0a8df86eb1c80021954b0f092e6e.png), then tap Show Priority.

Time-sensitive emails appear at the top of your inbox.

_Note:_ Priority Messages appear in the Primary category or in List View.

## Use Smart Reply in Mail

1. Go to the Mail app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/352a64cda8fca90aa29ad6f4f6185ee5.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap an email, tap ![the Reply button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/54b80d14295606b13cd15c37e62e32de.png), then tap Reply.

3. In the keyboard, tap a suggested reply.

Apple Intelligence drafts a reply in the email. To refine your reply, see [Use Writing Tools with Apple Intelligence on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/find-the-right-words-with-writing-tools-iph6f08da1d2/26/ios/26).

4. When you’re ready to send your reply, tap ![the Send button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f5dabff8f401af1580540b6ab7aedcb8.png).

## Summarize your email

Apple Intelligence can help you summarize a complex email, or catch up on a long email thread in seconds.

1. Go to the Mail app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/352a64cda8fca90aa29ad6f4f6185ee5.png) on your iPhone.

A short summary automatically appears under each email in your inbox.

2. Tap an email, then tap Summarize.

A summary appears at the top of the screen. If the message is part of an email thread, the contents of the thread are summarized.

metadata.title: Use Apple Intelligence in Mail on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Apple Intelligence in Mail on iPhone - Apple Support]

nder each email in your inbox.

2. Tap an email, then tap Summarize.

A summary appears at the top of the screen. If the message is part of an email thread, the contents of the thread are summarized.

## Turn email summary previews on or off

When you turn on Apple Intelligence, summaries automatically appear under your emails. You can turn off summaries if you want to see the first lines of the most recent message instead.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Apps, then tap Mail.

3. Turn Summarize Message Previews on or off.

**See also** [Use Writing Tools with Apple Intelligence on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/find-the-right-words-with-writing-tools-iph6f08da1d2/26/ios/26) [Check your email in Mail on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/check-your-email-iph461684497/26/ios/26) [Automatically categorize incoming emails in Mail on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-categories-iphfe4a36baf/26/ios/26)

# Use Apple Intelligence in Photos on iPhone

With Apple Intelligence,\* you can find just about anything in the photos and videos you capture, create memory movies of stories you want to see, and focus on the most important parts of your photos by removing distracting objects.

![An iPhone showing photos in the Photos app. A search bar with a description appears at the bottom of the screen.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ddae5be834de7d0634d3bd0c898fb127.png)

_Note:_ Apple Intelligence is not available on all [iPhone models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph275f4d617/26/ios/26) or in all languages or regions.\*\* To access the most recent available features, make sure you’re using the [latest version of iOS](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/update_os/26/ios/26) and have [Apple Intelligence turned on](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph3212e4900/26/ios/26).

metadata.title: Use Apple Intelligence in Photos on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Apple Intelligence in Photos on iPhone - Apple Support]

[latest version of iOS](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/update_os/26/ios/26) and have [Apple Intelligence turned on](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph3212e4900/26/ios/26).

## Search for specific photos and videos with Apple Intelligence

Apple Intelligence makes it even easier to find a specific photo or a key moment in a video—just describe what you want to find.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/caecf4fd3e5c79b7fa0d25f92ad0dc0f.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Search button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/bb6bd114806074af6a834132111bdb2b.png), then enter a description using natural language—“Maya skateboarding in a tie-dye shirt,” or “Sadie cartwheeling on the grass,” for example.

_Note:_ For more information on naming people and pets in photos, see [Find and name people and pets in Photos](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/find-and-name-people-and-pets-iph9c7ee918c/26/ios/26).

3. Do any of the following:

   - _See all search results:_ Tap See All.

   - _Select specific search results:_ Tap Select, then select the photos and videos you want to share or add to an album.

   - _Sort search results:_ Tap ![the Sort and Filter button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e4252df5eb17cf094c9e5c4924122f0b.png), then tap Sort by Recently Added or Sort by Date Captured.

   - _Filter search results:_ Tap ![the Sort and Filter button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/e4252df5eb17cf094c9e5c4924122f0b.png), tap Filter, then tap an option—like Favorites or Edited.
4. When you’re finished, tap ![the Close button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8632cceede5d336ff7e8e596c149eec1.png).

metadata.title: Use Apple Intelligence in Photos on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Apple Intelligence in Photos on iPhone - Apple Support]

like Favorites or Edited.
4. When you’re finished, tap ![the Close button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8632cceede5d336ff7e8e596c149eec1.png).

## Create a memory movie

You can create a custom memory movie of a story you want to see, right in Photos. Just type a description, and Apple Intelligence finds the best photos and videos, crafts a storyline with unique chapters and a narrative arc, and sets it to music.

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/caecf4fd3e5c79b7fa0d25f92ad0dc0f.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Collections, tap Memories, then tap Create.

3. Enter a description of the movie you want to create from your library, then tap Done.

## Remove distractions from your photos

You can use the Clean Up tool to remove distracting objects in the background of a photo.

_Note:_ Clean Up is not available in all languages or regions. For more information, see the Apple Support article [Requirements to use Clean Up in Photos](https://support.apple.com/121429).

1. Go to the Photos app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/caecf4fd3e5c79b7fa0d25f92ad0dc0f.png) on your iPhone.

2. Open a photo, tap ![the Edit button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/2168728be5f88df306e409efc69488a0.png), then tap Clean Up.

_Note:_ After you tap Clean Up, some items may be highlighted automatically so you can quickly tap to remove them.

3. Tap, brush, or circle what you want to remove.

Use two fingers to pinch to zoom or pan the image.

_Note:_ If you brush over a person’s face, the face may become blurred with a pixelated effect.

4. When you’re finished, tap Done.

# Use AssistiveTouch on iPhone

With AssistiveTouch on iPhone, you can adapt the touchscreen to ﬁt your needs. If certain gestures don’t work for you, replace them with a tap or other gesture, or use a simple sound. You can also use AssistiveTouch when you need to press iPhone buttons. You can use AssistiveTouch without any accessory or with a compatible adaptive accessory (such as a joystick).

![An iPhone with the AssistiveTouch menu visible, showing controls for Notification Center, Device, Control Center, Home, Gestures, and Custom.](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/f7245da4d3523960cb78cc6fd0f1b2d7.png)

You can use AssistiveTouch to perform actions such as the following:

- Go to the Home Screen

- Double-tap

- Perform multifinger gestures

- Perform scroll gestures

- Activate Siri

- Access Control Center, notifications, the Lock Screen, or the App Switcher

- Adjust the volume on iPhone

- Shake iPhone

- Take a screenshot

- Use 3D Touch (on [supported iPhone models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph945ccc462/18.0/ios/18.0))

- Use Apple Pay

- Use Emergency SOS

- Speak screen

- Adjust dwell settings

- Restart iPhone

metadata.title: Use AssistiveTouch on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use AssistiveTouch on iPhone - Apple Support]

on [supported iPhone models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph945ccc462/18.0/ios/18.0))

- Use Apple Pay

- Use Emergency SOS

- Speak screen

- Adjust dwell settings

- Restart iPhone

## Set up AssistiveTouch

**Siri:** Say something like: “Turn on AssistiveTouch” or “Turn off AssistiveTouch.” [Learn how to use Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-siri-iph83aad8922/18.0/ios/18.0).

Or do the following:

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Touch > AssistiveTouch.

2. Turn on AssistiveTouch. The AssistiveTouch menu button ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/a5428eecd6c9cc0547febfe1534eb144.png) appears on the screen.

3. To customize AssistiveTouch, tap any of the following:

   - _Customize Top Level Menu:_ Tap an icon to change its action. Tap ![the Decrement button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/43215f37ef59b6605cde4d5da99f6161.png) or ![the Increment button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/1c855795afee486e11acd25121b275da.png) to change the number of icons in the menu. The menu can have up to eight icons.

   - _Single-Tap, Double-Tap, Long Press, or 3D Touch:_ Assign custom actions that run when you interact with the menu button. 3D Touch is available only on [supported iPhone models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph945ccc462/18.0/ios/18.0).

   - _Create New Gesture:_ Add your favorite gestures.

   - _Idle Opacity:_ Adjust the visibility of the menu button when not in use.

   - _Confirm with AssistiveTouch:_ On an [iPhone with Face ID](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph38269d9c5/18.0/ios/18.0), confirm payments with Face ID by using AssistiveTouch instead of double-clicking the side button.

**Tip:** To quickly turn AssistiveTouch on or off, you can [set up the Accessibility Shortcut](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/quickly-turn-accessibility-features-on-or-off-iph3e2e31a5/18.0/ios/18.0#iphc2d3bad8e), then triple-click the side or Home button.

metadata.title: Use AssistiveTouch on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use AssistiveTouch on iPhone - Apple Support]

up the Accessibility Shortcut](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/quickly-turn-accessibility-features-on-or-off-iph3e2e31a5/18.0/ios/18.0#iphc2d3bad8e), then triple-click the side or Home button.

## Use AssistiveTouch

When AssistiveTouch is on, tap the AssistiveTouch menu button ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/a5428eecd6c9cc0547febfe1534eb144.png), then choose an action or gesture.

To return to the previous menu, tap the arrow in the center of the menu. To exit the menu without performing a gesture, tap anywhere outside the menu.

To move the AssistiveTouch menu button, drag it to a new location on the screen.

For a multifinger gesture, do the following:

- _Pinch and Rotate:_ Tap Custom, then tap Pinch and Rotate. When the two pinch circles appear, touch anywhere on the screen to move the pinch circles, then drag them in or out to perform a pinch or rotate gesture. When you finish, tap the menu button.

- _Multifinger swipe or drag:_ Tap Device > More > Gestures, then tap the number of digits needed for the gesture. When the circles appear on the screen, swipe or drag in the direction required by the gesture. When you finish, tap the menu button.

## Control iPhone with a pointer device

You can connect Bluetooth® and USB assistive pointer devices, such as trackpads, game controllers, and mouse devices.

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Touch > AssistiveTouch.

2. Turn on AssistiveTouch.

3. Below Pointer Devices, tap any of the following:

   - _Devices:_ Pair or unpair devices and customize buttons.

   - _Mouse Keys:_ Allow the AssistiveTouch pointer to be controlled using the keyboard.

   - _Pointer Style:_ Adjust the size, color, and auto-hide settings.

   - _Show Onscreen Keyboard:_ Display the onscreen keyboard.

   - _Sound on Click:_ Play a sound when the pointer device is clicked.

   - _Always Show Menu:_ Show the AssistiveTouch menu when a pointer device is connected.

   - _Tracking Sensitivity:_ Adjust how quickly the pointer moves when you drag the mouse or your finger on the trackpad.

You can also [change the appearance of the pointer](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/adjust-pointer-settings-iphec6e1e60b/18.0/ios/18.0).

metadata.title: Use AssistiveTouch on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use AssistiveTouch on iPhone - Apple Support]

hen you drag the mouse or your finger on the trackpad.

You can also [change the appearance of the pointer](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/adjust-pointer-settings-iphec6e1e60b/18.0/ios/18.0).

## Set up Dwell Control

iPhone performs a selected action when you hold the cursor still on a screen element or an area of the screen.

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Touch > AssistiveTouch, then turn on Dwell Control.

2. Adjust any of the following:

   - _Fallback Action:_ Turn on to revert the dwell action to the selected fallback action after performing an operation.

   - _Movement Tolerance:_ Adjust the distance the cursor can move while dwelling on an item.

   - _Hot Corners:_ Perform a selected action—such as take a screenshot, open Control Center, activate Siri, scroll, or use a shortcut—when the cursor dwells in a corner of the screen.

   - _Time needed to initiate a dwell action:_ Tap ![the Decrement button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/43215f37ef59b6605cde4d5da99f6161.png) or ![the Increment button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/1c855795afee486e11acd25121b275da.png).

## Use simple sounds to perform gestures

With AssistiveTouch, you can have iPhone perform a gesture or other action when you make a simple sound, such as a mouth pop or an S-sound.

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Touch > AssistiveTouch, then turn AssistiveTouch on.

2. Tap Sound Actions, then tap a sound.

3. Select the gesture or other action you want performed when you make the sound.

4. To perform the gesture or action, just make the sound.

metadata.title: Use AssistiveTouch on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use AssistiveTouch on iPhone - Apple Support]

sistiveTouch on.

2. Tap Sound Actions, then tap a sound.

3. Select the gesture or other action you want performed when you make the sound.

4. To perform the gesture or action, just make the sound.

## Create custom gestures

You can add gestures you use often (such as touch and hold or two-finger rotation) to the AssistiveTouch menu. You can even create several gestures with, for example, different degrees of rotation.

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Touch > AssistiveTouch > Create New Gesture.

2. Perform your gesture on the recording screen. For example:

   - _Touch-and-hold gesture:_ Touch and hold your finger in one spot until the recording progress bar reaches halfway, then lift your finger. Be careful not to move your finger while recording, or the gesture will be recorded as a drag.

   - _Two-finger rotation gesture:_ Rotate two fingers on the iPhone screen around a point between them. (You can do this with a single finger or stylus—just create each arc separately, one after the other.)

If you record a sequence of taps or drag gestures, they’re all played back at the same time. For example, using one finger or a stylus to record four separate, sequential taps at four locations on the screen creates a simultaneous four-finger tap.

3. If your gesture doesn’t turn out quite right, tap Cancel, then try again.

4. When you’re satisfied with your gesture, tap Save, then name the gesture.

To use your custom gesture, tap the AssistiveTouch menu button ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/a5428eecd6c9cc0547febfe1534eb144.png), tap Custom, then choose the gesture. When the blue circles representing your gesture appear, drag them to where you want to use the gesture, then release.

**See also** [Accessibility features for mobility on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/overview-accessibility-features-mobility-iph76d37ce7d/18.0/ios/18.0) [Adjust how iPhone responds to your touch](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/adjust-how-iphone-responds-to-your-touch-iph77bcdd132/18.0/ios/18.0)

# Use contacts from the Phone app on iPhone

In the Phone app on iPhone, you can call contacts and add recent callers to the Contacts app.

## Add a contact to Favorites

You can add VIP contacts to your Favorites list for quick dialing.

1. Go to the Contacts app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/16b966e440ec5fb9278ce6235caabc74.png) on your iPhone.

2. Select a contact, scroll down, then tap Add to Favorites.

Calls from these contacts bypass Do Not Disturb (see [Turn on or schedule a Focus](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/turn-a-focus-on-or-off-iph5c3f5b77b/26/ios/26)).

## Add a recent caller to Contacts

1. Go to the Phone app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/4651af24ad17cb6ac594c96c6641ba6f.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap a number in the Calls list.

3. Tap Create New Contact, then enter the contact’s information.

To add a recent caller to an existing contact, tap Add to Existing Contact, then choose a contact.

metadata.title: Use contacts from the Phone app on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use contacts from the Phone app on iPhone - Apple Support]

. Tap a number in the Calls list.

3. Tap Create New Contact, then enter the contact’s information.

To add a recent caller to an existing contact, tap Add to Existing Contact, then choose a contact.

## Automate dialing an extension or passcode

If the number you’re calling requires dialing an extension, iPhone can enter it for you. When editing a contact’s phone number, tap ![the Symbols button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/01291458f61f4700d8e72db61ac00121.png), then do any of the following:

- Tap Pause to enter a two-second pause (a two-second pause is represented as a comma in the phone number).

- Tap Wait to stop dialing until you tap Dial again (wait-to-dial is represented as a semicolon in the phone number).

**See also** [Add and use contact information on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-and-use-contact-information-iph3e0ca2db/26/ios/26)

# Use Dual SIM on iPhone

Here are some of the many ways you can use Dual SIM:

- Use one number for business and another number for personal calls.

- Add a local data plan when you travel to another country or region.

- Have separate voice and data plans.

_Note:_ To use two different carriers, your iPhone must be unlocked. See the Apple Support article [How to unlock your iPhone for use with a different carrier](https://support.apple.com/109316).

You can set up Dual SIM with the following:

- One physical SIM and one eSIM; supported on iPhone SE (2nd generation), iPhone XR, iPhone XS, iPhone 11, iPhone 12, iPhone 13, and iPhone 14, and later

- Two eSIMs; supported on iPhone SE (3rd generation), iPhone 13, and later

_Note:_ Physical SIM isn’t supported on all models. eSIM isn’t available in all countries or regions.

metadata.title: Use Dual SIM on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Dual SIM on iPhone - Apple Support]

## Set up Dual SIM

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/f6b5581fdf38013a42e265720f20b597.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Cellular, then make sure you have at least two lines (below SIMs). To add a line, see [Set up cellular service](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-cellular-service-iph3f11fba92/26/ios/26).

3. Turn on two lines—tap a line, then tap Turn On this Line.

You can also change settings such as Cellular Plan Label, Wi-Fi Calling (if available from your carrier), Calls on Other Devices, or SIM PIN. The label appears in Phone, Messages, and Contacts.

4. Choose the default line for cellular data—tap Cellular Data, then tap a line. To use either line depending on coverage and availability, turn on Allow Cellular Data Switching.

You may incur roaming charges if Data Roaming is on and you’re outside the country or region covered by the carrier’s network.

5. Choose the default line for voice calls—tap Default Voice Line, then tap a line.

When using Dual SIM, note the following:

- Wi-Fi Calling must be turned on for a line to enable that line to receive calls while the other line is in use for a call. If you receive a call on one line while the other is in use for a call, and no Wi-Fi connection is available, iPhone uses the cellular data of the line that is in use for the call to receive the other line’s call. Charges may apply. The line that is in use for the call must be permitted for data use in your Cellular Data settings (either as the default line, or as the non-default line with Allow Cellular Data Switching turned on) to receive the other line’s call.

- If you don’t turn on Wi-Fi Calling for a line, any incoming phone calls on that line (including calls from emergency services) go directly to voicemail (if available from your carrier) when the other line is in use; you won’t receive missed call notifications.

If you set up conditional call forwarding (if available from your carrier) from one line to another when a line is busy or not in service, the calls don’t go to voicemail; contact your carrier for setup information.

- If you make a phone call from another device, such as your Mac, by relaying it through your iPhone with Dual SIM, the call is made using your default voice line.

- If you start an SMS/MMS Messages conversation using one line, you can’t switch the conversation to your other line; you need to delete the conversation and start a ne

metadata.title: Use Dual SIM on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Dual SIM on iPhone - Apple Support]

using your default voice line.

- If you start an SMS/MMS Messages conversation using one line, you can’t switch the conversation to your other line; you need to delete the conversation and start a new conversation using the other line. Also, you may incur additional charges if you send SMS/MMS attachments on the line that’s not selected for cellular data.

- Instant Hotspot and Personal Hotspot use the line selected for cellular data.

**See also** [Connect to a cellular network if Wi-Fi isn’t available](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/connect-to-the-internet-iphd1cf4268/26/ios/26#iphbba3b6ccd) [View or change cellular data settings on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/view-or-change-cellular-data-settings-iph3dd5f213/26/ios/26)

# Use Photographic Styles with your iPhone camera

On [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph80f8f71ff/26/ios/26), Photographic Styles intelligently adjust specific colors in different parts of your photos to create a customized look. The style you select applies automatically to all photos you take, but you can make adjustments in Camera or edits later in the Photos app.

_Note:_ On [models that support latest generation Photographic Styles](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph2c22a8a5e/26/ios/26), Camera needs to capture photos with High Efficiency image capture to edit Photographic Styles later in Photos. To turn on High Efficiency, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Camera > Formats, then tap High Efficiency.

metadata.title: Use Photographic Styles with your iPhone camera - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Photographic Styles with your iPhone camera - Apple Support]

ficiency, go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Camera > Formats, then tap High Efficiency.

## Select a Photographic Style

Select a Photographic Style to change the overall mood before you take a photo. You can also fine-tune its tone, color, and intensity.

1. Open Camera ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/71ce3044f246f5f1f828b819f1c395b6.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Camera Controls button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8ed35ca4bcbec2cdd97804e0495a881b.png) or swipe up from the camera modes at the bottom of the screen.

3. Tap Styles, then swipe left or right to preview the available styles.

4. To customize the style, do any of the following (depending on your iPhone model):

   - Drag your finger on ![Camera Control Status](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/55392a1c35be33a75747e4568b0c831b.png) to simultaneously adjust the tone and color.

   - Drag the slider to control how strongly the style affects your photos.

   - Drag the Tone and Warmth sliders to control how strongly the style affects your photos.

   - Tap ![the Reset button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/b286b5723904022212d1b07955ddfbc1.png) to return to default values.
5. Tap ![the Dismiss Styles Control button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/6b95c3060c7ea35acdffad3d68c54277.png) to close the Style menu.

To reset or change the style you selected, follow the instructions above, then select Standard, or choose a different style.

After you take a photo, you can further refine the Photographic Style values in the Photos app. See [Edit the Photographic Style of a photo](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/edit-photos-and-videos-iphb08064d57/26/ios/26#iphc0cdae33a).

metadata.title: Use Photographic Styles with your iPhone camera - Apple Support

[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Photographic Styles with your iPhone camera - Apple Support]

## Remove or reset a Photographic Style

You can remove your Photographic Style or set a new one.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Camera, tap Photographic Styles, then select “Use Standard” or choose a different style.

# Use Siri on iPhone

Get everyday tasks done with just your voice. Use Siri to translate a phrase, set an alarm, find a location, report on the weather, get summarized knowledge from the web, and more.

![An iPhone screen. Near the top of the screen, a notification from the Clock app shows that an alarm is turned on for 8:00 a.m. An icon at the bottom of the screen indicates that Siri is listening.](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/ca16a21a630ea68c257e199710cd52da.png)

## Set up Siri

If you didn’t set up Siri when you first set up your iPhone, do the following:

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Siri (or Apple Intelligence & Siri), then tap Talk to Siri.

2. Choose “Hey Siri” or “Siri” (if you have that option).

_Note:_ The option to say just “Siri” is available on [supported iPhone models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph3e7bd872f/18.0/ios/18.0) in some languages and regions.

To change additional Siri settings, see [Change Siri settings on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-siri-settings-iphc28624b81abc/18.0/ios/18.0).

metadata.title: Use Siri on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Siri on iPhone - Apple Support]

0) in some languages and regions.

To change additional Siri settings, see [Change Siri settings on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-siri-settings-iphc28624b81abc/18.0/ios/18.0).

## Activate Siri with your voice

When you activate Siri with your voice, Siri responds out loud.

Say “Siri” or “Hey Siri,” then ask a question or make a request.

For example, say something like “Hey Siri, what’s the weather for today?” or “Siri, set an alarm for 8 a.m.”

**Tip:** To prevent iPhone from responding to “Siri” or “Hey Siri,” place your iPhone face down, or go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Siri (or Apple Intelligence & Siri), tap “Listen for,” then choose Off.

You can also activate Siri with your voice while you’re wearing supported AirPods. See [Set up Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/airpods/devc2c0f438a) in the AirPods User Guide.

## Activate Siri with a button

When you activate Siri with a button, Siri responds silently when iPhone is in [silent mode](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/silence-iphone-iph81c7fd7d1/18.0/ios/18.0#iphcc98fdd04). When silent mode is off, Siri responds out loud. To change this behavior, see [Change how Siri responds](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-siri-settings-iphc28624b81abc/18.0/ios/18.0#iph6c3b97280).

1. Do one of the following:

   - _On an iPhone with Face ID:_ Press and hold the side button.

   - _On an iPhone with a Home button:_ Press and hold the Home button.

   - _With EarPods:_ Press and hold the center or call button.

   - _With CarPlay:_ Press and hold the voice command button on the steering wheel, or touch and hold the Home button on the CarPlay Home Screen. (See [Use Siri to Control CarPlay](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-siri-iph0aa8c80e6/18.0/ios/18.0).)

   - _With Siri Eyes Free:_ Press and hold the voice command button on your steering wheel.
2. Ask a question or make a request.

For example, say something like “What’s 18 percent of 225?” or “Set the timer for 3 minutes.”

You can also activate Siri with a touch on supported AirPods. See [Set up Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/airpods/devc2c0f438a) in the AirPods User Guide.

metadata.title: Use Siri on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Siri on iPhone - Apple Support]

5?” or “Set the timer for 3 minutes.”

You can also activate Siri with a touch on supported AirPods. See [Set up Siri](https://support.apple.com/guide/airpods/devc2c0f438a) in the AirPods User Guide.

## Make back-to-back requests

After you activate Siri, you can make follow-up requests without reactivating it. (Available on [supported iPhone models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph2dad477f9/18.0/ios/18.0) in some countries or regions.)

1. Activate Siri, then make a request.

2. Immediately after you make the request, make another one.

For example, say “Hey Siri, what’s the weather like in San Francisco? What about Cupertino?”

To override a request, interrupt Siri while Siri is speaking.

## Make a correction if Siri misunderstands you

- _While making back-to-back requests:_ Repeat your request in a different way.

- _If you aren’t making back-to-back requests:_ Tap ![the Listen button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/a0156eb1792fb9e10831999f3ad8343c.png), then repeat your request in a different way.

- _Spell out part of your request:_ Tap ![the Listen button](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/a0156eb1792fb9e10831999f3ad8343c.png), then repeat your request by spelling out any words that Siri didn’t understand. For example, say “Call,” then spell the person’s name.

- _Change a message before sending it:_ Say “Change it.”

- _Edit your request with text:_ If you see your request onscreen, you can edit it. Tap the request, then use the onscreen keyboard.

**Tip:** To correct your request without reactivating Siri, make the corrected request immediately after the original one. (Available on supported iPhone models in some countries and regions.)

## Type instead of speaking to Siri

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/683A54F5227CBBDB98051B96/683A54FC545676DF600F97D4/en_US/88b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Siri, then turn on Type to Siri.

2. To type a request, activate Siri, then use the keyboard and text field to ask a question or make a request.

metadata.title: Use Siri on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Siri on iPhone - Apple Support]

b2400e45bcf521514b7252cbb2d959.png) \> Accessibility > Siri, then turn on Type to Siri.

2. To type a request, activate Siri, then use the keyboard and text field to ask a question or make a request.

## More about Siri

Siri is designed to protect your information and to let you choose what you share. To learn more, see the [Siri, Dictation & Privacy website](https://www.apple.com/legal/privacy/data/en/ask-siri-dictation) and the [Improve Siri and Dictation & Privacy website](https://www.apple.com/legal/privacy/data/en/improve-siri-dictation).

For some requests, iPhone must be [connected to the internet](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/connect-to-the-internet-iphd1cf4268/18.0/ios/18.0). Cellular charges may apply.

If Siri doesn’t work as expected on your iPhone, see the Apple Support article [If Siri isn’t working on your iPhone](https://support.apple.com/105037).

**See also** [Find out what Siri can do on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/find-out-what-siri-can-do-ipha48873ed6/18.0/ios/18.0) [Change Siri accessibility settings on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/change-siri-accessibility-settings-iphaff1d606/18.0/ios/18.0)

# Use Smart Lists in Reminders on iPhone

In the Reminders app, you can easily filter items across all your lists using Smart Lists. You can create custom Smart Lists to automatically include items filtered by tags, dates, times, locations, flags, and priority. You can choose more than one tag (such as #gardening and #errands) and combine them with other filters.

![The Flagged Smart List in the Reminders app, which automatically collects all reminders marked with a flag into a single, consolidated list.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/0c74e92cdefa0bb4680fab0513ca1dff.png)

_Note:_ All Reminders features described in this guide are available when you use [updated reminders in iCloud](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/add-or-remove-accounts-iph8739025dd/26/ios/26#iph8bbc4e260). Some features aren’t available when using other accounts.

## Automatically organize items using the default Smart Lists

1. Go to the Reminders app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ec6119fc5bf93b83f78120434ec8118d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/aad05ec99d91fb0497859fc9c0c181fb.png), then tap Edit Lists.

3. Turn on or off the following Smart Lists:

   - _Today:_ Items scheduled for today and overdue items

   - _Scheduled:_ Items scheduled by date or time

   - _All:_ All items across all your lists

   - _Flagged:_ Items with a flag

   - _Completed:_ Items with a checkmark

   - _Assigned to Me:_ Items assigned to you in shared lists

   - _Groceries:_ Grocery items

   - _Siri Suggestions:_ Suggested items detected in Mail and Messages

metadata.title: Use Smart Lists in Reminders on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Smart Lists in Reminders on iPhone - Apple Support]

mpleted:_ Items with a checkmark

   - _Assigned to Me:_ Items assigned to you in shared lists

   - _Groceries:_ Grocery items

   - _Siri Suggestions:_ Suggested items detected in Mail and Messages

## Make a custom Smart List

1. Go to the Reminders app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ec6119fc5bf93b83f78120434ec8118d.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap ![the Add List button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/d9e8f0f8e558a6f1ee445c2e1b20e088.png).

3. Enter a name for the list, tap List Type, then choose Smart List.

4. Tap Edit Filters, choose one or more filters, then choose to include items matching any or all of the selected filters. You can filter by tags, dates, locations, and more. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

5. Choose a color and symbol for the list (optional).

6. Tap ![the Done button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/8cf38aa668e293079ba389088a78ec31.png).

## Convert a list to a Smart List

When you convert a list, its items are moved to the top level of the default list and tagged with the name of the Smart List.

_Note:_ You can’t convert a shared list.

1. Go to the Reminders app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/ec6119fc5bf93b83f78120434ec8118d.png) on your iPhone.

2. View the list you want to convert.

3. Tap ![the More button](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/aad05ec99d91fb0497859fc9c0c181fb.png), tap Show List Info, scroll to the bottom, then tap Convert to Smart List.

**See also** [Tag items in Reminders on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/tag-items-iph474a6685c/26/ios/26) [Organize multiple lists in Reminders on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/organize-multiple-lists-iph2c6cf708e/26/ios/26)

# Use Stolen Device Protection on iPhone

You can use Stolen Device Protection to protect against the rare instance when someone has stolen your iPhone and knows your passcode. Stolen Device Protection prevents the person from performing critical device and Apple Account operations (like changing your device passcode or Apple Account password). It does this by requiring biometric authentication with Face ID or Touch ID. There is no passcode to fall back on.

When Stolen Device Protection is turned on, more sensitive operations require a Security Delay: a successful Face ID or Touch ID, an hour wait, then an additional successful biometric authentication. Security Delay helps prevent someone from making changes to settings that can lock you out of your iPhone or Apple Account. These measures help protect your device and account, and give you more time to [turn on Lost Mode using the Find My app](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/mark-a-device-as-lost-iph7cc193cfc/26/ios/26) or [find devices on iCloud.com](https://www.icloud.com/find).

## Turn on Stolen Device Protection

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png), then do one of the following:

   - _On an iPhone with Face ID:_ Tap Face ID & Passcode, then enter your passcode.

   - _On an iPhone with a Home button:_ Tap Touch ID & Passcode, then enter your passcode.
2. Scroll down and tap Stolen Device Protection.

_Note:_ If you don’t see Stolen Device Protection, you may need to [set up Face ID](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-face-id-iph6d162927a/26/ios/26) or [Touch ID](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/set-up-touch-id-iph672384a0b/26/ios/26) first.

3. Turn on Stolen Device Protection.

4. Do one of the following:

   - _Use Stolen Device Protection when you’re away from home or work:_ Tap Away from Familiar Locations.

   - _Use Stolen Device Protection everywhere:_ Tap Always.

metadata.title: Use Stolen Device Protection on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use Stolen Device Protection on iPhone - Apple Support]

n.

4. Do one of the following:

   - _Use Stolen Device Protection when you’re away from home or work:_ Tap Away from Familiar Locations.

   - _Use Stolen Device Protection everywhere:_ Tap Always.

## Turn off Stolen Device Protection

1. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png), then do one of the following:

   - _On an iPhone with Face ID:_ Tap Face ID & Passcode, then enter your passcode.

   - _On an iPhone with a Home button:_ Tap Touch ID & Passcode, then enter your passcode.
2. Scroll down and tap Stolen Device Protection.

3. Turn off Stolen Device Protection.

If you have Stolen Device Protection set to Away from Familiar Locations and you’re not at home or work, or you have Stolen Device Protection set to Always, a Security Delay is required to turn off Stolen Device Protection. Tap Start Security Delay, then follow the onscreen instructions.

For more detailed information about Stolen Device Protection, see the Apple Support article [About Stolen Device Protection for iPhone](https://support.apple.com/120340).

**See also** [Sign in and manage your Apple Account on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/sign-in-and-manage-your-apple-account-iph76e54c61e/26/ios/26) [Reset privacy and security settings in an emergency](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/reset-privacy-security-settings-emergency-iph72e6a13f7/26/ios/26) [Locate a device in Find My on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/locate-a-device-iph09b087eda/26/ios/26)

# Use the Camera Control on iPhone

The Camera Control on ( [supported models](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/aside/iph345af6a5d/26/ios/26)) lets you quickly open your iPhone camera and access common camera settings. The location of the Camera Control is shown below.

![An arrow points to the location of the Camera Control on the side of iPhone.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/fa6ad5fe06b2137eea07499439cb869c.png)

## Open Camera with the Camera Control

Click the Camera Control to open Camera ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/71ce3044f246f5f1f828b819f1c395b6.png).

## Take a photo or video with the Camera Control

1. Click the Camera Control to open Camera ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/71ce3044f246f5f1f828b819f1c395b6.png).

2. Click again to take a photo, or click and hold to record a video.

_Note:_ If your camera is set to Video mode when Camera opens, click to start recording.

## Settings available with the Camera Control

You can select and adjust any of the following settings with the Camera Control:

- _Exposure:_ Brighten or darken the scene by increasing or decreasing the exposure.

- _Depth:_ Increase or decrease the background depth-of-field effect (this setting turns on Portrait mode if it wasn’t already selected).

- _Zoom:_ Zoom in or out.

- _Cameras:_ Switch between cameras, including the front-facing camera.

- _Styles:_ Change the [Photographic Style](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-photographic-styles-iph629d2cd37/26/ios/26).

- _Tone:_ Lighten or deepen the mood by increasing or decreasing the tone.

_Note:_ Not all controls are available in all [camera modes](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/camera-basics-iph263472f78/26/ios/26#iphac6618d52).

metadata.title: Use the Camera Control on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use the Camera Control on iPhone - Apple Support]

the mood by increasing or decreasing the tone.

_Note:_ Not all controls are available in all [camera modes](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/camera-basics-iph263472f78/26/ios/26#iphac6618d52).

## Select and adjust the Camera Control settings

1. Click the Camera Control to open Camera ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/71ce3044f246f5f1f828b819f1c395b6.png).

2. Lightly press the Camera Control twice to open an overlay that shows the available settings for that camera mode.
![The Camera Control menu is open in Camera. The Zoom control is selected.](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/9769500a727a310a1a095c731aa3da12.png)
3. Slide your finger on the Camera Control to scroll through the settings choices, then lightly press to select the setting you want.

4. Slide your finger on the Camera Control to adjust the setting.

The setting is saved and appears the next time you lightly press the Camera Control.

5. Click the Camera Control to take your shot.

**Tip:** You can choose which Camera Control settings are available in the settings overlay, and change the order they appear in. Go to Settings ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) \> Camera > Camera Control > Customize.

## Lock the exposure and focus with the Camera Control

You can use the Camera Control to lock the exposure and focus settings on a subject as you take photos and record video.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Camera, tap Camera Control, then turn on Lock Focus and Exposure.

After you complete steps 1 and 2, you don’t need to repeat them.

3. Click the Camera Control to open Camera ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/71ce3044f246f5f1f828b819f1c395b6.png).

4. Lightly press and hold the Camera Control until you see AE/AF Lock above the frame.

5. Keep your finger touching the Camera Control to maintain the AE/AF Lock.

_Note:_ If you release the Camera Control, the AE/AF Lock turns off.

6. Click the Camera Control to take photos or click and hold to record video.

7. Release the Camera Control to turn off the AE/AF Lock.

metadata.title: Use the Camera Control on iPhone - Apple Support

text:
[Source: apple_support] [Title: Use the Camera Control on iPhone - Apple Support]

If you release the Camera Control, the AE/AF Lock turns off.

6. Click the Camera Control to take photos or click and hold to record video.

7. Release the Camera Control to turn off the AE/AF Lock.

## Customize how you open the Camera Control

You can change the number of clicks it takes to open Camera with the Camera Control from one click to two.

1. Go to the Settings app ![](https://help.apple.com/assets/69F8EBBDF3B89A4F6E0C704C/69F8EBC43862495245036393/en_US/610900798ec772fa266e98f9f6042807.png) on your iPhone.

2. Tap Camera, tap Camera Control, then tap Single Click or Double Click.

You can also adjust accessibility options for the Camera Control, such as the pressure required to lightly press or the speed required to lightly double-press. See [Adjust accessibility settings for the Camera Control](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/adjust-camera-control-settings-iph22c8345f8/26/ios/26).

## Use visual intelligence with Camera Control

If Apple Intelligence\* is turned on, you can use visual intelligence with Camera Control to quickly learn more about the places and objects around you. For example, get details about a restaurant or business, have text translated or read aloud, find out where to buy something you see, and more. See [Use visual intelligence on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-visual-intelligence-iph12eb1545e/26/ios/26).

**See also** [Use the Camera Control to open another app on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/use-the-camera-control-to-open-another-app-iph3940f00d2/26/ios/26) [Adjust accessibility settings for the Camera Control on iPhone](https://support.apple.com/guide/iphone/adjust-camera-control-settings-iph22c8345f8/26/ios/26)
